languages.english: They laid Him in a tomb which had just been hewn out of the rock; it was never used before. They rolled a stone against the door of the tomb.
languages.arabic: ووضعوا الطيب على المبارك. وكان قبر جديد فى البستان طاهراً نقياً لم يوضع أحداً فيه. فوضعوا جسد الوحيد فى ذلك القبر وتركوا حجراً عليه

languages.english: Then they rested on the Sabbath in observance of the commandment. Mary Magdalene and Mary the mother of Jesus saw where He was laid.
languages.arabic: . فإستراحوا كالوصية، من أجل السبت صنعوا هكذا. وكن نسوة واقفات ينظرن ما كان. المجدلية ومريم الآخرى علمن جيداً أين وضع.

title.english: Psalm 3 - O Lord, why have they...
title.arabic: المزمور الثالث - يا رب لماذا كثر...

readings.reference: Psalm 3 - O Lord, why have they...

languages.english: O Lord, why have they who afflict me multiplied? Many have risen up upon me. Many say unto my soul, “There is no salvation for him in his God.” But You, O Lord, are my supporter, my glory, and the elevation of my head.
languages.arabic: يا رب لماذا كثر الذين يحزنونني، كثيرون قاموا عليَّ. كثيرون يقولون لنفسي، ليس له خلاص بإلهه. أنت يا رب أنت هو ناصري، مجدي ورافع رأسي.

languages.english: With my voice I cried unto the Lord, and He heard me out of His holy mountain. I laid down and slept...
languages.arabic: بصوتي إلى الرب صرخت. فاستجاب لي من جبل قدسه. أنا اضطجعت ونمت...

languages.coptic: Ebolqen pideuteronomion n~te Mwu~c/c pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from the Book of Deuteronomy of Moses the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من سفر التثنية لموسى النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Peje Mwu~c/c n~nens/ri m~pIicra/l: je ]ermeyre nwten m~voou n~t~ve nem p~kahi: je qen outako e~retene~tako.
languages.english: [Then Moses said to the children of Israel,] “I testify against you today [before heaven and earth], you shall surely perish.
languages.arabic: قال موسى لبنى إسرائيل: انى أشهد عليكم اليوم السماء والأرض انكم تهلكون لا محالة.

languages.coptic: Mv~r/] m~p~cwjp n~nieynoc: n/e~ta P[oic Vnou] takwou qat~h/ m~petenho: pair/] tetennatako e~v~ma je m~petencwtem n~cat~c~m/ m~P[oic petennou].
languages.english: As the remaining nations the Lord destroys before your face, so you shall perish, because you would not be obedient to the voice of the Lord your God.
languages.arabic: كبقية الأمم التى أبادها الرب من أمام وجهكم. كذلك تهلكون لأجل انكم لم تصغوا لصوت الرب إلهكم.

languages.coptic: Cwtem p~iIcra/l n~yok ,~naerjinior m~Piiordan/c m~voou: e~se e~qoun e~erk~l/ronomin n~hannis] n~eynoc: nem hanbaki eutakt/out n~cobt sa e~h~r/i e~t~ve.
languages.english: "Hear, O Israel, You are to cross over the Jordan today and go in to inherit nations greater and mightier than yourself, cities great and fortified up to heaven,
languages.arabic: إسمع يا إسرائيل. أنت اليوم ستعبر الأردن لكي تدخل وترث أمماً عظيمة ومدناً محصنة ومشيدة إلى السماء،

languages.coptic: Ounis] n~laoc e~naswf: nis/ri n~te nia~vwv: n/e~tekcwoun m~mwou n~yok: e~akcwtem eyb/tou: nim eynas~ohi e~ratf m~pem~yo n~nis/ri n~te Enak:
languages.english: A people, great and large and tall, the sons of Anak, whom you know and of whom you heard it said, 'Who can stand against the face of the sons of Anak?
languages.arabic: قوما عظاماً، بنى الجبابرة، الذين عرفتهم وسمعت عنهم. ومن الذي يقدر أن يقف أمام بنى عناق.

languages.coptic: Eke~mi m~voou je P[oic peknou] etersorp m~mosi qat~h/ m~pekho: ou,~rwm efouwm pe: efe~fotou e~bol efe~takwou n~,wlem: katav~r/] e~tafcaji n~je P[oic.
languages.english: Therefore understand today, the Lord your God is He who goes over before you as a consuming fire. He will destroy them and turn them from your face; and you will destroy them, as the Lord said to you.
languages.arabic: فاعلم اليوم أن الرب إلهك هو الذي يتقدم بالمسير قدام وجهك. بنار آكلة هو يبيدهم ويستأصلهم سريعاً كما قال الرب.

languages.coptic: Ouoh m~pejoc qen pekh/t je eybe tameym/i afent e~bol n~je P[oic e~erk~l/ronomin m~pikahi n~a~gayoc: alla eybe nimeta~ceb/c n~te naieynoc:
languages.english: Do not say in your heart, after the Lord your God consumes these nations before you, saying, 'Because of my righteousness the Lord brought me in to inherit this good land;
languages.arabic: لا تقل فى قلبك إنه من أجل برٌى أخرجنى الرب لأمتلك هذه الأرض الجيدة.

languages.coptic: P[oic nafotou e~bol qat~h/ m~pekho.
languages.english: but because of the ungodliness of these nations, the Lord will destroy them before your face.
languages.arabic: ولأجل اثم هؤلاء الشعوب يبيدهم الرب من أمام وجهك.

languages.coptic: Eybe tekmeym/i an: oude eybe p~toubo an n~te pekh/t n~yok ,~nasenak n~tek erkl/ronomin m~pikahi:
languages.english: It is not because of your righteousness or the uprightness of your heart that you are going in to inherit their land,
languages.arabic: ليس من أجل برٌك وطهارة قلبك تمضى لترث أرضهم.

languages.coptic: Alla hina n~teftahe ]dia~y/k/ e~ratc: y/e~tafwrk m~moc n~nekio] Abraam nem Icaak nem Iakwb.
languages.english: But because of the ungodliness of these nations, the Lord will destroy them before your face, that He may establish His covenant He swore to your fathers, to Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob.
languages.arabic: بل ليفى بالعهد الذي أقسم به لابائك. إبراهيم وإسحق ويعقوب.

languages.coptic: Eke~e~mi m~voou je eybe tekmeym/i an: P[oic na] m~paikahi nak e~erk~l/ronomin m~mof: je n~yok oulaoc n~nast nahbi.
languages.english: Thus you will know today, the Lord your God is not giving You this good land to inherit because of your righteousness, for you are a stiff-necked people.
languages.arabic: وأعلم اليوم إنه ليس من أجل برٌك يعطيك الرب هذه لترثها لأنك شعب صلب الرقبة.

languages.coptic: Ariv~meui~ je ak] ou/r n~jwnt m~P[oic hi p~safe:
languages.english: Remember, do not forget how you provoked the Lord your God to wrath in the desert.
languages.arabic: وأذكر ولا تنس انك كم أغضبت الرب فى البرية.

languages.coptic: Icjen pie~hoou e~tareteni~ e~bolqen p~kahi n~</mi sateteni~ e~paima:
languages.english: From the day you came out of Egypt until you came into this land,
languages.arabic: من اليوم الذي خرجتم فيه من أرض مصر إلى أن جئتم إلى هذا المكان.

languages.coptic: e~retenoi n~atowt n~h/t oube P[oic:
languages.english: you continued being disobedient to the Lord.
languages.arabic: ولا تزالوا تقاومون الرب.

languages.coptic: Ouoh qen <wreb a~reten]jwnt m~P[oic: afm~bon n~je P[oic e~jen y/nou e~fety/nou e~bol.
languages.english: Also in Horeb you provoked the Lord, and the Lord was angry enough with you to destroy you,
languages.arabic: حتى فى حوريب أسخطتم الرب. فغضب الرب عليكم ليبيدكم.

languages.coptic: Ainasen/i a~nok e~h~r/i e~pitwou e~[i n~nip~lax c~nou] n~te ]dia~y/k/: n/e~taf cemn/tou nemwten n~je P[oic:
languages.english: when I went up into the mountain to receive the tablets of stone, the tablets of the covenant the Lord established with you;
languages.arabic: فمضيت أنا وصعدت إلى الجبل لآخذ لوحي العهد الذي عاهدكم الرب.

languages.coptic: Nai,/ e~h~r/i e~pitwou n~h~me n~e~hoou nem h~me n~e~jwrh: wik m~piouwmouoh mwou m~picw.
languages.english: and I stayed on the mountain forty days and forty nights. I neither ate bread nor drank water.
languages.arabic: ومكثت على الجبل أربعين نهاراً وأربعين ليلة لم آكل خبزاً ولم أشرب ماء.

languages.coptic: Ouoh af] n/i n~je P[oic n~]p~lax c~nou] n~w~ni euc~q/out m~pit/b n~te Vnou]: euc~q/out hiw~tou n~je nicaji t/rou n/e~ta Vnou] caji m~mwou nemwten hipitwou m~pie~hoou n~te piywou].
languages.english: Then the Lord delivered two tablets of stone to me, written with the finger of God, and on them were all the words the Lord spoke to you on the mountain, in the day of the assembly.
languages.arabic: وأعطانى الرب لوحى الحجر المكتوبين بإصبع الله. مكتوب عليهما جميع الكلام الذي كلمكم الله به على الجبل فى يوم الإجتماع.

languages.coptic: Ouoh acswpi hiten h~me n~e~hoou nem h~me n~e~jwrh: a~ P[oic ]n/i n~]p~lax c~nou] n~te]dia~y/k/.
languages.english: And it came to pass, at the end of forty days and forty nights, the Lord gave me the two tablets of stone, the tablets of the covenant.
languages.arabic: وفى نهاية الأربعين نهاراً والأربعين ليلة. أعطانى الرب لوحى العهد

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje P[oic n/i: je twnk masenak e~pec/t e~bol tai n~,wlem: je afera~nomin n~je peklaoc n/e~take~nou e~bolqen p~kahi n~</mi: aferparabenin n~,wlem hipimwit e~takhenhwnou e~rof: auyamio nwou n~ouwth.
languages.english: "Then the Lord said to me, 'Arise, go down quickly from here, for your people you brought out of Egypt have acted lawlessly; they turned aside quickly from the way I commanded them; they made themselves a molten image.'
languages.arabic: وقال لى الرب: قم إنزل عاجلاً من هنا لانه قد أثم شعبك الذي أخرجته من أرض مصر وزاغوا سريعاً عن الطريق التى سننتها لهم. وصنعوا لهم تمثالاً مسبوكاً.

languages.coptic: Peje P[oic n/i je aicaji n~oucop nem c~nau eijwm~moc: je pailaoc hannast nahbi ne: <at n~tafotou e~bol capec/t n~t~ve:
languages.english: Furthermore, the Lord spoke to me, saying, 'I told you once and twice, I see this people, and indeed they are a stiff-necked people. Let Me destroy them and blot out their name from under heaven;
languages.arabic: وقال لي الرب: قد قلت لك مرة وأثنتين أن هذا الشعب صلب الرقبة، دعنى فابيدهم من تحت السماء.

languages.coptic: Ntaaik n~ounis] n~eynoc efos e~naswf e~hote vai: tai kott aii~ e~pec/t e~bol hipitwou:ouoh pitwou nafmoh n~,rwm: ]p~lax c~nou] n~wni nau,/ qen tajij c~nou].
languages.english: And I will make of you a nation, greater and stronger and larger than they.' "I turned and came down from the mountain, and the mountain burned with fire; and the two tablets were in my two hands.
languages.arabic: وأجعلك لشعب أعظم وأكثر كثيراً من هذا. فرجعت ونزلت من الجبل والجبل يشتعل بالنار ولوحى الحجارة فى يدي الإثنتين.

languages.coptic: Etainau je a~retenernobi m~pem~yo m~P[oic: a~retenyamio nwten n~oumaci n~ouwth: a~retenerparabenin e~bol hipimwit e~ta P[oic henhen y/nou e~rof.
languages.english: Then I looked, and you had sinned against the Lord your God and made for yourselves a molten image. You turned aside quickly from the way the Lord commanded you.
languages.arabic: فلما رأيت انكم قد أخطأتم إلى الرب وصنعتم لكم عجلاً مسبوكاً وزغتم عن الطريق التى أوصاكم بها الرب،

languages.coptic: Aiborber n~]p~lax c~nou] e~bolqen najij: aiqemqwmou m~petenm~yo e~bol.
languages.english: Then I took the two tablets and threw them down with my two hands, and broke them before you.
languages.arabic: طرحت اللوحين من يدي وكسرتهما

languages.coptic: Ai]ho m~P[oic m~v~mah cop c~nau: kata v~r/] n~sorp: n~h~me n~e~hoou nemh~me n~e~jwrh: wik m~piouwm: ouoh mwou m~picw: eybe netennobi t/rou n/eta~retenswft m~mwou:
languages.english: And I prayed before the Lord a second time, as at the first, forty days and forty nights; I neither ate bread nor drank water, because of all your sins you committed in doing wickedly in the sight of the Lord your God to provoke Him.
languages.arabic: قدامكم ثم طلبت من الرب مرة ثانية كالأولى أربعين نهاراً وأربعين ليلة. لم آكل خبزاً ولم أشرب ماء. من اجل كل خطاياكم التى أخطأتم بها.

languages.coptic: Ei~ri m~pipethwou m~pem~yo m~P[oic e~]jwnt naf. Ouoh ]erho] je afjwnt n~je P[oic e~jen y/nou: e~fety/nou e~bol: Afcwtem e~roi n~je P[oic qen pic/ou e~tem~mau:
languages.english: For I was afraid because of the anger and the wrath. For the Lord was provoked by you to destroy you. But the Lord listened to me at this time also.
languages.arabic: بعملكم الشر أمام الرب لاغاظته. ففزعت إذ غضب الله عليكم ليبيدكم فاستجاب لى الرب فى ذلك الزمان.

languages.coptic: AP[oic jwnt e~Aa~rwn e~masw e~fotf e~bol: aitwbh e~jen Aa~rwn qen pic/ou e~tem~mau.
languages.english: The Lord was also very angry with Aaron and would have destroyed him; so I also prayed for Aaron at that time.
languages.arabic: وغضب الرب على هرون جداً ليبيده. فصليت من أجل هرون أيضاً فى ذلك الزمان.

languages.coptic: Ouoh pinobi eta~retenaif: pimaci ai[itf airokhf qen ou,~rwm ai[et[wtf: aimonkf satefswpi efsom: aferm~v~r/] n~ouswis: aihioui~ m~piswis e~pec/t e~pimouncwrem eyn/ou e~pec/t e~bol qen pitwou.
languages.english: Then I took the sin you committed, the calf you made, and burned it with fire, and crushed it, and ground it very small until it was as fine as dust; and I threw its dust into the brook that descended from the mountain.
languages.arabic: أما الخطية التى عملتموها والعجل الذي صنعتموه. فانى أخذته وأحرقته بالنار. وحطمته وسحقته حتى صار ناعماً كالغبار. ثم طرحت غباره فى أسفل وادى السيل المنحدر من الجبل

languages.coptic: Qen pirwkh qen pipiracmoc qen pim~hau n~te]e~piyumia~: nareten ]jwnt m~P[oic peten nou].
languages.english: "Also at the Burning and the Temptation and the Graves of Lust, you provoked the Lord your God to wrath.
languages.arabic: وبالحريق والتجربة فى قبر الشهوات كنتم تغضبون الرب إلهكم.

languages.coptic: Hote eta~ P[oic ouerpy/nou e~bol qen Kad/c barn/ efjwm~moc: je masenwten e~h~r/i a~rik~l/ronomin m~pikahi v/a~nok e~]nat/if nwten:
languages.english: Likewise, when the Lord sent you from Kadesh Barnea, saying, 'Go up and inherit the land I give you,'
languages.arabic: وعندما أرسلكم الرب من قادش برنيع قائلاً: أصعدوا لترثوا الأرض التى أنا أعطيها لكم.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~reten eratcwtem n~ca p~caji nem P[oic: m~petennah] e~rof oude m~peten cwtem n~catefc~m/.
languages.english: then you disobeyed the word of the Lord your God, and you did not believe Him nor listen to His voice.
languages.arabic: عصيتم قول الرب ولم تؤمنوا به ولم تسمعوا لصوته.

languages.coptic: Naretenoi gar n~atcwtem n~ca P[oic icjen pie~hoou e~tafouonhf e~rwten.
languages.english: You were disobedient to the Lord from the day He became known to you.
languages.arabic: ولم تزالوا عاصين الرب من يوم أن ظهر لكم.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen ?ca/~ac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Isaiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من إشعياء النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Cwtem t~ve ouoh [ic~m/ p~kahi: je P[oic petafcaji: hans/ri aij~vwou ouoh ai[a~cou: n~ywou de auera~yetin m~moi.
languages.english: Hear, O heavens, and listen, O earth. For the Lord has spoken: "I have begotten and brought up sons, but they rejected Me.
languages.arabic: إسمعى أيتها السموات وأصغى أيتها الأرض فان الرب تكلم: ربيت بنين ونشأتهم أما هم فعصونى.

languages.coptic: A oue~he couen v/e~tafsopc: ouoh oue~w~ afcouen v~ouohnf n~te pef[oic: p~iIcra/l de m~pefcouwnt: ouoh palaoc m~pefka] e~roi:
languages.english: The ox knows its owner and the donkey its master's crib; but Israel does not know Me, and the people do not understand Me."
languages.arabic: الثور يعرف قانيه، والحمار معلف صاحبه. أما إسرائيل فلم يعرفنى، وشعبى لم يفهمنى.

languages.coptic: Ouoi m~pis~lol n~refernobi: pilaoc eymeh n~a~martia~: p~j~roj m~pipon/ron nis/ri n~a~nomoc: a~reten,a P[oic n~cay/nou: peyouab m~pIicra/l a~reten ]jwnt naf.
languages.english: Alas, sinful nation, a people full of sins, an evil seed, lawless children. They forsook the Lord; they provoked to anger the Holy One of Israel.
languages.arabic: ويل للأمة الخاطئة. الشعب الممتلئ إثماً. النسل المخالف. تركتم الرب وأغضبتم قدوس إسرائيل.

languages.coptic: Ouoh hanerqot e~reten na[itou: arten touho a~nouia~: a~ve niben e~p~m~kahi: ouoh h/t niben e~p~m~kah n~h/t.
languages.english: Why should you still be struck as you continue in lawlessness? The whole head is in pain, and the whole heart in sadness.
languages.arabic: ستحل بكم ضربات لأنكم إزددتم إثماً. كل رأس للوجع. وكل قلب للحزن.

languages.coptic: Icjen ouvat sa oua~ve: n~ouvwlh an pe: oude n~oulele,emi an pe: oude n~ouerqot an pe eft~q~mom m~mon n~oumalagma e~t/if e~rof: oude ouneh oude hanmour.
languages.english: From the feet all the way to the head, there is no soundness in them, only wounds and bruises and festering sores. They have not been closed or bandaged, or soothed with ointment.
languages.arabic: من القدم إلى الرأس جراح وكلوم وقرحة ملتهبة وما من مرهم يوضع عليها ولا دهن ولا عصائب.

languages.coptic: Peten kahi efe~swf: netenbaki eue~rokhou qen pi,~rwm: neten,wra e~ouo~mou m~petenm~yo e~bol: acswf ouoh acowjp n~ten hanlaoc n~semmo.
languages.english: Your land is desolate; your cities are burned with fire. Strangers take over your land in your presence and it is made desolate, overthrown by foreign people.
languages.arabic: أرضكم تخرب. ومدنكم تحرق بالنار وكوركم تؤكل قدامكم. وقد خربت وإنهدمت من الشعوب الغرباء.

languages.coptic: Eue~cwjp n~t~seri n~Ciwn m~v~r/] n~ouc~kun/ qen ouiah a~loli: nem m~v~r/] n~oumana~reh n~jwji qen oubon]: nem m~v~r/] n~oubaki euswl m~moc:
languages.english: So the daughter of Zion is forsaken, like a tent in a vineyard, like a hut in a garden of cucumbers, like a besieged city.
languages.arabic: وتبقى إبنة صهيون كمظلة فى كرم. وكمثل المحرس المنقطع فى المقثأة. وكالمدينة المنهوبة.

languages.coptic: ouoh e~b/l je a~ P[oic cabawy cojp n~ouj~roj nan: neicy~nei aner m~v~fr/] n~Codoma ouoh ani~ni n~Gomora.
languages.english: Unless the Lord of hosts had left us a seed, we would have become like Sodom and been made like Gomorrah.
languages.arabic: ولولا أن رب الجنود أبقى لنا بقية. لصرنا مثل سدوم وشابهنا عمورة.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Iermiac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Jeremiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من أرميا النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Pkahi p~kahi cwtem e~p~caji m~P[oic: Cqai m~pirwmi je ourwmi pe auhitf: je f~naernis] an qen nefe~hoou: oude n~nerwmi aiai qen pefc~perma: eyrefhemci hijen piy~ronoc n~Lauid efoi n~ar,wn icjen ]nou qen p/i n~Iouda.
languages.english: "O land, land, hear the word of the Lord: 'Write this man as a banished man, for none at all from his seed shall be raised to sit on the throne of David, or still as a ruler in Judah."
languages.arabic: يا أرض يا أرض إسمعى كلمة الرب. إكتبوا هذا الرجل رجلاً مرذولاً. لا يصير عظيماً فى أيامه ولايكبر من ذريته إنسان يجلس على كرسى داود رئيساً من الآن على بيت يهوذا.

languages.coptic: W nimane~cwou ettako ouoh etjwr e~bol qen poumanmoni: Eybe vai nai ne n/e~tefjw m~mwou n~je P[oic e~jen nimane~cwou eymoni m~palaoc: je n~ywten a~tetenjwr e~bol n~nae~cwou: ouoh a~tetennospou ouoh m~petenjem pousini:
languages.english: "Woe to the shepherds who scatter and destroy the sheep of My pasture!" Therefore thus says the Lord against those who tend My people: "You have scattered My sheep and driven them out. You did not care for them.
languages.arabic: أيها الرعاة المفسدون والمبددون فى مكان رعيتكم. لذلك هذا ما يقوله الرب عن الرعاة الذين يرعون شعبى: أنتم قد شتتم غنمى وبددتموها ولم تتعهدوها.

languages.coptic: H/ppe a~nok eie~[im~p~sis qen y/nou kata netenh~b/oui~ ethwou peje P[oic. Anok hw ]naswp e~roi m~p~cepi m~palaoc qen p~kahi t/rf e~tai jorou e~bol n~q/tf: ouoh nta e~nou e~qoun e~pouman~moni n~touaiai ouoh n~toua~sai.
languages.english: Behold, I shall punish you according to your evil practices. I will receive the remnant of My people from every land where I have driven them. I will establish them in their pasture, and they shall increase and be multiplied.
languages.arabic: فهأنذا أنتقم منكم حسب أعمالكم الشريرة يقول الرب. أجمع بقية شعبى من جميع الأراضى التى فرقتها فيها وأتى بها إلى مراعيهم فتنمو وتكثر. وأقيم عليها رعاة يرعونها.

languages.coptic: Ouoh eie~tounoc hanman~e~cwou nai eynaa~moni m~mwou: ouoh cenaerho] an icjen tnou: oude cena s~yorter an peje P[oic. Ic hane~hoou cen/ou peje P[oic: n~tataho e~ratf m~picaji e~tai cemn/tf e~jen p~iIcra/l nem p~/i n~Iouda:
languages.english: I will set up shepherds over them who will feed them. They shall fear no more, nor be terrified," says the Lord.
languages.arabic: فلا تخاف منذ الآن ولا ترتعد يقول الرب. وهوذا ستأتى أيام يقول الرب وأقيم الكلمة الصالحة التى قررتها على إسرائيل وبيت يهوذا.

languages.coptic: n~h~r/i qen nie~hoou e~tem~mau ]na]ouw~ n~oua~natol/ m~meym/i n~Dauid: ouoh n~teferouro n~je ououro n~dikeoc efka]:
languages.english: "Behold, days are coming," says the Lord, "when I will raise up for David the Righteous Orient, and a King shall reign.
languages.arabic: فى تلك الأيام أشرق نور البر لداود فيملك ملك بار فيهم

languages.coptic: Ouoh efi~ri n~ouhap nem oudikeocun/ hijen p~kahi. Ouoh n~h~r/i qen nefe~hoou f~naoujai n~je Iouda ouoh p~iIcra/l naswpi qen outajro.
languages.english: He will understand and bring about judgment and righteousness on the earth. In His days Judah will be saved, and Israel shall dwell in confidence.
languages.arabic: ويجرى الحكم والعدل على الأرض وفى أيامه يخلص يهوذا ويسكن إسرائيل آمنا مطمئناً.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.english: The following reading has no reference in the modern biblical text, whether Septuagint or Masoretic. It is found only in Coptic manuscripts, one of which is in the British museum. However, this reading is the only surviving primary source that corroborates Matthew 27:9-10, in which St. Matthew writes that the following text is from Jeremiah the prophet, while modern Old Testament text places it in Zechariah 11 (also read below). The likely location of this prophecy is Jeremiah 39 in the Septuagint numbering, Jeremiah 32 in the Masoretic numbering. Source: Clarke, Adam. "Commentary on Matthew 27:9". "The Adam Clarke Commentary". 1832.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Iermiac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Jeremiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من أرميا النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Palin afjoc n~je ieremiac n~Lafaqsour je e~reten e~swpi qen ouc/ou nem netenio] teten] e~h~ren ]meym/i: nem netens/ri eynai~ menenca y/nou: nai eynai~ri n~oua~nomia~ ecw~reb e~hote y/nou:
languages.english: And Jeremiah said to Pashhur, “You were resisting the truth for sometime with your father and your children who did sin worse than you.
languages.arabic: ثم قال أرميا لفشحور: إنكم كنتم زماناً مع أبائكم مقاومين للحق وأولادكم الذين يآتٍون بعدكم هؤلاء الذين يصنعون خطية أشر منكم.

languages.coptic: Je n~ywou eyna ]tim/ qav/ e~tem~mon tim/ n~taf: ouoh eue~er b~laptin m~v/ettal[o n~niswni: ouoh e~,anobi e~bol:
languages.english: For they set a price to him who has no price, and grieved him who heals sickness and forgives sins.
languages.arabic: لأنهم يثمنون الذي ليس له ثمن. ويؤلمون الذي يشفى الأمراض ويغفر الذنوب

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~[in~]map n~hat t~tim/ m~v/e~touna t/if n~je nens/ri m~p~iIcra/l: ouoh e~t/ic qapiiohi n~te pikerameuc: Mv~r/] e~taf ouahcahni n~je P[oic: ouoh pair/] ]nacaji.
languages.english: They took the price upon which the sons of Israel agreed, thirty pieces of silver. And gave it to the potter’s house. As the Lord commanded me, so I say.
languages.arabic: ويأخذون الثلاثين من الفضة الثمن الذي شارط عليه بنى إسرائيل. ويدفعونها فى حقل الفاخورى. كما أمرنى الرب وهكذا أقول.

languages.coptic: Cenai~ e~h~r/i e~jwou n~je ouhap n~te p~tako sae~neh: nem e~jen nous/ri: eybe je auhioui~ n~ouc~nof n~aynobi e~p~hap.
languages.english: The judgment of perdition will be upon them and their children forever, for they judged innocent blood.
languages.arabic: ستآتٍى عليهم دينونة الهلاك إلى الأبد وعلى أولادهم. لأنهم ألقوا دماً ذكياً فى الحكم.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen }covia~ n~te Colomon pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from the Wisdom of Solomon the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من حكمة سليمان النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Efe~jorjf n~ou,~rwm eybe je m~pef] h~loj e~penh/t: ouoh ef] e~h~ren nenh~b/oui~ ouoh e]ersws nan e~jen nen swft e~jen pinomoc. Efouwnh e~h~r/i e~jwn e~jen ninobi n~te ]metatywt n~h/t:
languages.english: "Let us lie in ambush for the righteous man, because he is useless to us and opposes our deeds; He denounces us for our sins against the law and accuses us of sins against our upbringing.
languages.arabic: و لنكمن للصديق لأنه ثقيل علينا. لأنه يقاوم أعمالنا ويقرعنا على مخالفتنا للناموس. ويظهر علينا خطايا العصيان

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~ouon e~mi nemaf e~bolhiten Vnou]: eue~mou] e~rof je p~s/ri m~Vnou].
languages.english: He claims to have knowledge of God, and he calls himself a child of the Lord.
languages.arabic: وأن عنده علم من عند الله. ويسمى إبن الله.

languages.coptic: Efe~swpi nan n~ourefcohi e~jen nenouws: efouwh e~h~r/i e~jwn e~p~jinnau e~rof:
languages.english: He has become for us as a refutation of our purposes; even seeing him is a burden to us,
languages.arabic: يصير مبكتاً لنا على أفكارنا. ومنظره ثقيل علينا أن نراه.

languages.coptic: eybe je peflaoc o~ni n~keouon an: ouoh nefmwit cevwnh n~toten: ouoh en/p n~totf je qae~.
languages.english: Because his life is unlike that of others; for his paths go in a different direction.
languages.arabic: لأجل أن شعبه لا يشبه غيره. وطرقه مخالفة لنا. وحسبنا عنده متأخرين.

languages.coptic: Efouei n~nenmwit m~v~r/] e~tefouei m~mof n~niy/rion: efe~m~bon e~jenhan ouon n~te nie~leuyeroc: ouoh efsousou m~mof je Vnou] pefiwt.
languages.english: We are considered by him as a hybrid, and he avoids our ways as something immoral. He considers the last things of the righteous as blessed and pretends that God is his Father.
languages.arabic: وهو متباعد عن طرقنا كتباعده عن الوحوش. ويغضب على القوم الأحرار. ويفتخر بأن الله أبوه.

languages.coptic: Tenj je a~r/ou nefcaji hanmeym/i ne: euerdokimazin m~v/eynaswpi menenca pefjwk e~bol:
languages.english: Let us see if his words are true, and let us put these last things to the test at the end of his life.
languages.arabic: فلننظر لعل كلامه حق. ولنختبر ما سيكون عند نهايته.

languages.coptic: Je n~ne ous/ri m~Vnou] qen oumeym/i: f~nanohem n~tef'u,/ efe~cotc qen nenjij n~nian tikimenoc.
languages.english: For if the righteous man is a son of God, He will help him, and deliver him from the hand of those who oppose him.
languages.arabic: فان كان هو حقاً إبن الله فهو يخلص نفسه وينقذها من أيدى المضادين.

languages.coptic: Anerpirazin m~mof e~bolhiten ousws: nem oubacanoc: eue~mi qen vai m~pefyebio: eue~mi e~tefmetremraus nem tefhupomon/:
languages.english: Let us test him with insult and torture that we may know his gentleness and test his patient endurance.
languages.arabic: فلنمتحنه بالشتم والعذاب حتى نعلم بهذا تواضعه. ونختبر دعته وصبره.

languages.coptic: Eue~]hap e~rof qen oumou efs/s: hina n~te ]lwiji swpi e~jwf e~bolqen pefcaji.
languages.english: Let us condemn him to a shameful death, for there shall be a visitation because of his words."
languages.arabic: ولنحكم عليه بأشنع ميته. لكى تكون الحجة عليه من كلامه.

languages.coptic: Eue~firwous qen vai ouoh eue~cwrem: afywm m~pousousou
languages.english: So they reasoned these things and were led astray, for their malice blinded them.
languages.arabic: هذا ما ارتأوه فضلوا، لأن فخرهم أعماهم,

languages.coptic: Ouoh mpoue~mi e~nimuct/rion n~te Vnou]: ouoh m~pouernoinm~v~be,e n~te niym/i: ouoh m~pouerv~meui~ n~nienkot n~ni.u,/ e~te m~mon a[ni n~q/tou.
languages.english: But they did not know the mysteries of God, nor hope for the wages of holiness, nor judge the reward of blameless souls.
languages.arabic: ولم يدركوا أسرار الله ولم يرجوا أجرة الأبرار. ولم يفكروا فى راحة النفوس التى لا عيب فيها.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Za,ariac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Zechariah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من زكريا النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aue~mi n~je ni,ananeoc niecwou e~toua~reh e~rwou je oucaji n~te P[oic pe.
languages.english: And in that day they will be scattered about. And the Canaanites shall know the flock is kept safe, for this is the word of the Lord.
languages.arabic: وعلم الكنعانيون، الأغنام المحروسة، أنها كلمة الرب.

languages.coptic: Eie~joc nwou je icje na nee m~peten m~yo: moi n/i e~retencemne pabe,e: ie m~mon a~ri potazecye: ouoh aucemni mpabe,e mmap n~hat.
languages.english: And I shall say to them, "If it is fitting in your judgment, give me my payment, or refuse it." And they established my wage at thirty pieces of silver.
languages.arabic: أقولها لهم: أن حسن لديكم فأعطونى أجرتى التى قررتموها والا فامتنعوا. فقرروا أجرتى ثلاثين من الفضة.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje P[oic n/i hitou e~pimanouwth: ouoh jemp~sini je an f~cwtp m~v~r/] e~tauerdokimazin m~moi e~h~r/i e~jwou:
languages.english: And the Lord said to me, "Drop them into the smelting furnace and see if it is proven, as in the same manner I was proven for their sake."
languages.arabic: وقال لى الرب إلقها فى الخزأنه، وإفحص هل هى مختارة مثل ما جربونى بها.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ai[i n~]map n~hat aihitou e~qoun ep~/i m~P[oic e~pimanouwth.
languages.english: And I took the thirty pieces of silver and threw them into the smelting furnace in the house of the Lord.
languages.arabic: فأخذت الثلاثين من الفضة وطرحتها داخل بيت الرب فى الخزأنه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aiborber m~pis~bwt m~mah c~nau e~te pinoh n~rws pe: e~p~jinjwr e~bol m~pia~mahi qen y~m/] nIouda nem qen y~m/] mp~iIcra/l.
languages.english: And I threw aside the second staff, the Line, to disband the possessions between Judah and Israel.
languages.arabic: وطرحت العصاة الثانية التى هى حبل القياس. لابعد العز من بين يهوذا وإسرائيل.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Mi,eoc pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Micah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من ميخا النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoi n~ta'u,/ je afmounk n~je pireferho] e~bol hijen p~kahi: ouoh v/etcoutwn qen nirwmi f~sop an: ce[i hapt/rou e~han c~nof: v~ouai v~ouai f~hojhej m~pefsvr qen ouhojhej.
languages.english: Woe is me, O my soul! For the God-fearing man has perished from the earth, and there is no one upright among the men. They all lie in wait, even unto blood, and each one grievously oppresses his neighbor.
languages.arabic: الويل لنفسى لأنه قد فنى التقى من الأرض. ولا يوجد مستقيم بين الناس. جميعهم يكمنون للدماء. كل واحد يضايق صاحبه ضيقا.

languages.coptic: Eucob] n~noujij e~pipethwou: f~e~retin n~je piar,wn ouoh piref]hap afcaji n~hancaji n~hir/n/: afje peyranaf n~tef'u,/ n~je pijwr:
languages.english: They prepare their hands for evil; the prince demands a gift, and the judge speaks flattering words; it is the desire of their soul.
languages.arabic: ويعدون أيديهم للشر. الرئيس يسأل. والقاضى يتكلم بكلام السلامة والقوى ينطق بهوى نفسه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ]nawli nnoua~gayon m~v~r/] n~ouholi ecouwm qen oue~hoou n~,aki: ouoi ouoi aui n~je ni[i m~p~sis: ]nou efe~swpi n~je ourimi:
languages.english: "Thus I shall take away their good things as the moth who eats away, and as one proceeding by the rule in the day of the watch. Alas! alas! The time of your vengeance is come; now shall be their lamentation.
languages.arabic: فسأنزع خيراتهم مثل السوس الذي يأكل فى يوم مظلم. الويل الويل فقد جاء الأنتقام. فالآن يكون البكاء.

languages.coptic: Mpertenhet y/nou qen netens~v/r: oude m~pererhelpic n~netenhugoumenoc: a~reh e~rok e~y/e~tekenkot nemac: e~s~tem ,ah~li qatotc.
languages.english: Do not trust in friends, nor put your hope in those who govern. Beware of your wife and do not tell her anything.
languages.arabic: لا تأمنوا لاصدقائكم ولا تتكلوا على مدبريكم. إحفظ نفسك من النائم معها ولا تضع عندها شيئاً. لأن الإبن يهين أبيه.

languages.coptic: Je ou/i ous/ri f~nasws m~pefiwt ouseri c~natwnc e~jen tecmau: ouselet e~tecswmi: hanjaji m~pirwmi ne n/et qen pef/i.
languages.english: For a son dishonors his father, a daughter will rise up against her mother, a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law; a man's enemies are all the people of his own house."
languages.arabic: والإبنة تقوم على أمها والعروس على حمآتٍها وأعداء الآنسان أهل بيته.

languages.coptic: Anok de ]najoust e~jen P[oic: ]namoun e~bol e~jen Vnou] pacwt/r. panou] nacwtem e~roi.
languages.english: But I will look to the Lord. I will wait for God my Savior, for my God will hear me.
languages.arabic: وأما أنا فاترقب الرب. وأتمسك بالله مخلصى فيستجيب لى الهي.

languages.coptic: Mperrasi m~moi tajaji: je aisanhei m~natwnt on: je ou/i aisanhemci qen p~,aki: fnaerouwini e~roi n~je P[oic.
languages.english: Do not rejoice over me, O my enemy, for though I have fallen, yet will I arise, because even if I should sit in darkness, the Lord will be a light to me.
languages.arabic: ولا يفرح بى أعدائى. فانى إذا سقطت سأقوم أيضاً. وأن جلست فى الظلمة فالرب سينير لي.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

title.arabic: عظة لابينا القديس أنبا يوحنا ذهبى الفم

languages.coptic: Oukat/,/cic n~te peniwt eyouab abba Iwann/c pi,~rucoctomoc: e~re pefc~mou eyouab swpi neman: a~m/n.
languages.english: A homily of our holy father Abba John Chrysostom. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: عظة لابينا القديس يوحنا ذهبى الفم: بركته المقدسة تكون معنا آمين.

languages.coptic: Ou pe ten nas~jof w~nenc~n/ou m~menrit: eybe ]parabacic n~te Iouda. Ve~taf] m~P[oic. Pejaf gar je ouai e~bolqen pim/t cnau:
languages.english: Beloved brethren, what can we say about the transgression of Judas who has delivered his Master. It was said that one of the twelve,
languages.arabic: ماذا نقول أيها الإخوة الأحباء عن معصية يهوذا الذي أسلم سيده. قيل أن واحداً من الأثنى عشر.

languages.coptic: Ete Ioudac pe piIckariwt/c: afsenaf ha niar,/e~reuc ouoh pejaf nwou. Je ou pe e~tetenouws e~t/ifn/i ouoh a~nok ]nat/if nwten: ouoh aucemn/tc nemaf m~map n~hat.
languages.english: Who is Judas Iscariot went to the chief priests and said to them; “How much do you want to pay and I will deliver Him to you? They bargained to pay him thirty pieces of silver.”
languages.arabic: الذي هو يهوذا الإسخريوطى. مضى إلى رؤساء الكهنة وقال لهم. ماذا تريدون أن تعطونى وأنا أسلمه إليكم؟ فساوموه على ثلاثين من الفضة.

languages.coptic: Wtai nis] m~metath/t n~tai mai~/ mallon de tainis] m~metmaihat: n~yoc gar eymici m~pethwou niben:
languages.english: What great foolishness; rather, what a great infatuation with silver, which is the source of all evil.
languages.arabic: يا لهذا الجهل العظيم. وبالأحرى يا لهذه المحبة العظيمة للفضة التى هى أصل لكل الشرور.

languages.coptic: Eta~vai gar ere~piyumin e~roc af] m~pefcaq: ounouni gar echwou te ]met maihat:
languages.english: For when he coveted it, he sold his rightous Teacher and Lord, and landed himself in the depths of death.
languages.arabic: لأن هذا لما إشتهاه باع معلمه الصالح وسيده البار. فطوح بنفسه فى هوة الهلاك.

languages.coptic: Echwou e~demwn niben: Ouoh ni'u,/ e~tecna tahwou sac y~roulibi n~ceyaq:
languages.english: How awful is the love of money. It invited every evil and is worse than Satan’s manipulation.
languages.arabic: لأنه كم هو ردئ حب المال. فهو مجلبة لكل شر وأردأ من حيل الشياطين.

languages.coptic: Nceerate~mi e~rwou m~min m~mwou: ouoh on sauswpi euoi n~atcwoun n~ouon niben: Sau ,w n~cwou m~v~nomoc n~]vucic:
languages.english: If the love of money possesses someone, it renders him captivated by it. They can no longer fathom themselves and ignore others.
languages.arabic: فالنفوس التى يتسلط عليها يجعل أصحابها يجنون ولعاً بها. فلا يعرفون ذواتهم بل ويتعامون عن معرفة الأخرين.

languages.coptic: Safk/n n~je pouh/t m~mau ouoh sautwmt.
languages.english: They will reject nature’s laws and become anxious at heart.
languages.arabic: ويرفضون ناموس الطبيعة. ويكون قلبهم فزعاً حائراً.

languages.coptic: Anau je: je a~]metmaihat hioui~ e~bol n~ou/r m~peynanef e~bolqen t'u,/ n~Ioudac.
languages.english: See how many blessings the love of silver took away from Judas.
languages.arabic: أنظروا كم من النعم نزعتها محبة الفضة من نفس يهوذا.

languages.coptic: Loipon nafjw erwou n~oum/s n~caji qen ouwnh e~bol n~je P[oic I/couc Pi,~rictoc: eybe ]geenna nem eybe ]metouro n~te niv/oui:
languages.english: Because Christ was talking to them openly about the comforts of the kingdom of heaven and the horrors of hell.
languages.arabic: لأن سيدنا يسوع المسيح كان يخاطبهم علانية عن هول الجحيم ونعيم ملكوت السموات.

languages.coptic: Eftame ouon niben e~y~mai/ n~]kolacic n~te nirefernobi: ouoh ef] taio n~ouon niben eynakayartou m~p~taho e~ratf n~te nou'u,/.
languages.english: Every one of them knew how terrible it would be, the sufferings of sinners. Christ also honored all those who strived for their soul’s salvation.
languages.arabic: ويعرف كل واحد مقدار عذاب الخطاة. ويكرم كل المجاهدين لخلاص نفوسهم.

languages.coptic: Marener c~v~rajizin n~]kat/,/cic n~te peniwt eyouab abba Iwann/c pi,~rucoctomoc v/e~taferouwini m~pennouc: nem nibal n~te nenh/t: qen v~ran m~Viwt nem Ps/ri nem Pip~neuma eyouab ounou] n~ouwt: a~m/n.
languages.english: We conclude the homily of our holy father Abba John Chrysostom who enlightened our minds and our hearts. In the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit, one God. Amen.
languages.arabic: فلنختم موعظة أبينا القديس يوحنا ذهبى الفم: الذي أنار عقولنا وعيون قلوبنا بإسم الآب والإبن والروح القدس الإله الواحد آمين.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: Je autwounou e~h~r/i ejwi n~je hanmetmeyreu n~ref[i n~jonc: ouoh a~]met[i n~jonc je meynouj e~roc m~min m~moc:
languages.english: For unjust witnesses rise up against me and injustice lies to itself.
languages.arabic: لأنه قام علىّ شهود زور: وكذب الظلم لذاته.

languages.coptic: Autwounou e~h~r/i e~jwi n~je hanmeyreu n~oji: nausini m~moi e~n/e~te n~]cwoun m~mwou an: Au]n/i n~han pethwou n~t~sebiw~ n~han peynaneu: e~auq~rajrej n~nounajhi e~h~r/i e~jwi: all/louia.
languages.english: Unjust witnesses rose up against me; asking me things I knew nothing about. They repaid me evil for good and bereavement for my soul. Alleluia.
languages.arabic: قام علىّ شهود جور وعما لا أعلم سألونى. جازونى بدل الخير شراً صارين علىَّ بأسنانهم. الليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Matyeon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Matthew. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار متى البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Eta tooui~ de swpi auerouco[ni t/rou n~je niar,/e~reuc nem nip~recbuteroc n~te pilaoc qa I/couc: hwcte n~ce qoybef:
languages.english: When morning came, all the chief priests and elders of the people plotted against Jesus to put Him to death.
languages.arabic: ولما كان الغد تشاور جميع رؤساء الكهنة وشيوخ الشعب على يسوع لكى يقتلوه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tauconhf auolf ouoh aut/if m~Pilatoc pih/gemwn.
languages.english: And when they had bound Him, they led Him away and delivered Him to Pontius Pilate the governor.
languages.arabic: فأوثقوه وأخذوه وأسلموه إلى بيلاطس الوالى.

languages.coptic: Tote e~tafnau n~je Ioudac v/e~taf t/if je auer katak~rinin m~mof e~tafouem h~y/f: aftacyo n~]map n~hat ha niar,/e~reuc nem nip~recbuteroc.
languages.english: Then Judas, His betrayer, seeing that He had been condemned, was remorseful and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders.
languages.arabic: حينئذ لما رأى يهوذا الذي أسلمه انه قد دين ندم ورد الثلاثين من الفضة إلى رؤساء الكهنة والشيوخ.

languages.coptic: Efjw m~moc jw aiernobi je ai] n~ouc~nof n~y~m/i: n~ywou de pejwou naf: je a~qon a~non n~yok eke~rwsi:
languages.english: Saying, “I have sinned by betraying innocent blood.” And they said, “What is that to us? You see to it!”
languages.arabic: قائلاً إنى قد أخطأت إذ سلمت دماً زكياً. فقالوا له: ماذا علينا أبصر أنت.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afborber n~nihat e~qoun e~piervei: Ouoh afsenaf afojhf:
languages.english: Then he threw down the pieces of silver in the temple and departed, and went and hanged himself.
languages.arabic: فطرح الفضة فى الهيكل. ومضى فخنق نفسه.

languages.coptic: Niar,/e~reuc de etau[i n~nihat: pejwou je c~se nan an e~hitou e~pikorbanon: je t~tim/ n~ouc~nof pe.
languages.english: But the chief priests took the silver pieces and said, “It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, because they are the price of blood.”
languages.arabic: فأخذ رؤساء الكهنة الفضة وقالوا: لا يحل أن نجعلها فى بيت القربان. لأنها ثمن دم.

languages.coptic: Etauer ouco[ni de auswp e~bol n~q/tou m~piiohi n~te pikerameuc euma n~ywmc n~nisemmwou: Eybe vai aumou] e~v~ran m~piiohi e~tem~mau: je piiohi m~pic~nof sa e~qoun evoou:
languages.english: And they consulted together and bought with them the potter’s field, to bury strangers in. Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day.
languages.arabic: فتشاوروا وإبتاعوا بها حقل الفخارى مقبرة للغرباء ولذلك دعى اسم ذلك الحقل: حقل الدم إلى هذا اليوم.

languages.coptic: Tote afjwk[oic e~bol n~je v/e~tafjof e~bolhitotf n~Ieremiac pip~rov/t/c efjw m~moc je au[i n~]map n~hat ]tim/ n~te v/e~tauertim/ e~rof n~je nens/ri m~p~iIcra/l.
languages.english: Then was fulfilled what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet, saying, “And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the value of Him who was priced, whom they of the children of Israel priced,
languages.arabic: حينئذ تم ما قيل بأرميا النبى القائل أخذوا الثلاثين من الفضة ثمن المثمن الذي ثمنه بنو إسرائيل.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aut/itou qa piiohi m~pikerameuc: kata v~r/] e~taf ouahcahnin/i n~je P[oic:
languages.english: and gave them for the potter’s field, as the LORD directed me.”
languages.arabic: ودفعوها عن حقل الفخارى كما أمرنى الرب.

languages.coptic: I/couc de afohi e~ratf m~pem~yo m~pihugemwn ouoh afsenf n~je pihugemwn efjw m~moc: je n~yok pe p~ouro n~te niIoudai: I/couc de pejaf je n~yok peetjw m~moc:
languages.english: Now Jesus stood before the governor. And the governor asked Him, saying, “Are You the King of the Jews?” Jesus said to him, “It is as you say.”
languages.arabic: فوقف يسوع أمام الوالى فسأله الوالى قائلاً. أأنت ملك اليهود؟ فقال يسوع له: أنت قلت.

languages.coptic: Ouoh qen p~jiny~rou erkat/gorin e~rof n~je niar,/e~reuc nem nip~recbuteroc m~pefe~rouw~ n~h~li.
languages.english: And while He was being accused by the chief priests and elders, He answered nothing.
languages.arabic: وفيما كان رؤساء الكهنة والشيوخ يشتكون عليه لم يجب بشئ.

languages.coptic: Tote peje Pilatoc naf je n~k~cwtem an je ceermeyre qarok n~ou/r:
languages.english: Then Pilate said to Him, “Do You not hear how many things they testify against You?”
languages.arabic: فقال له بيلاطس: أما تسمع كم يشهدون عليك؟

languages.coptic: Ouoh m~pefe~rouw~ naf h~li n~caji hwcte n~tefers~v/ri n~je pihugemwn.
languages.english: But He answered him not one word, so that the governor marveled greatly.
languages.arabic: فلم يجبه عن كلمة واحدة. حتى تعجب الوالى جداً.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab kata Markon a~giou.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel according to Saint Mark. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للأنجيل المقدس حسب معلمنا مار مرقس البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Markon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Mark. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار مرقس البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh catotou avco[ni n~ouco[ni n~swrp n~je niar,/ e~reuc nem nip~recbuteroc nem nicaq nem piman ]hap t/rf:
languages.english: Immediately, in the morning, the chief priests held a consultation with the elders and scribes and the whole council;
languages.arabic: وللوقت فى الصباح تشاور رؤساء الكهنة مع الشيوخ والكتبة والمحفل كله.

languages.coptic: e~aucwnh n~I/couc au[itf aut/if e~Pilatoc:
languages.english: and they bound Jesus, led Him away, and delivered Him to Pilate.
languages.arabic: وأوثقوا يسوع ومضوا به ودفعوه إلى بيلاطس.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afsenf n~je Pilatoc: je n~yok pe p~ouro n~te niIoudai: n~yof de afe~rouw naf pejaf je n~yok petjw m~moc.
languages.english: Then Pilate asked Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?” He answered and said to him, “It is as you say.”
languages.arabic: فسأله بيلاطس: هل أنت ملك اليهود. أما هو فأجابه قائلاً: أنت قلت.

languages.coptic: Ouoh nauerkat/gorin n~hanm/s qarof n~je niar,/e~reuc: Pilatoc de on afsini m~mof efjwmmoc: je n~k~e~rouw~ n~h~li an: anau je ceerkat/gorin e~rok n~ou/r:
languages.english: And the chief priests accused Him of many things, but He answered nothing. Then Pilate asked Him again, saying, “Do You answer nothing? See how many things they testify against You!”
languages.arabic: وكان رؤساء الكهنة يشتكون عليه كثيراً. ثم سأله بيلاطس ثانية قائلاً: أما تجيب بشئ؟ أنظر كم يشكونك.

languages.coptic: I/couc de m~pefe~rouw~ n~h~li: hwcte n~tefers~v/ri n~je Pilatoc.
languages.english: But Jesus still answered nothing, so that Pilate marveled.
languages.arabic: أما يسوع فلم يعد يجبه بشئ حتى تعجب بيلاطس.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab kata Loukan a~giou.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel according to Saint Luke. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للأنجيل المقدس حسب معلمنا مار لوقا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Loukan a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Luke. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار لوقا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~ta pih~oou swpi auywou] n~je nip~recbuteroc n~te pilaoc: nem niar,/e~reuc nem nicaq: ouoh auenf e~qoun e~pouman~]hap:
languages.english: As soon as it was day, the elders of the people, both chief priests and scribes, came together and led Him into their council, saying, “If You are the Christ, tell us.”
languages.arabic: ولما كان النهار إجتمع شيوخ الشعب ورؤساء الكهنة والكتبة وأدخلوه إلى محفلهم قائلين: إن كنت المسيح فقل لنا.

languages.coptic: Eujw m~moc je icje n~yok pe Pi,~rictoc a~joc nan pejafje nwou je aisanjoc nwten tetennanah] an: Eswp de on aisan sen y/nou teten nae~rouw~ an.
languages.english: But He said to them, “If I tell you, you will by no means believe. And if I also ask you, you will by no means answer Me or let Me go.
languages.arabic: فقال لهم: إن قلت لكم لاتؤمنون. وإن سألتكم لا تجيبونى ولا تطلقونى.

languages.coptic: Icjen ]nou de efe~swpi n~je p~s/ri m~v~rwmi efhemci caoui~nam n~]jom n~te Vnou]: Pejwou de t/rou je n~yok oun pe p~s/ri m~Vnou]:
languages.english: Hereafter the Son of Man will sit on the right hand of the power of God.” Then they all said, “Are You then the Son of God?”
languages.arabic: ولكن من الآن يكون إبن الآنسان جالساً عن يمين قوة الله. فقال الجميع: أفأنت إبن الله؟

languages.coptic: Nyof de pejaf nwou je n~ywten etjw m~moc je a~nok pe: Nywou le pejwou je n~tener,ria~ an je n~oumetmeyre: a~non gar ancwtem e~bolqen rwf.
languages.english: So He said to them, “You rightly say that I am.” And they said, “What further testimony do we need? For we have heard it ourselves from His own mouth.”
languages.arabic: فقال لهم: أنتم تقولون إنى أنا هو. أما هم فقالوا: ما حاجتنا إلى شهادة. فاننا نحن قد سمعنا من فمه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aftwnf n~je poum/s t/rf auenf haPilatoc:
languages.english: Then the whole multitude of them arose and led Him to Pilate.
languages.arabic: فقام جميع جمهورهم وجاءوا به إلى بيلاطس.

languages.coptic: Auerh/tc de n~erkat/gopin e~rof eujw m~moc: ouoh eftahno e~stem ]hw] m~p~ouro efjw m~moc e~rof je a~nok pe Pi,~rictoc Pouro:
languages.english: And they began to accuse Him, saying, “We found this fellow perverting the nation, and forbidding to pay taxes to Caesar, saying that He Himself is Christ, a King.”
languages.arabic: وطفقوا يشتكون عليه قائلين: إننا وجدنا هذا يفسد أمتنا ويمنع أن نؤدى الجزية لقيصر ويدعى إنه هو المسيح الملك.

languages.coptic: Pilatoc de afsenaf efjw m~moc: je n~yok pe p~ouro n~te niIoudai: n~yof de afe~rouw~ je n~yok pe etjw m~moc.
languages.english: Then Pilate asked Him, saying, “Are You the King of the Jews?” He answered him and said, “It is as you say.”
languages.arabic: فسأله بيلاطس قائلاً: هل أنت ملك اليهود؟ أما هو فأجابه قائلاً: أنت قلت.

languages.coptic: Pilatoc de pejaf n~niar,/ e~reuc nem nim/s: jen~]jem h~li n~etia~ an qen pairwmi:
languages.english: So Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowd, “I find no fault in this Man.”
languages.arabic: فقال بيلاطس لرؤساء الكهنة وللجموع: إنى لم أجد على هذا الرجل علة.

languages.coptic: Nywou de naujemjom eujw m~moc je f~s~yorter m~pilaoc: ef]c~bw qen ]Ioudea~ t/rc: ouoh aferh/tc icjen ]Galilea~ sa paima: Pilatoc de e~tafcwtem afsini je an ou Galileoc pe pairwmi.
languages.english: But they were the more fierce, saying, “He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee to this place.” When Pilate heard of Galilee, he asked if the Man were a Galilean.
languages.arabic: وأما هم فكانوا يشددون قائلين: إنه يهيَّج الشعب. إذ يعلم فى اليهودية كلها. مبتدءاً من الجليل إلى هنا. فلما سمع بيلاطس ذكر الجليل سأل: أهذا الرجل جليلى؟

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafe~mi je oue~bol pe qen p~ersisi n~te /~rwd/c pe: afouorpf ha Prwd/c: ef,/ hwf qen Ieroucal/m qen pie~hoou e~te m~mau:
languages.english: And as soon as he knew that He belonged to Herod’s jurisdiction, he sent Him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time.
languages.arabic: ولما علم إنه من سلطنة هيرودس، أرسله إلى هيرودس إذ كان فى تلك الأيام فى أورشليم.

languages.coptic: ?rwd/c de e~tafnau e~I/couc afrasi e~masw: nafouws gar e~nau e~rof ic oum/s n~,~ronoc: Eybe je nafcwtem eyb/tf: ouoh naf erhelpic e~nau e~oum/ini n~totf efi~ri m~mof.
languages.english: Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad; for he had desired for a long time to see Him, because he had heard many things about Him, and he hoped to see some miracle done by Him.
languages.arabic: لما رأى هيرودس يسوع فرح جداً لأنه من زمان طويل كان يشتهى أن يراه لسماعه عنه أشياء كثيرة. وكان يرجو أن يرى منه آية يصنعها له.

languages.coptic: Nafsini de m~mof qen hanm/s n~caji: n~yof de m~pefe~rouw~ n~h~li: Nauohi de e~ratou pe n~je nia~r,/e~reuc nem nicaq: euerkat/gorin e~rof e~masw.
languages.english: Then he questioned Him with many words, but He answered him nothing. And the chief priests and scribes stood and vehemently accused Him.
languages.arabic: فجعل يسأله كثيراً فلم يجبه بشئ. وكان رؤساء الكهنة والكتبة واقفين يشكونه بلجاجة.

languages.coptic: Etafsosf de n~je pike ?rwd/c nem nefmatoi: ouoh e~tafcwbi m~mof afjolhf n~ouhebcw ecveriw~ou: afouorpf e~Pilatoc.
languages.english: Then Herod, with his men of war, treated Him with contempt and mocked Him, arrayed Him in a gorgeous robe, and sent Him back to Pilate.
languages.arabic: فازدراه هيرودس مع جنوده. وهزأ به وألبسه برفيراً, ورده إلى بيلاطس.

languages.coptic: Auers~v/r de e~nouer/ou[oic /rwd/c nem Pilatoc qen pie~hoou e~tem~mau: nausop gar n~sorp pe qen oumetjaji nem noue~r/ou.
languages.english: That very day Pilate and Herod became friends with each other, for previously they had been at enmity with each other.
languages.arabic: فصار بيلاطس وهيرودس صديقين مع بعضهما فى ذلك اليوم. لأنهما كانا من قبل متعاديين

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab kata Iwann/n a~giou.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel according to Saint John. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للأنجيل المقدس حسب معلمنا مار يوحنا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Iwann/n a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint John. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار يوحنا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Aui~ni oun n~i/couc e~bol ha Kaia~va e~qoun e~pip~retwrion: ne swrp de pe: ouoh n~ywou m~poui~ e~qoun e~pip~retwrion: hina je n~noucwf satououwm m~pipac,a.
languages.english: Then they led Jesus from Caiaphas to the Praetorium, and it was early morning. But they themselves did not go into the Praetorium, lest they should be defiled, but that they might eat the Passover.
languages.arabic: وجاءوا بيسوع من عند قيافا إلى داخل الايوان. وكان صبح. فلم يدخلوا إلى دار الولاية لئلا يتنجسوا فيمتنعوا عن أكل الفصح.

languages.coptic: Afi oun e~bolharwou n~je Pilatoc pejaf nwou: je ou n~kat/goria~ e~teteni~ni m~moc e~h~r/i e~jen pairwmi:
languages.english: Pilate then went out to them and said, “What accusation do you bring against this Man?”
languages.arabic: فخرج بيلاطس إليهم وقال لهم: أية شكاية تقدمونها على هذا الرجل؟

languages.coptic: Aue~rouw~ pejwou naf: je e~ne vai oucampethwou an pe: nannat/if nak an pe.
languages.english: They answered and said to him, “If He were not an evildoer, we would not have delivered Him up to you.”
languages.arabic: فأجابوا وقالوا له: لو لم يكن هذا فاعل شر لما أسلمناه إليك.

languages.coptic: Peje Pilatoc nwou je [itf n~ywten ouoh mahap e~rof kata petennomoc: Pejwou nafnje niIoudai: je a~non n~c~se nan an e~qwteb nh~li:
languages.english: Then Pilate said to them, “You take Him and judge Him according to your law.” Therefore the Jews said to him, “It is not lawful for us to put anyone to death,”
languages.arabic: فقال لهم بيلاطس: خذوه أنتم وأحكموا عليه بحسب ناموسكم. فقال له اليهود: نحن لا يجوز لنا أن نقتل أحداً.

languages.coptic: hina n~te p~caji n~I/couc jwk e~bol v/e~tafjof: efercummenin je qen as m~mou f~namou
languages.english: that the saying of Jesus might be fulfilled which He spoke, signifying by what death He would die.
languages.arabic: ليتم قول يسوع الذي قاله، مشيراً إلى أية ميتة كان مزمعاً أن يموتها.

languages.coptic: Palinon afi n~je Pilatoc e~qoun e~pipretwrion ouoh afmou] e~I/couc pejaf naf: je n~yok pe p~ouro n~te niIoudai.
languages.english: Then Pilate entered the Praetorium again, called Jesus, and said to Him, “Are You the King of the Jews?”
languages.arabic: ثم دخل بيلاطس أيضاً إلى دار الولاية ودعا يسوع وقال له: أأنت ملك اليهود؟

languages.coptic: Afe~rouw~ naf n~je I/couc ouoh pejaf: je n~yok etjw m~vai e~bol hitotk sanhan ke,wouni petaujoc nak eyb/t:
languages.english: Jesus answered him, “Are you speaking for yourself about this, or did others tell you this concerning Me?”
languages.arabic: أجاب يسوع وقال: أمن ذآتٍك تقول هذا أم آخرون قالوا لك عنى؟

languages.coptic: Afe~rouw~ n~je Pilatoc efjwm~moc: je m/ a~nok ouIolai hw: pekeynoc m~min m~mok nem niar,/e~reuc n~ywou petau/ik: ou pe e~takaifi:
languages.english: Pilate answered, “Am I a Jew? Your own nation and the chief priests have delivered You to me. What have You done?”
languages.arabic: فأجاب بيلاطس قائلاً: ألعلى أنا أيضاً يهودى؟ إن أمتك ورؤساء الكهنة هم الذين أسلموك إلىّ. فماذا فعلت؟

languages.coptic: Afe~rouw~ n~je I/couc je tametouro a~nok ya paIkocmoc an te: e~ne tametouro oue~bolqen paikocmoc te: nare nahuperet/c na] e~h~r/i e~jwi pe: hina n~touwtem t/it n~niIoudai: ]nou de ta metouro ya paikocmoc an te.
languages.english: Jesus answered, “My kingdom is not of this world. If My kingdom were of this world, My servants would fight, so that I should not be delivered to the Jews; but now My kingdom is not from here.”
languages.arabic: أجاب يسوع: إن مملكتى ليست من هذا العالم. ولو كانت كذلك لكان خدامى يجاهدون عنى لكى لا أسلم إلى اليهود. والآن فإن مملكتى ليست من هذا العالم.

languages.coptic: Pilatoc oun pejaf je m/ oun n~yok ououro:
languages.english: Pilate therefore said to Him, “Are You a king then?”
languages.arabic: فقال له بيلاطس: أملك إذا أنت؟

languages.coptic: afe~rouw n~je I/couc je n~yok petjw m~moc je a~nok ououro: ke gar e~taumact a~nok e~paihwb: ouoh eybe vai aii~ e~pikocmoc e~p~ jintaermetmeyre n~]meym/i: ouon niben e~te han e~bol qen ]meym/i saucwtem e~tac~m/:
languages.english: Jesus answered, “You say rightly that I am a king. For this cause I was born, and for this cause I have come into the world, that I should bear witness to the truth. Everyone who is of the truth hears My voice.”
languages.arabic: أجاب يسوع: أنت قلت إنى ملك. وإنى لهذا العمل قد ولدت. ولهذا قد أتيت إلى العالم لاشهد للحق. فكل من هو من الحق يسمع صوتى.

languages.coptic: Peje Pilatoc naf je ou pe ]meym/i: ouoh vai e~tafjof palin on ae~bolha niIoudai pejaf nwou: je a~nok n~]jem h~li n~e~ti~a an qen pairwmi:
languages.english: Pilate said to Him, “What is truth?” And when he had said this, he went out again to the Jews, and said to them, “I find no fault in Him at all.“
languages.arabic: فقال له بيلاطس: ما هو الحق؟ قال هذا وخرج إلى اليهود وقال لهم: أننى لا أجد علة واحدة فى هذا الرجل.

languages.coptic: Tetencun/yia~ de hina n~ta,a ouai nwten e~bol qen pipac,a tetenouws oun n~ta,w nwten e~bol m~p~ouro n~te niIoudai:
languages.english: But you have a custom that I should release someone to you at the Passover. Do you therefore want me to release to you the King of the Jews?”
languages.arabic: وأن لكم عادة أن أطلق لكم واحداً فى الفصح. أفتريدون أن أطلق لكم ملك اليهود؟

languages.coptic: Auws de e~bol t/rou eujw m~moc:je m~per,a vai e~bol: alla ,a Barabbac e~bol: pai Barabbac de ne ouconi pe.
languages.english: Then they all cried again, saying, “Not this Man, but Barabbas!” Now Barabbas was a robber.
languages.arabic: فصرخوا جميعاً قائلين: لا تخلى هذا. بل إطلق باراباس وكان باراباس لصاً.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: At the time when Jesus, God incarnate, was on earth, the Jews were under the rule of the Romans. Pontius Pilate was their governor as representative of the Roman Emperor, Tiberius. When the Jews arrested Jesus, brought Him to the High Priest’s house at night, and condemned Him to death, they could have executed their judgment. However, fearing the anger of the Jewish people, they decided to deliver Him to Pilate to rid themselves of the responsibility of killing Him, “And when they had bound Him, they led Him away and delivered Him to Pontius Pilate the governor”
languages.arabic: كان اليهود في الزمن الذي ظهر فيه المسيح على الأرض خاضعين للحكم الروماني. وكان بيلاطس البنطي والياً عليهم من قبل طيباريوس قيصر ملك الرومان. فلما قَبض اليهود على السيد المسيح وجاءوا به إلى دار رئيس الكهنة ليلا وقضوا عليه بالموت. وقد كان يُمكنهم تنفيذ ما قضوا به إلا أنهم خوفاً من هياج الشعب ضدهم وليلقوا تبعة موته عنهم، انتهت مشورتهم إلى تسليمه لبيلاطس الوالي "فأوثقوه ومضوا به ودفعوه إلى بيلاطس البنطي الوالي".

languages.english: This occurred in the morning. “But they themselves did not go into the Praetorium, lest they should be defiled, but that they might eat the Passover.” Pilate therefore came out to them and asked them what they wanted. They answered that they had come with this guilty man who the Sanhedrin had condemned to die by crucifixion by. Pilate asked them, "What accusation do you bring against this Man?" They answered, "We found this fellow perverting the nation, and forbidding them to pay taxes to Caesar, saying that He Himself is Christ, a King."
languages.arabic: وكان ذلك في الصباح. "ولم يدخلوا هم إلى دار الولاية لكي لا يتنجسوا فيأكلون الفصح". فخرج إليهم بيلاطس وسألهم ماذا تريدون؟ فقالوا جئنا بهذا الإنسان المذنب وقد حَكمَ عليه مَجمعْ السنهدريم بالموت صلباً. فقال لهم: "أية شكاية تُقدمون على هذا الإنسان". فقالوا له "إننا وجدنا هذا يَفسد الأمة ويَمنع أن تُعطىَّ الجزية لقيصر قائلا أنه هو مسيح ملك".

languages.english: Their accusation had two-fold. First, perverting the nation, which is obviously false because the Lord Christ always promoted love, to the forgiveness of trespasses and bearing offenses. Since Pilate had already heard about the teachings of Christ in this regard, he gave no attention to the first accusation. The second fold of the accusation can also be divided into two points. First, that He was encouraging the people not to pay taxes to Caesar. This was a lie since Pilate heard that Jesus said, “Render therefore to Caesar the things that are Caesar's,” The second point regarding the “King” terrified Pilate and concerned him.
languages.arabic: وتشتمل هذه الشكاية على تهمتين: الأولى إفساده الأمة وهذه باطلة من طبعها لأن السيد المسيح كان دائما يحث على المحبة والإغضاء عن الإساءة وتحمل الأذى. ولما كان بيلاطس بالطبع قد سمع بعض تعاليمه بهذا الخصوص فقد أغضى عن هذه التهمة ولم يعرها أذناً صاغية. والتهمة الثانية تحتوي على فقرتين: الأولي منهما أنه يمنع أن تعطى الجزية لقيصر. وقد كذبوا فيما ادعوا لأن بيلاطس قد سمع عنه أنه قال لهم: "أعطوا ما لقيصر لقيصر". وأما الفقرة الثانية الخاصة بالملك فهذه قد أرعبت بيلاطس وجعلته أن يهتم بها.

languages.english: “Then Pilate entered the Praetorium again, called Jesus, and said to Him, "Are You the King of the Jews?" Jesus answered him, "Are you speaking for yourself about this, or did others tell you this concerning Me?" Pilate answered, "Am I a Jew? Your own nation and the chief priests have delivered You to me. What have You done?" Jesus answered, "My kingdom is not of this world” which means that Jesus’ kingdom is a spiritual kingdom in which He rules over the hearts. “Pilate therefore said to Him, "Are You a king then?" Jesus answered, "You say rightly that I am a king.” Because I am truly King, I reign over the believers with faith and grace and I lead them to the kingdom of Heaven.
languages.arabic: "ثم دخل بيلاطس إلى دار الولاية ودعا يسوع وقال له: أأنت ملك اليهود. أجابه يسوع أمن ذاتك تقول هذا أم آخرون قالوا لك عني. أجابه بيلاطس ألعلي أنا يهودي. أمُتك ورؤساء الكهنة أسلموك إليَّ. أجابه يسوع مملكتي ليست من هذا العالم" وإنما هي مملكة روحية أسود بها على القلوب. "فقال بيلاطس أفأنت إذن ملك؟ أجاب يسوع أنت تقول أني ملك" لأنني حقاً كذلك فإني أملك على المؤمنين بالإيمان والنعمة وأقودهم إلى مملكة السماء.

languages.english: “And while He was being accused by the chief priests and elders, He answered nothing.” To fulfill the prophecy “But I, like a deaf man, do not hear; And I am like a mute who does not open his mouth. Thus I am like a man who does not hear, And in whose mouth is no response.” And the saying, “He was led as a lamb to the slaughter, And as a sheep before its shearers is silent, So He opened not His mouth.”
languages.arabic: وبينما كان رؤساء الكهنة والشيوخ يشتكون عليه لم يُجب بشيء" ليتم قول الكتاب "وأما أنا فكأصم: لا أسمع. وكأبكم: لا يفتح فاه. وأكون مثل إنسان لا يسمع". وقوله: "ولم يفتح فاه كشاة تُساق إلى الذبح وكنعجة صامتة أمام جازيها فلم يفتح فاه".

languages.english: “So Pilate said to the chief priests and the crowd, "I find no fault in this Man." He said this when Jesus told him that His kingdom is a spiritual one and “is not of this world.” However, the Jews were even fiercer, saying, "He stirs up the people, teaching throughout all Judea, beginning from Galilee to this place.
languages.arabic: "وقال بيلاطس لرؤساء الكهنة والجموع إني لا أجد علة في هذا الإنسان". (قال ذلك لما علم من يسوع أن مملكته روحية و"ليست من هذا العالم"). أما رؤساء الكهنة "فكانوا يُشددون قائلين أنه يُهيج الشعب وهو يُعلم في كل اليهودية مبتدئا من الجليل إلى هنا.

languages.english: When Pilate heard “Galilee,” he asked if the Man were a Galilean." When he realized that Jesus is under Herod's jurisdiction, he wanted to save himself from this critical situation for he was caught between two choices either he releases Him so they report him to Caesar or condemn Him to death with the fear that if Caesar finds out He is innocent he will deal with him harshly. Therefore, when he heard He is a Galilean, he rejoiced and sent Him to Herod, who was also in Jerusalem at that time. Now when Herod saw Jesus, he was exceedingly glad; for he had desired for a long time to see Him, because he had heard many things about Him, and had hoped to see Him do some miracles done. However, the Lord had no purpose except to die for us. He also knew that Herod just wanted to see a miracle for amusement. Herod had no interest in the truth; otherwise, he would have accepted it from John the Baptist. The Lord did not perform any miracles for him. “Then he questioned Him with many words, but He answered him nothing.”
languages.arabic: فلما سمع بيلاطس ذِكر الجليل سأل هل الرجل جليلي. وحين علم أنه من سلطنة هيرودس" أراد أن يبرئ نفسه ويُخلصها من هذا المأزق الحرج لأنه كان بين عاملين: إن أطلقه فيشكونه لقيصر، وإن حَكم عليه بالموت فيخاف أن يعلم القيصر ببراءته فيناله منه ما يناله ولذلك فإنه لما سمع أنه من الجليل فرح جداً و"أرسله إلى هيرودس" رئيس ربع الجليل وكان يومئذ "في أورشليم" ليحضر عيد الفصح. "وأما هيرودس فلما رأى يسوع فرح جداً لأنه كان يريد من زمان طويل أن يراه لسماعه عنه أشياء كثيرة وتَرجى أن يرى آية تُصنع منه". فلأن الرب لم يكن له غرض سوى الموت عنا ولأنه يعلم أيضاً أن هيرودس يريد أن يلهو بما يراه من الآيات لأنه لو كان قابلاً للحق لقبِلَهُ من يوحنا المعمدان. لم يصنع أمامه آية "وسأله بكلام كثير فلم يجبه بشيء".

languages.english: When the chief priests witnessed this, they were happy, “and vehemently accused Him.” Hoping that Herod would sentence Him but he refused. Then either to revenge himself of Jesus because He had refused to perform a miracle in front of him or to please the Jews, he “treated Him with contempt and mocked Him, arrayed Him in a gorgeous robe, and sent Him back to Pilate,” This proves that Herod also found Him blameless fulfilling the prophet’s words, “The kings of the earth set themselves, And the rulers take counsel together, Against the Lord and against His Anointed,” “Kings of the earth” refers to Pilate and Herod. “The rulers” refers to the chief Priests of the Jews and their conspiracy against God and His anointed One because every insult addressed to the Son is also addressed to the Father as He said, glory be to Him, “He who does not honor the Son does not honor the Father who sent Him.” Pilate and Herod were sworn enemies but by sending Christ to one another, they were reconciled, “That very day Pilate and Herod became friends.” The Lord came to the world to reconcile man with his God and bring peace among kings.
languages.arabic: فلما رأى رؤساء الكهنة سكوت يسوع فرحوا وأخذوا "يشتكون عليه باشتداد". وكان غرضهم من هيرودس أن يحكم عليه ولكنه أبى ذلك. وإنما أراد أن يُشفي غليله منه لأنه لم يُجبه إلى طلبه من جهة ويرضي اليهود من جهة أخرى. "فاحتقره... مع عسكره واستهزأ به وألبسه لباساً لامعاً ورده إلى بيلاطس". وهذه دلالة على أنه وجده بريئاً. وقد تم قول النبي: "قام ملوك الأرض وتآمر الرؤساء معاً على الرب ومسيحه". فقوله: "ملوك الأرض" يريد بهم بيلاطس وهيرودس. والرؤساء يريد بهم رؤساء كهنة اليهود وتآمرهم على الرب وعلى مسيحه لأن كل إساءة إلى الابن هي لأبيه كما قال له المجد: "من لا يُكرِّم الابن لا يُكرِّم الآب الذي أرسله". ولأنه كان يوجد بين بيلاطس وهيرودس عداوة شديدة فبتبادل المسيح بينهما حصل الصلح وصارا "صديقين مع بعضهما". لأن الرب لهذا جاء إلى العالم أن يكون سبب الصلح بين آدم وخالقه وبين الملوك وبعضهم.

languages.english: “Then Judas, His betrayer, seeing that He had been condemned, was remorseful and brought back the thirty pieces of silver to the chief priests and elders, Saying, "I have sinned by betraying innocent blood,"” revealing his horrible deed and the error of the Jews when they condemned Jesus who was innocent, “And they said, "What is that to us? You see to it!" Then he threw the pieces of silver in the temple then departed, and went and hanged himself.”
languages.arabic: "حينئذ لما رأى يهوذا الذي أسلمه أنه قد دينَ نَدمَ ورد الثلاثين من الفضة إلى الكهنة والشيوخ. قائلا قد أخطأت إذ سَلَّمت دماً بريئاً". وقد أوضح بذلك فظاعة الأمر الذي أتاه وغَلط اليهود في حكمهم على يسوع وهو برئ. "فقالوا ماذا علينا. أنت أبصَر. فطَرح الفضة في الهيكل وأنصرف ثم مضى وخَنق نفسه".

languages.english: It is understood that Judas was one of the twelve disciples and was responsible for the moneybox. So when the devil noted Judas’ love for silver, he made him steal from the box that was in his possession, to the extent that he was upset at the woman who rubbed the feet of the Lord with an expensive perfume wishing that the cost of the perfume would have gotten into the box so that he could dip into it. When the Lord rebuked the disciples who agreed with Judas’ corrupt opinion, Judas resented the Lord even more and hated Him enough to deliver Him into the hands of the Jews. But when he saw Him condemned to death he regretted what he had done and returned the silver pieces. When the devil sensed his regret, he made him think that his deed could not be forgiven except if he killed himself. He therefore made for himself a hanging device on a high place. He put the rope around his neck and hanged himself but the rope did not tighten enough around his neck to kill him. He continued to breathe in but not out. He became inflated and was in a very miserable condition. Because of his disturbance, the rope broke and he fell to the ground, “and falling headlong, he burst open in the middle and all his entrails gushed out.” He lost his life on earth and in the age to come and fulfilled the words of the Bible, “When he is judged, let him be found guilty, And let his prayer become sin Let his days be few, And let another take his office Let his children be fatherless, And his wife a widow”
languages.arabic: والمفهوم أن يهوذا كان ضمن تلاميذ السيد الإثني عشر وكان صندوق النفقة عنده. فلما وجد فيه الشيطان ميلاً إلى محبة الفضة جعله يسرق من الصندوق الذي كان معه حتى أنه تذمر على الإمرأة التي دَهنت قدمي الرب بالطيِب وتمنى لو أنه حصل على هذا المبلغ في الصندوق لينال منه جزء. ولما وبخ السيد بالتلاميذ الذين وافقوه على رأيه الفاسد ازدادت بغضته للسيد بعد المحبة المفرطة تلك البغضة التي جعلته يُسَلم سيده إلى اليهود. ولكنه لما رأى أنهم قد حكموا عليه بالموت. نَدم على فعلهِ وأعاد الفضة إليهم. فلما رأى الشيطان أنه قد ندم جعل في قلبه فكراً بأن لا غفران لذنبه إلا بقتل نفسه. فَعمَل مشنقة لنفسه في مكان عالٍ عن الأرض ثم جعلها في رقبته وتعلق بها. فلم تخنقه خنقاً يوجب الموت وصار يستنشق الهواء ولم يستطع أن يتنفسه صاعداً حتى إنتفخ وصار في كرب شديد ومن كثرة اضطرابه انقطعت به المشنقة فسقط إلى الأرض و"انشقَ من الوسط فانسكَبت أحشاؤه كلها". وهلك من الدهرين. هنا والآتي: وفاتته الحياتان وتمَّ فيه قول الكتاب: "وصلاته فلتكُن خطيئة. لتكن أيامه قليلة ووظيفته ليأخذها آخر. ليكن بنوه أيتاماً وامرأته أرملة".

languages.english: “But the chief priests took the silver pieces and said, "It is not lawful to put them into the treasury, because they are the price of blood." This verse indicates that the chief Priests of the Jews realized their error and realized that the silver Judas returned could not be accepted as offerings, “And they consulted together and bought with them the potter's field, to bury strangers in it” they did this to hide their evil deed under the pretext of strangers, “Therefore that field has been called the Field of Blood to this day,” because with its price they bought the blood of Christ and killed him. “Then was fulfilled what was spoken by Jeremiah the prophet, saying, "And they took the thirty pieces of silver, the value of Him who was priced, whom they of the children of Israel priced, and gave them for the potter's field, as the Lord directed me."” The prophet had already foretold of this evil that the Jews willingly committed. We ask our Lord to make us always unconfident of ourselves so that we do not fall into sin and that we continuously cling and hold onto the faith in His name and our hope in Him. Glory be to Him Forever Amen.
languages.arabic: "فأخذ رؤساء الكهنة الفضة وقالوا لا يحل أن نلقيها في الخزانة لأنها ثمن دمٍ". ومن ذلك نعلم أن رؤساء كهنة اليهود قد شهدوا على أنفسهم بالخطأ وأن الفضة التي أعادها يهوذا لا يحل أن تُجعل في موضع قرابينهم "فتشاوروا واشتروا بها حقل الفخاري مقبرة للغرباء ليستروا عملهم القبيح باسم الغرباء. "لهذا سمى ذلك الحقل حقل الدم إلى هذا اليوم" لأنه بثمنه ابتاعوا دم المسيح وقتلوه. "حينئذ تم ما قيل بأرمياء النبي القائل وأخذوا الثلاثين من الفضة ثمن المُثمَّنْ الذي ثَمَّنوه من بني إسرائيل. وأعطوها عن حقل الفخاري كما أمرني الرب". وقد سبق فقال النبي هذه النبوة عن هذا الشر الذي ارتكبه اليهود بحرِيتهم ونسأل إلهنا أن يجعلنا دائماً غير واثقين من أنفسنا حتى لا نسقط في هاوية الخطيئة وأن نكون دائماً متعلقين به ومتمسكين بالإيمان باسمه والرجاء به، له المجد دائماً أبدياً آمين.

languages.english: The exposition of the First Hour of Great Friday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة الاولى من يوم الجمعة العظيمة من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: Early on Friday, the chief priests convened together and condemned the Savior so that they may deliver Him to Pilate to kill Him.
languages.arabic: باكر يوم الجمعة إجتمع رؤساء الكهنة معاً وصنعوا الحكم على المخلص لكى يسلموه إلى بيلاطس ليقتله.

languages.english: When he saw what he did, Judas regretted what he did and returned the silver to the chief priests saying; "I have sinned in betraying innocent blood."
languages.arabic: فيهوذا لما نظر ما كان ندم على ما فعله وأعاد الفضة إلى رؤساء الكهنة قائلاً: إننى ألقيت للحكم دماً ذكياً.

languages.english: He went and hung himself adding a transgression to a transgression. The chief priests took the silver and bought with it a potter's field to bury strangers in,
languages.arabic: فمضى وخنق نفسه وحده وأكمل إثماً على إثم. فأخذ رؤساء الكهنة الفضة وابتاعوا بها حقل الفاخورى

languages.english: So that it may be fulfilled what had been spoken about the thirty pieces of silver; the price of the Precious One.
languages.arabic: لكى يكمل الذي قيل من أجل الثلاثين من الفضة ثمن الذكى.

languages.english: They stood Jesus before Pilate, who asked Him, "Are You a king?" Jesus replied, "You have said so." 'They accused Him a lot but He gave no answer. "Do you hear all this testimony?" Pilate asked. He did not open his mouth to utter a word. The governor wondered greatly at His calmness and silence.
languages.arabic: فأقاموا يسوع أمام بيلاطس فسأله: أأنت ملك؟ أنت قلت أم آخر أخبرك؟ أنا آتٍيت يا بيلاطس من أجل الحق. فإشتكوا عليه فلم يجب المبارك بشئ - أما تسمع هذه الشهادات الكثيرة؟ فلم يفتح فمه ليقول كلمة. فتعجب الوالى جداً من أجل صمته وهدوءه.

languages.english: How can the Lamb who came to take away the sins of the world open His mouth. Isaiah prophesied about him saying, "By oppression and judgment he was taken away. O Jerusalem, you killer of prophets, your dwellers will weep for you today." He is not a prophet but God, and His blood wipes off the sins.
languages.arabic: كيف يفتح الحمل فاه الذي آتٍى ليحمل خطايا العالم؟ وقد شهد إشعياء من أجله قائلاً: أن مداينته قد ارتفعت مثل الخروف. قد كملت اليوم هذه النبوة فى أورشليم فى وسط إسرائيل. يبكى عليك بنوك اليوم يا أورشليم يا قاتلة الأنبياء. هذا ليس نبى لكنه إله ودمه يمحى الآثام.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Pjom n~}genecic n~te Mwu~c/c pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from the Book of Genesis of Moses the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من سفر التکوين لموسى النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Acswpi de meneca nai caji autame Iwc/f eujw m~moc: je pekiwt s~yorter: ouoh af[i m~pefs/ri c~nau Manacc/ nem Evrem afi~ ha Iakwb:
languages.english: Now it came to pass after these things that Joseph was told, "Indeed your father is sick"; and he took with him his two sons, Manasseh and Ephraim, and went to Jacob.
languages.arabic: وحدث بعد هذه الأمور أن أخبروا يوسف قائلين: أن أباك مضطرب. فأخذ إبنيه منسى وإفرايم وجاء إلى يعقوب.

languages.coptic: Autame Iakwb de eujw m~moc naf: je h/p peic peks/ri Iwc/f fn/ou harok: afjemnom] n~je p~iIcra/l afhemci hijen pi[~loj:
languages.english: Thus Jacob was told, "Look, your son Joseph is coming to you"; and Israel strengthened himself and sat up on the bed.
languages.arabic: فأخبروا يعقوب قائلين له: هوذا إبنك يوسف قادم إليك. فتشدد اسرائيل وجلس على السرير.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Iakwb n~Iwc/f je panou] afouonhf e~roi qen Louza: qen p~kahi n~<anaan afc~mou e~roi:
languages.english: Then Jacob said to Joseph," My God appeared to me at Luz in the land of Canaan and blessed me,
languages.arabic: فقال يعقوب ليوسف: أن إلهى ظهر لى فى لوز فى أرض كنعان وباركنى,

languages.coptic: Ouoh pejaf n/i je h/ppe eie~eyrek a~sai eie~aik n~hanywoutc n~s~lol: ouoh eie~] m~paikahi nak: nem pekjroj menencwk eua~mahi sae~neh:
languages.english: And said to me, 'Behold, I will increase and multiply you; and I will make of you a gathering of nations, and give this land to your seed after you as an everlasting possession.'
languages.arabic: وقال لى: ها أنا أنميك وأكثرك وأجعلك جمهور من الأمم. وأعطى هذه الأرض لكل نسلك من بعدك ملكاً أبدياً.

languages.coptic: ]nou on peks/ri c~nau e~takswpi nak qen </mi m~pa]i~ harok e~</mi noui~ ne: Evrem nem Manacc/: m~v~r/] n~Roub/n. nem m~v~r/] n~Cumewn: Nijvo de e~tek naj~vwou:
languages.english: "Now therefore, your two sons, Ephraim and Manasseh, who were born to you in the land of Egypt before I came to you in Egypt, are mine; as Reuben and Simeon, they shall be mine.
languages.arabic: والآن فإبناك اللذان صارا لك فى أرض مصر قبل قدومى إليك إلى أرض مصر هما لي. إفرايم ومنسى مثل رأوبين وشمعون يكونان لى

languages.coptic: Menenca nai eue~swpi nak: eue~mou] e~rwou e~v~ran n~nouc~n/ou n~h~r/i qen touk~l/ronomia~.
languages.english: Your offspring whom you beget after them shall take possession of their inheritances within the tribes of their brothers.
languages.arabic: ومن يولد بعدهما من البنين فأنه يكونون لك ويدعون بإسم إخوتهم فى ميراثهم.

languages.coptic: Anok de ein/ou e~bolqen ]mecopodia~ n~te ]curia: acmou n~je Ra,/l tekmau qen p~kahi n~<anaan: einaqwnt e~pip~rodromoc n~te <abraoa: ouoh aioomcc hipimwit n~te pip~rodromoc n~te B/yleem.
