languages.english: But as for me, when I came from Mesopotamia of Syria, Rachel died beside me in the land of Canaan on the way, when there was but a little distance to go to Ephrath; and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath" (that is, Bethlehem).
languages.arabic: وأنا حين جئت من بين النهرين السوريين. ماتت راحيل أمك فى أرض كنعان. وأنا قريب من حدود أفراثا. ودفنتها على قارعة طريق بيت لحم.

languages.coptic: Etafnau de n~je pIicra/l e~nens/ri n~Iwc/f pejafje nim n~tak ne nai.
languages.english: Then Israel saw Joseph's sons, and said, "Who are these with you?"
languages.arabic: ولما رأى اسرائيل إبنى يوسف قال: من هذان؟

languages.coptic: Peje Iwc/f de m~pefiwt: je nas/ri n/e~ta Vnou] t/itou n/i m~paima: ouoh peje Iakwb je a~nitou haroi hina n~tac~mou erwou.
languages.english: So Joseph said to his father, "They are my sons God gave me here." Jacob then said, "Bring them to me that I may bless them."
languages.arabic: فقال يوسف لإبيه: هما ولداى اللذان وهبهما الله لي ههنا. فقال يعقوب: قدمهما إليّ لإباركهما.

languages.coptic: Ne auh~ros de pe n~je nenbal n~Iakwb e~bolqen ]metqello: ouoh nafs~jemjom n~nau m~bol an pe: afy~rouqwnt e~rof af]vi e~rwou ouoh afa~malej e~rwou.
languages.english: Now the eyes of Israel were dim by reason of old age, and he could not see. Then Joseph brought them near him, and he kissed and embraced them.
languages.arabic: وكانت عينا يعقوب قد ثقلتا من الشيخوخة ولا يقدر أن يبصر. فقربهما إليه فقبلهما وإحتضنهما.

languages.coptic: Peje p~iIcra/l n~Iwc/f: je h/ppe m~pousonht e~nau e~pekho: h/ppe a~Vnou] tamoi e~pekjroj.
languages.english: Then Israel said to Joseph, "I had not thought to see your face; but in fact, God has also shown me your seed."
languages.arabic: وقال اسرائيل ليوسف: هوذا أنا لم أحرم من أن أرى وجهك. وهوذا الرب قد أرانى نسلك.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~Iwc/f henou cabol nnefa~lwj auouwst m~mof hijen pouho hijen p~kahi.
languages.english: So Joseph brought them from beside his knees, and they bowed down with their face to the ground.
languages.arabic: ثم أخرجهما يوسف من بين فخذيه. وسجدا له بوجهيهما إلى الأرض.

languages.coptic: AIwc/f de [i m~pefs/ri c~nau af,a Evrem cajaa[/ m~Piicra/l: Manacc/ de caoui~nam m~Piicra/l: afy~rouqwnt e~rof.
languages.english: Then Joseph took them both, Ephraim with his right hand toward Israel's left hand, and Manasseh with his left hand toward Israel's right hand, and brought them near him.
languages.arabic: وأخذ يوسف ولديه وجعل أفرايم عن يسار اسرائيل ومنسى عن يمين اسرائيل، وقربهما إليه.

languages.coptic: Ap~iIcra/l de couten tefjij n~oui~nam e~bol aftaloc e~jen t~a~ve n~Evrem ne vai de pe pikouji: ouoh tefja[/ aftaloc e~jen t~a~ve m~Manacc/: e~afsebt nefjij: Manacc/ de pe pisorp m~mici.
languages.english: Thus Israel stretched out his right hand and laid it on Ephraim's head, who was the younger, and his left hand on Manasseh's head, guiding his hands knowingly, for Manasseh was the firstborn.
languages.arabic: فمد اسرائيل يمينه ووضعها على رأس أفرايم وهو الصغير ويساره على رأس منسى. وخالف يديه مع أن منسى هو البكر.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afc~mou e~rwou pejaf: je Vnou] v/e~ta naio] ranaf m~pefm~yo Abraam nem Icaak: Vnou] v/e~tafsans m~moi icjen tameta~lou sa e~qoun e~paie~hoou.
languages.english: Then he blessed them and said: "God, before whom my fathers Abraham and Isaac were well-pleasing, the God who has fed me all my life long to this day.
languages.arabic: وباركهما وقال: اللهم الذي أحسن أمامه أبواي إبراهيم وإسحق، الله الذي عالنى منذ صباى إلى هذا اليوم.

languages.coptic: Piaggeloc eynohem m~moi e~bolqen nipethwou t/rou: c~mou e~nai a~lwoui~: ouoh eue~mou] e~paran e~h~r/i e~jwou: nem v~ran n~naio] Abraam nem Icaak: eua~sai n~ounis] n~asai efos hijen p~kahi.
languages.english: The Angel who redeemed me from all evil, bless these lads; let my name be named upon them, and the name of my fathers Abraham and Isaac; and let them grow into a great multitude in the midst of the earth."
languages.arabic: الملاك الذي خلصنى من كل شر، يبارك على هذين الغلامين. ويدعى أسمى عليهما. وأسم أبواى إبراهيم وإسحق. فيكثرا كثرة عظيمة على الأرض.

languages.coptic: Etafnau n~je Iwc/f je a pefiwt tale tefjij n~oui~nam e~jen t~a~ve n~Evrem: a~pihwb h~ros m~pefm~yo: ouoh aIwc/f a~moni n~t~jij mpefiwt afolc e~bol e~jen t~a~ve n~Evrem aftaloc e~jen t~a~ve m~Manacc/.
languages.english: Now when Joseph saw his father put his right hand on the head of Ephraim, it displeased him; so he took hold of his father's hand to remove it from Ephraim's head to Manasseh's head.
languages.arabic: فلما رأى يوسف أن أباه قد وضع يده على رأس أفرايم ثقل الأمر أمامه فأمسك يوسف بيد أبيه لينقلها عن رأس أفرايم إلى منسى.

languages.coptic: Peje Iwc/f de mpefiwt: je pair/] an paiwt: vai gar pe pisorp m~mici: ,a tekjij n~oui~nam e~jen tefa~ve. Ouoh m~pefouws: alla pejaf je: ]e~mi pas/ri ]e~mi.
languages.english: So Joseph said to his father, "Not so, my father, for this one is the firstborn; put your right hand on his head." But his father refused and said, "I know, my son, I know”.
languages.arabic: وقال يوسف لإبيه ليس هكذا يا أبى لإن هذا هو البكر. ضع يمينك على رأسه. فأبى أبوه وقال أنا أعلم يا ولدى أنا أعلم.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen ?ca/~ac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Isaiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من إشعياء النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: P[oic pet]n/i n~oulac n~c~bw: eyrie~mi a~rewan t~ho] n~oucaji swpi: af,w n/i n~hana~tooui~:
languages.english: "The Lord gives Me the tongue of the learned, so as to know when to speak a word at a fitting time; and He causes My ear to listen each morning.
languages.arabic: قد أعطانى السيد الرب لسان العلماء لأعلم إذا صار خوف الكلام. وفى الاسحار جعل لي وزادنى أذناً لاسمع تعليم الرب.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aftouho e~roi n~oumasj eyricwtem e~t~c~bw m~P[oic eyrououwn n~namasj: a~nok de ]legin an. Ai] n~ta[ici e~hanmactigx: ouoh naouoji e~hansenkour: paho de m~pitacyof e~hap~sipi n~te hanyaf.
languages.english: The Lord's instruction opens My ears, and I am not disobedient, nor do I contradict Him. "I gave My back to whips, and My cheeks to blows; and I turned not away My face from the shame of spitting.
languages.arabic: ولكى تنفتح أذناى. وأنا لم أقاوم بل بذلت ظهرى للضرب. وخدى للطم ولم أردُ وجهى عن خزى البصاق.

languages.coptic: P[oic afswpi n/i n~oubo/yoc: eybe vai m~pi[isipi: alla ai,w m~paho m~v~r/] n~oupetra ecjor: ouoh aie~mi je n~na[isipi.
languages.english: The Lord became My helper; therefore, I was not disgraced. But I made My face like a solid rock and knew I would not be ashamed.
languages.arabic: والرب كان لي عوناً لذلك لا أخجل. لكننى جعلت وجهى كالصخرة الصلبة. وعلمت إنى لا أخزى

languages.coptic: Je f~qent n~je v/ety~maioi:
languages.english: For He who pronounces Me righteous draws near.
languages.arabic: لأن الذي يبررنى قريب منى،

languages.coptic: Nim eyna[ihap nem/i marefqwnt e~roi. H/ppe ic P[oic eynaerbo/yin e~roi: nim eynas~t~hemkoi:
languages.english: Who is he who judges Me? Let him come near Me. Behold, the Lord will help Me. Who will harm Me?
languages.arabic: من يحاكمنى فليقترب إليّ. ها الرب يعيننى فمن يؤلمنى.

languages.coptic: H/ppe n~ywten t/rou m~v~r/] n~ouh~bwc tetennaera~pac: ouholi de ece~ouem y/nou.
languages.english: Behold, all of you will grow old like a garment, and old age will devour you, as a moth does a garment.
languages.arabic: ها أنتم كلكم كمثل الثوب تبلون ويأكلكم السوس.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen ?ca/~ac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Isaiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من إشعياء النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoi n~tou'u,/ je auco[ni n~ouco[ni qarwou m~min m~mwou eujw m~moc. Je marencwnh m~piy~m/i je f~oi n~atsau nan:
languages.english: Woe to their soul, for they planned evil upon themselves, saying, "Let us hand over the righteous man, for he is burdensome to us."
languages.arabic: ويل لنفوسهم لأنهم تآمروا وتآمروا على نفوسهم قائلين: لنوثق البار فأنه غير نافع لنا.

languages.coptic: }nou de eue~ouwm n~nioutah n~te nih~b/oui~ n~te noujij. Ouoi m~pia~nomoc hanpethwou kata nih~b/oui~ n~te nefjij eue~i~ e~jwf.
languages.english: They shall eat the fruit of their doings. Woe to the wicked! It shall be ill with him, for the reward of his hands shall be given him.
languages.arabic: والآن يأكلون من ثمار أعمال أيديهم. الويل للمنافق. شرور أعمال يديه تؤتى عليه.

languages.coptic: Palaoc netenp~raktwr cec~rit m~mwten: n/etsi] m~mwten ceoi n~[oic e~rwten: palaoc n/etermakarizin m~mwten cecwrem m~mwten: ouoh v~mwit n~neten[a~lauj ces~yorter m~mof.
languages.english: "O My people, your punishers gather you, and those who make demands of you rule over you. O My people, those who bless you lead you astray and trouble the path of your feet."
languages.arabic: يا شعبى رؤساؤكم يقطفونكم والذين يلتمسون منكم يتسلطون عليكم. ياشعبى الذين يكرمونكم يضلونكم. ويقلقون طريق ارجلكم.

languages.coptic: Alla ]nou P[oic nai~ e~p~hap: ouoh f~nai~ nem peflaoc e~p~hap: nem nip~recbuteroc nem niar,wn. Nywten de eybe ou tetenhicah] e~pai iaha~loli: p~hwlem n~nih/ki f~,/ qen neten/i.
languages.english: The Lord stands up to plead and stands to judge His people. The Lord will enter into judgment with the elders of His people and His princes: "For you set fire to My vineyard and the plunder of the poor is in your houses.
languages.arabic: ولكن الآن يأتى الرب للحكم ويأتى مع شعبه إلى المحاكمة مع المشايخ والرؤساء وأنتم لماذا تلقون ناراً على هذا الكرم. إغتصاب الفقراء فى بيوتكم.

languages.coptic: Eybe ou n~ywten teten[i m~palaoc n~jonc: ouoh niho n~te nih/ki teten]s~vit n~wou.
languages.english: What do you mean by crushing My people and grinding the faces of the poor?"
languages.arabic: ما بالكم تظلمون شعبى وتخجلون وجوه المساكين.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen ?ca/~ac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Isaiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من إشعياء النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Nim pe v/eon/ou e~bol qen e~dwm p~y~rosres n~nefh~bwc e~bol qen Bwcor: efcaiw~ou m~pair/] qen ouc~tol/ n~jonc qen oujom: a~nok de e]nacji n~oumeym/i nem ouhap n~oujai.
languages.english: Who is this who comes from Edom with red garments from Bozrah, so beautiful in His apparel, with force of strength? "I speak of righteousness and saving judgment."
languages.arabic: من ذا الآتى من آدوم. وثيابه حمر من بُصرة؟. بهي هكذا فى حلة الإعتزاز بالقوة. أنا المتكلم بالعدل وحكم الخلاص.

languages.coptic: Eybeou nekh~bwc eyresrws: ouoh tekhebcw hwc e~bolqen ourefhwmi n~te ouh~rwt.
languages.english: Why are Your garments red and Your apparel as one who treads in a winepress?
languages.arabic: ما بال ثيابك حمراء. ولباسك كدائس المعصرة.

languages.coptic: Eaihomc m~mauat ouoh ne m~mon rwmi n~te nieynoc nem/i: ouoh aihwmi e~jwou qen pajwnt: aiywlc e~jwou m~v~r/] n~oukahi: ouoh aii~ni m~pouc~nof e~q~r/i e~p~kahi.
languages.english: "I am full of the treading of grapes, and there is no man with Me among the nations. I trampled them in My anger, and crushed them like earth, and brought down their blood to the ground.
languages.arabic: إنى دستها وحدى، ومن الشعوب لم يكن معى أحد. قد دستهم بغضبى. ووطئتهم كما تطأ الأرض وأسلت دمهم على الأرض.

languages.coptic: Oue~hoou gar n~te ou]sebiw~ afi~ e~jwou: ouoh ourompi n~cw] aci~:
languages.english: For the day of recompense has come upon them, and the year of redemption has arrived.
languages.arabic: لأن يوم النقمة قد جاء عليهم وسنة الخلاص قد أتت.

languages.coptic: Aijoust ouoh ne m~mon bo/yoc: ai] n~h~y/i ouoh ne m~mon h~li n~totou: ouoh f~nanahmet n~je paj~voi: ouoh pambon afi~:
languages.english: I looked, but there was no helper; and I observed, but there was no one to help; therefore, My arm delivered them, and My anger was suddenly present.
languages.arabic: فنظرت ولم يكن من ناصر. وتأملت ولم يكن أحد لديهم، فسيخلصنى ذراعى ورجزى قد اتى.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aihwmi e~jwou qen pajwnt ouoh aii~ni m~pouc~nof e~pec/t e~p~kahi:
languages.english: So I trampled them in My wrath and brought their blood down to the ground."
languages.arabic: وطئتهم بسخطى وأسلت دمهم على الأرض.

languages.coptic: Vnai m~P[oic aierpefmeui~: nia~ret/ n~te P[oic qen hwb niben e~tef] m~mwou nan n~sebiw.
languages.english: I remembered the Lord's mercy, the praises of the Lord in all things in which the Lord recompenses us.
languages.arabic: رحمة الرب ذكرتها وحسنات الرب فى جميع الأشياء التى يجازينا بها.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Iwb piy~m/i: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Job the righteous, may his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من أيوب الصديق بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Etaucwtem e~roi au]h~y/ou: au,arwou de e~h~r/i e~jen paco[ni:
languages.english: "Men heard and listened to me and kept silence for my counsel.
languages.arabic: لما سمعونى نصتوا، وسكتوا عند مشورتى.

languages.coptic: Oude m~poutouho e~jen nacaji: Saurasi de e~swp aisancaji nemwou m~v~r/] n~oukahi efjoust e~bol qat~h/ n~oumounhwou:
languages.english: At my word they did not speak again, and they were very glad when I spoke to them.
languages.arabic: وعلى كلامى لم يزيدوا. ويفرحون إذا كلمتهم.

languages.coptic: Pair/] nai hwou naujoust e~bol qat~h/ n~nacaji.
languages.english: They waited for my speech as the thirsty earth for the rain.
languages.arabic: كمثل أرض منتظرة للمطر هكذا هؤلاء

languages.coptic: Aisancwbi nemwou m~pautenhout: ouoh m~pare v~ouwini hei e~bolqen paho: aicwtp m~poumwit ouoh aihemci eioi n~ar,wn:
languages.english: If I laughed at them, they did not believe it, and the light of my countenance did not fail.
languages.arabic: أيضاً كانوا ينتظرون كلامى. وإذا ضحكت معهم لا يصدقونى، والنور لا يسقط من وجهى.

languages.coptic: ouoh naioueh m~v~r/] n~ououro qen hanmona zwmenoc: m~v~r/] n~hanouon euerh/bi eie~]nom] n~wou.
languages.english: I chose the way for them and sat as a ruler; and I dwelt as a king in the army, as one who comforts mourners.
languages.arabic: ثم إخترت طريقهم وجلست رئيساً وكنت أسكن كملك فى الحصون ومثل قوم نائحين أعزيهم.

languages.coptic: }nou de aucwbi m~moi n~je hana~la,ictoc: ]nou de ce]c~bw n/i qen oumeroc: n~je n/e~naiswsf n~nouio]: e~nai,w m~mwou jece m~paima an: nem niouhwr n~te nimane~cwou:
languages.english: "But now they laugh me to scorn, men younger than I, whose fathers I disdained; whom I did not deem worthy to be put with the dogs of my pastures.
languages.arabic: أما الآن فقد هزأ بى أصاغر الناس، والآن يعلموننى شيئاً أولئك الذين رذلت أباءهم. الذين كنت أتكهم مع كلاب الرعاة، لأنهم ليسوا من هذا الموضع.

languages.coptic: Ke gar eueeroun/i e~t~jom n~te noujij: toucuntelia~ actako e~h~r/i e~jwou:
languages.english: Indeed, what profit is the strength of their hands to me? For them the full term of life was lost.
languages.arabic: ماذا يفعلون بى بقوة أيديهم؟ الذين فسدت عليهم آخرتهم

languages.coptic: Qen oumetreferqae~ nemouh~bwn n~ats/ri: n/etv/t qen ouma n~aymwou n~caf. Outako nem outalepwria~.
languages.english: In want and famine, they are childless. Yesterday they fled to a waterless desert in anguish and misery.
languages.arabic: بالعوز والغلاء والعقم. الهاربون بالأمس فى موضع بلا ماء.

languages.coptic: Nn/etkw] n~ca hana~rim qenouma efcencen: n/e~te hana~rimne nouq~r/oui~: Niatimoc n~capethwou eue~erqae~ n~a~gayon niben: n/e~te sauouojouej e~jen hannouni n~te ourokh qen ouh~bwn efos:
languages.english: They are those who surround the noisy salt places, who have salty herbs for their food, and who are also considered worthless and deficient of every good thing, and chew on the roots of trees because of their great hunger.
languages.arabic: فساد وشقاء للذين يطلبون العشب من المواضع التى تطن. وجذور الرتم طعامهم. الأردياء الأشرار عادمى جميع الخيرات يمضغون جذور الحطب من شدة الغلاء.

languages.coptic: Autwounou e~h~r/i e~jwi n~je hanref[oui~:
languages.english: Thieves have risen up against me,
languages.arabic: قام علىَّ اللصوص,

languages.coptic: N/e~te han,ol m~petra ne nou/ou:
languages.english: Whose houses were holes in the rocks,
languages.arabic: الذين مساكنهم مغائر الصخور,

languages.coptic: Euws e~bolqen hanma eucencen n~kalwc.
languages.english: Who cry out among the rustling bushes and live under the wild shrubs.
languages.arabic: صارخين في مواضع حسنة الطنين.

languages.coptic: N/e~nausop e~qoun qen hanlebs n~agrion: Nis/ri n~te niatc~bw: napiran ets/s nem pisouswousi e~taf[eno hijen p~kahi:
languages.english: They are the sons of fools and dishonored men, whose name and fame were extinguished from the earth.
languages.arabic: الساكنون فى مواضع موحشة وأبناء الحمقى. أصحاب الأسم المرذول والذبيحة المنطفئة على الأرض.

languages.coptic: }nou de ]oin~kuyara nwou: ouoh a~nok etoi n~s~[/hi n~totou:
languages.english: "But now I am their lyre, And they have me as their byword.
languages.arabic: وأنا الآن لهم قيثارة وعندهم مثلا.

languages.coptic: Aumectwi ouoh auouei cabol mmoi m~pou]a~co de: auhiyaf e~qoun qen paho.
languages.english: They detest me and keep their distance. They do not hesitate to spit in my face.
languages.arabic: أبغضونى وأبتعدوا عنى ولم يشفقوا وبصقوا فى وجهي.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: Anok de ]cebtwt e~han mactiggoc: ouoh pam~kah m~pam~yo e~bol n~c/ou niben.
languages.english: For I am ready for wounds, and my pain is continually with me.
languages.arabic: أما أنا فمستعد للسياط. ووجعى مقابلى فى كل حين.

languages.coptic: Aukw] e~roi n~je ouyo n~ououhor: oucunagwg/ n~jabwn petac~amoni m~moi: all/louia.
languages.english: For many dogs surrounded me, an assembly of evildoers enclosed me. Alleluia.
languages.arabic: قد أحاط بى كلاب كثيرة، وزمرة من الأشرار أحدقت بى. الليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Matyeon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Matthew. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار متى البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Kata p~sai de ne t~kahc m~ihugemwn: e~,a ouai e~bolqen n/etconh m~pim/s v/e~sau ouasf:
languages.english: Now at the feast the governor was accustomed to releasing to the multitude one prisoner whom they wished.
languages.arabic: وكان للوالى عادة أن يطلق للجمع فى كل عيد أسيراً من أرادوه.

languages.coptic: Ne ouon ouai de efconh n~totou m~pic/ou e~tem~mau e~ouconi pe: eumou] e~rof je Barabbac:
languages.english: And at that time they had a notorious prisoner called Barabbas.
languages.arabic: وكان لهم فى ذلك الوقت أسير وهو لص يدعى باراباس.

languages.coptic: Etauywou] oun euma: peje Pilatoc nwou: je nim e~tetenouws n~ta,af nwten e~bol: Barabbac san I/couc v/e~toumou] erof je Pii,~rictoc.
languages.english: Therefore, when they had gathered together, Pilate said to them, “Whom do you want me to release to you? Barabbas, or Jesus who is called Christ?”
languages.arabic: ففيما هم مجتمعون. قال لهم بيلاطس: من تريدون أن أطلق لكم. باراباس أم يسوع الذي يقال له المسيح؟

languages.coptic: Nafe~mi gar pe je e~taut/if eybe v~yonoc:
languages.english: For he knew that they had handed Him over because of envy.
languages.arabic: لأنه كان يعلم أنهم إنما أسلموه حسداً.

languages.coptic: Efhemci de hijen pib/ma acouwrp harof n~je tefc~himi ecjwm~moc je m~pererh~li m~piy~m/i ett/: aisep oum/s gar n~qici eyb/tf m~pai e~jwrh qen y~racoui~:
languages.english: While he was sitting on the judgment seat, his wife sent to him, saying, “Have nothing to do with that just Man, for I have suffered many things today in a dream because of Him.”
languages.arabic: وإذ كان جالساً على كرسى الولاية، أرسلت له إمرأته قائلة: إياك وذاك الصديق، فانى قد توجعت الليلة كثيراً فى حلم من أجله.

languages.coptic: Niar,/e~reuc de nem nip~recbuteroc: auyeth~y/ou n~nim/s: hina n~cee~retin m~Barabbac: I/couc de n~cetakof.
languages.english: But the chief priests and elders persuaded the multitudes that they should ask for Barabbas and destroy Jesus.
languages.arabic: ولكن رؤساء الكهنة والشيوخ حرضوا الجمع على أن يطلبوا باراباس ويهلكوا يسوع.

languages.coptic: Aferouw~ n~je pihugemwn pejaf nwou: je nim e~tetenouasf n~ta,af nwten e~bol qen paic~nau: n~ywou de pejwou je Barabbac:
languages.english: The governor answered and said to them, “Which of the two do you want me to release to you?” They said, “Barabbas!”
languages.arabic: فأجاب الوالى وقال لهم: من من الإثنين تريدون أن أطلق لكم؟ أما هم فقالوا: باراباس.

languages.coptic: Peje Pilatoc nwou: je ou pe]naaif n~I/couc v/e~toumou] e~rof je Pi,~rictoc:
languages.english: Pilate said to them, “What then shall I do with Jesus who is called Christ?”
languages.arabic: فقال لهم بيلاطس: فماذا أفعل إذاً بيسوع الذي يدعى المسيح؟

languages.coptic: pejwou t/rou je marouasf: Pejaf nwou n~je pihugemwn: je ou gar m~pethwou e~tafaif: n~ywou de n~houo nauws e~bol eujw m~moc je asf.
languages.english: They all said to him, “Let Him be crucified!” Then the governor said, “Why, what evil has He done?” But they cried out all the more, saying, “Let Him be crucified!”
languages.arabic: فقال له الجمع: ليصلب. فقال لهم الوالى: وأى شر عمل؟ فأما هم فازدادوا صياحاً وقالوا: ليصلب.

languages.coptic: Etafnau de n~je Pilatoc je f~najemh/ou n~h~lian: alla mallon ous~yorter peynaswpi: af[i n~oumwou afiwi n~nefjij e~bol m~pem~yo m~pim/s efjwm~moc: je ]oi n~aynobi e~bolha p~c~nof n~te paiy~m/i: n~ywten e~retene~rwsi. m~mof: aft/if eyrouasf.
languages.english: When Pilate saw that he could not prevail at all, but rather that a tumult was rising, he took water and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, “I am innocent of the blood of this just Person. You see to it.”
languages.arabic: فلما رأى بيلاطس انه لا ينفع شيئاً بل بالحرى يحدث شغب، أخذ ماء وغسل يديه قدام الجمع قائلاً: انى برئ من دم هذا الصديق، ابصروا أنتم.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afe~rouw n~je pilaoc t/rf pejwou: je pefc~nof e~h~r/i e~jwn nem e~jen nens/ri.
languages.english: And all the people answered and said, “His blood be on us and on our children.”
languages.arabic: فأجاب كل الشعب وقالوا: دمه علينا وعلى أولادنا.

languages.coptic: Tote af,a Barabbac n~wou e~bol: I/couc de e~taferv~ragellion.
languages.english: Then he released Barabbas to them; and when he had scourged Jesus, he delivered Him to be crucified.
languages.arabic: حينئذ أطلق لهم باراباس وأما يسوع فجلده وأسلمه ليصلب.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab kata Markon a~giou.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel according to Saint Mark. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للأنجيل المقدس حسب معلمنا مار مرقس البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Markon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Mark. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار مرقس البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Kata p~sai de naf,w n~ouai efconh nwou e~bol: v/e~saue~retin m~mof.
languages.english: Now at the feast he was accustomed to releasing one prisoner to them, whomever they requested.
languages.arabic: وكان فى كل عيد يطلق لهم أسيراً واحداً من طلبوه.

languages.coptic: V/ de e~toumou] e~rof je Barabbac nafconh pe nem n/e~taui~ri n~ous~yorter: n/e~taui~ri n~ouqwteb qen pis~yorter.
languages.english: And there was one named Barabbas, who was chained with his fellow rebels; they had committed murder in the rebellion.
languages.arabic: وكان المسمى باراباس موثقاً مع أهل الفتنة الذين ارتكبوا القتل فى الفتنة.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafi~ e~p~swi n~je pim/s: aferh/tc n~eretin kata v~r/] e~nafi~ri n~wou:
languages.english: Then the multitude, crying aloud, began to ask him to do just as he had always done for them.
languages.arabic: فوثب الجمع وابتدأوا يطلبون ما كان يصنعه لهم دائماً

languages.coptic: Pilatoc de afe~rouw~ n~wou efjwm~moc: je tetenouws n~ta,w nwten e~bol m~p~ouro n~te niIoudai: Nafe~mi gar pe je e~taut/if eybe ouv~yonoc:
languages.english: But Pilate answered them, saying, “Do you want me to release to you the King of the Jews?” For he knew that the chief priests had handed Him over because of envy.
languages.arabic: فأجابهم بيلاطس قائلاً: آتٍريدون أن أطلق لكم ملك اليهود؟ لأنه كان قد علم أن رؤساء الكهنة انما أسلموه حسداً.

languages.coptic: Niar,/e~reuc de aukim m~pim/s: hina mallon n~tef,a Barabbac n~wou e~bol.
languages.english: But the chief priests stirred up the crowd, so that he should rather release Barabbas to them.
languages.arabic: فأما رؤساء الكهنة فهيجوا الجمع لكى يطلقوا لهم باراباس.

languages.coptic: Pilatoc de on e~tafe~rouw~ pejaf n~wou: je ou pe]naaif m~v/e~tetenjw m~moc e~rof je p~ouro n~niIoudai:
languages.english: Pilate answered and said to them again, “What then do you want me to do with Him whom you call the King of the Jews?”
languages.arabic: فأجاب بيلاطس أيضاً وقال لهم: فماذا أصنع بالذي تقولون عنه انه ملك اليهود؟

languages.coptic: Nywou de on auws e~bol je asf: Pilatoc gar nafjwm~moc nwou: je ou pe m~pethwou petafaif: n~ywou de n~houo nauws e~bol je asf.
languages.english: So they cried out again, “Crucify Him!” Then Pilate said to them, “Why, what evil has He done?” But they cried out all the more, “Crucify Him!”
languages.arabic: أما هم فصاحوا: أصلبه. فقال لهم بيلاطس: وأى شر عمل هذا؟ فأما هم فازدادوا جداً صراخاً: أصلبه.

languages.coptic: Pilatoc de efouws e~erpetehne pim/s: af,a Barabbac nwou e~bol: af] de n~I/couc e~erv~ragellion m~mof hina n~touasf:
languages.english: So Pilate, wanting to gratify the crowd, released Barabbas to them; and he delivered Jesus, after he had scourged Him, to be crucified.
languages.arabic: فبيلاطس إذ كان يريد أن يعمل للجمع ما يرضيهم، أطلق لهم باراباس وأسلم يسوع بعد ما جلد ليصلب.

languages.coptic: Nimatoi de au[itf e~qoun e~]aul/ n~te pip~retwrion: ouoh aumou] e~]c~pira t/rc e~h~r/i e~jwf:
languages.english: Then the soldiers led Him away into the hall called Praetorium, and they called together the whole garrison.
languages.arabic: فأخذه الجند إلى داخل دار الولاية وجمعوا عليه كل الكتيبة،

languages.coptic: Ouoh au]hiwtf n~ouh~bwc n~[/ji: ouoh auswnt n~ou,~lom e~bolqen hancouri au,af hijwf.
languages.english: And they clothed Him with purple; and they twisted a crown of thorns, put it on His head,
languages.arabic: وألبسوه ثوب ارجوان وضفروا إكليلاً من شوك ووضعوه عليه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh auerh/tc n~eracpazecye m~mof: je ,ere p~ouro n~niIoudai:
languages.english: And began to salute Him, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
languages.arabic: وابتدأوا يسلمون عليه قائلين: السلام يا ملك اليهود.

languages.coptic: Ouoh auhioui qen tefa~ve n~oukas: ouoh nauhiyaf qen pefho: ouoh euhioui~ m~mwou e~jen noukeli euouwst m~mof:
languages.english: Then they struck Him on the head with a reed and spat on Him; and bowing the knee, they worshiped Him.
languages.arabic: وكانوا يضربون رأسه بقصبة. ويتفلون فى وجهه. ويجثون على ركبهم ساجدين له.

languages.coptic: Ouoh hote e~taucwbi m~mof: aubasf mpih~bwc n~[/ji: ouoh au] n~nefh~bwc hiwtf: ouoh auenf e~bol hina n~ceasf.
languages.english: And when they had mocked Him, they took the purple off Him, put His own clothes on Him, and led Him out to crucify Him.
languages.arabic: وبعد ما هزأوا به. نزعوا عنه الثوب الارجواني وألبسوه ثيابه ثم أخرجوه ليصلبوه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh au[i n~ouai n~,~ba efciniw~ou: Cimwn pikurinneoc efn/ou e~bolqen t~koi: v~iwt n~Alexandroc nem Rovoc hina n~tefwli mpef/:
languages.english: Then they compelled a certain man, Simon a Cyrenian, the father of Alexander and Rufus, as he was coming out of the country and passing by, to bear His cross.
languages.arabic: وسخروا واحد عابراً آتٍياً من الحقل وهو سمعان القيروانى أبو الكسندروس وروفس ليحمل صليبه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh auenf epima n~te Golgoya: vai e~sauouahmef je pima n~te Pik~ranion.
languages.english: And they brought Him to the place Golgotha, which is translated, Place of a Skull.
languages.arabic: وجاءوا به إلى موضع الجلجثة الذي تأويله الجمجمة.

languages.coptic: Ouoh au] naf n~ou/rp efmoujt nem ouensasi: n~yof de m~pef[itf.
languages.english: Then they gave Him wine mingled with myrrh to drink, but He did not take it.
languages.arabic: وأعطوه خمراً ممزوجة بمر. أما هو فلم يقبله.

languages.coptic: Ouoh auasf ouoh auvws n~nefh~bwc e~hrau: ouoh auhiwp e~rwou je nim eynao~lou:
languages.english: And when they crucified Him, they divided His garments, casting lots for them to determine what every man should take.
languages.arabic: ولما صلبوه إقتسموا ثيابه بينهم مقترعين عليها ماذا يأخذ كل واحد منهما.

languages.coptic: Ne v~nau de n~ajp somt pe ouoh auasf.
languages.english: Now it was the third hour, and they crucified Him.
languages.arabic: وكان وقت الساعة الثالثة وصلبوه.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab kata Loukan a~giou.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel according to Saint Luke. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للأنجيل المقدس حسب معلمنا مار لوقا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Loukan a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Luke. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار لوقا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Pilatoc de e~tafmou] Eniar,/e~reuc nem niar,wn nem pilaoc:
languages.english: Then Pilate, when he had called together the chief priests, the rulers, and the people,
languages.arabic: فدعا بيلاطس رؤساء الكهنة والعظماء والشعب

languages.coptic: Pejaf nwou je a~reteni~ni n/i m~pai rwmi hwc efvwnh m~pilaoc e~bol: ouoh h/ppe a~nok e~taisenf e~h~raf m~petenm~yo: m~pijem h~li n~e~tia~ qen pai rwmi: qen n/e~tenerkat/gorin erof n~q/tou.
languages.english: Said to them, “You have brought this Man to me, as one who misleads the people. And indeed, having examined Him in your presence, I have found no fault in this Man concerning those things of which you accuse Him;
languages.arabic: وقال لهم: قد قدمتم إلىّ هذا الرجل كمن يضل الشعب وها أنا قد فحصته أمامكم ولم أجد فيه علة مما تشتكون به عليه.

languages.coptic: Alla oude p~ke/~rwd/c afouorpf gar haron: ouoh h/ppe m~mon h~li n~hwb n~q/tf e~aif efemp~sa m~v~mou.
languages.english: No, neither did Herod, for I sent you back to him; and indeed nothing deserving of death has been done by Him.
languages.arabic: بل ولا هيرودس أيضاً لأنه قد أرسله إلينا. وها هوذا لم يصنع شيئاً يستحق عليه الموت

languages.coptic: Nta]c~bw oun naf n~ta,af e~bol.
languages.english: I will therefore chastise Him and release Him”
languages.arabic: فأنا أؤدبه وأطلقه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh n~yof a~nagk/ naf pe e~,a ouai nwou e~bol kata p~sai.
languages.english: (for it was necessary for him to release one to them at the feast).
languages.arabic: وكان لا بد له أن يطلق لهم فى كل عيد أسيراً.

languages.coptic: A pim/s de t/rf ws e~bol efjwm~moc: je a~li vai ,a Barabbac nan e~bol:
languages.english: And they all cried out at once, saying, “Away with this Man, and release to us Barabbas”—
languages.arabic: فصرخوا بجملتهم قائلين: أرفع هذا وأطلق لنا باراباس.

languages.coptic: Vai e~tauhitf e~pis~teko eybe ous~yorter nem ouqwteb e~afswpi qen ]polic:
languages.english: Who had been thrown into prison for a certain rebellion made in the city, and for murder.
languages.arabic: وذاك كان قد ألقى فى السجن لأجل فتنة حدثت فى المدينة وقتل.

languages.coptic: Palin de on a~Pilatoc mou] e~rwou efouws e~,a I/couc e~bol.
languages.english: Pilate, therefore, wishing to release Jesus, again called out to them.
languages.arabic: فناداهم أيضاً بيلاطس وهو يريد أن يطلق يسوع،

languages.coptic: Nywou de auws e~bol eujw m~moc je asf asf.
languages.english: But they shouted, saying, “Crucify Him, crucify Him!”
languages.arabic: فأما هم فصرخوا قائلين: أصلبه أصلبه.

languages.coptic: Nyof de pejaf nwou m~v~mah somt n~cop: je ou gar m~pethwou peeta~ vai aif: m~pijem h~li n~e~tia~ n~te v~mou n~q/tf: n~ta]c~bw oun naf n~ta,af e~bol.
languages.english: Then he said to them the third time, “Why, what evil has He done? I have found no reason for death in Him. I will therefore chastise Him and let Him go.”
languages.arabic: فقال لهم مرة ثالثة: وأى شر عمل هذا؟ انى لم أجد فيه علة للموت. فأنا أؤدبه وأطلقه.

languages.coptic: Nywou de e~tauouahtotou qen ounis] n~c~m/ eue~retin m~mof eyrouasf:
languages.english: But they were insistent, demanding with loud voices that He be crucified.
languages.arabic: وأما هم فألحوا عليه بأصوآتٍ عالية أن يصلبه.

languages.coptic: ouoh naujemjom n~je nouc~ m/:
languages.english: And the voices of these men and of the chief priests prevailed.
languages.arabic: واشتدت أصواتٍهم.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a Pilatoc ]hap eyrefswpi n~je poue~t/ma:
languages.english: So Pilate gave sentence that it should be as they requested.
languages.arabic: فحكم بيلاطس ان يجرى مطلبهم.

languages.coptic: Af,w de e~bol m~v/e~tauhitf e~pis~teko eybe ouqwteb nem ous~yorter v/e~tau e~retin m~mof: I/couc de aut/if kata pououws.
languages.english: And he released to them the one they requested, who for rebellion and murder had been thrown into prison; but he delivered Jesus to their will.
languages.arabic: فأطلق لهم الذي طلبوه، ذاك الذي ألقى فى السجن لأجل فتنة وقتل، وأسلم يسوع لارادتهم.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab kata Iwann/n a~giou.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel according to Saint John. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للأنجيل المقدس حسب معلمنا مار يوحنا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Iwann/n a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint John. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار يوحنا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Tote Pilatoc af[i n~I/couc afermactiggoin m~mof.
languages.english: So then Pilate took Jesus and scourged Him.
languages.arabic: حينئذ أخذ بيلاطس يسوع وجلده.

languages.coptic: Ouoh nimatoi auswnt n~ou,~lom e~bolqen hancouri: ouoh aut/if e~jen tefa~ve: ouoh aujolhf n~ouhebcw n~[/ji:
languages.english: And the soldiers twisted a crown of thorns and put it on His head, and they put on Him a purple robe.
languages.arabic: وضفر العسكر إكليلاً من شوك ووضعوه على رأسه. وألبسوه ثوباً من ارجوان.

languages.coptic: Ouoh naun/ou harof pe eujw m~moc: je ,ere p~ouro n~te niIoudai ouoh nau]kour naf pe:
languages.english: Then they said, “Hail, King of the Jews!” And they struck Him with their hands.
languages.arabic: وكانوا يقبلون إليه ويقولون: السلام يا ملك اليهود. وكانوا يلطمونه.

languages.coptic: Palin on afi~ e~bol n~je Pilatoc ouoh pejaf nwou: je h/ppe ]naenf nwten e~bol: hina n~tetene~mi je n~]jemh~li n~e~tia~ [ie~rof an:
languages.english: Pilate then went out again, and said to them, “Behold, I am bringing Him out to you, that you may know that I find no fault in Him.”
languages.arabic: فخرج بيلاطس أيضاً وقال لهم: ها أنا أخرجه إليكم لتعلموا إنى لست أجد فيه علة واحدة.

languages.coptic: Tote afie~bol n~je I/couc efervorin m~pi,~lom n~couri nem pih~bwc n~[/ji:
languages.english: Then Jesus came out, wearing the crown of thorns and the purple robe.
languages.arabic: حينئذ خرج يسوع وهو حامل إكليل الشوك وثوب الارجوان.

languages.coptic: ouoh peje Pilatoc nwou je h/ppe ic pirwmi:
languages.english: And Pilate said to them, “Behold the Man!”
languages.arabic: فقال لهم بيلاطس: هوذا الرجل.

languages.coptic: <ote oun e~taunau e~rof n~je niar,/e~reuc nem nihuperet/c: auws e~bol eujw m~moc je asf asf: peje Pilatoc n~wou: je mwini e~rof n~ywten asf a~nok gar n~]jemh~li n~e~tia~[ie~rof an.
languages.english: Therefore, when the chief priests and officers saw Him, they cried out, saying, “Crucify Him, crucify Him!” Pilate said to them, “You take Him and crucify Him, for I find no fault in Him.”
languages.arabic: فلما رأه رؤساء الكهنة والخدام صرخوا قائلين: إصلبه إصلبه. فقال لهم بيلاطس خذوه أنتم وإصلبوه، لانى لست أجد فيه علة.

languages.coptic: Aue~rouw naf n~je niIoudai je a~non ouon n~tann~ounomoc: ouoh kata pen nomoc f~emp~sa m~v~mou: je afaif n~s/ri m~V]:
languages.english: The Jews answered him, “We have a law, and according to our law He ought to die, because He made Himself the Son of God.”
languages.arabic: أجابه اليهود إن لنا ناموساً وبحسب ناموسنا هو مستوجب الموت لانه جعل نفسه إبن الله.

languages.coptic: <ote de e~tafcwtem epai caji n~je Pilatoc aferho] n~houo:
languages.english: Therefore, when Pilate heard that saying, he was the more afraid,
languages.arabic: فلما سمع بيلاطس هذا الكلام ازداد خوفاً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afi~ on e~qoun e~pip~retwrion ouoh pejaf n~I/couc je n~yok ou e~bolywn: I/couc de m~pefe~rouw naf.
languages.english: and went again into the Praetorium, and said to Jesus, “Where are You from?” But Jesus gave him no answer.
languages.arabic: ودخل أيضاً إلى الايوان وقال ليسوع: من أين أنت؟ أما يسوع فلم يعطه جواباً.

languages.coptic: Peje Pilatoc naf: je eybe ou kcaji nem/i an: san ke~mi an je ouon] ersisi m~mau e~ask: ouoh ouon] ersisi m~mau on e~,ak e~bol:
languages.english: Then Pilate said to Him, “Are You not speaking to me? Do You not know that I have power to crucify You, and power to release You?”
languages.arabic: فقال له بيلاطس: لماذا لم تكلمنى؟ أما تعلم ان لى سلطاناً أن أصلبك ولى سلطاناً أيضاً أن أطلقك.

languages.coptic: Afe~rouw n~je I/couc je m~mon je m~montek h~li n~ersisi m~mau e~qoun e~roi: e~ne mpout/ic nak e~bol m~p~swi: eybe vai v/e~taft/it nak ouon tefnis] n~nobi m~mau n~houo:
languages.english: Jesus answered, “You could have no power at all against Me unless it had been given you from above. Therefore the one who delivered Me to you has the greater sin.”
languages.arabic: أجاب يسوع قائلاً: لم يكن لك علىّ سلطان لو لم تكن قد أعطيت من فوق، من أجل هذا خطية الذي أسلمنى إليك هى أعظم.

languages.coptic: Eybe vai oun Pilatoc nafkw] pe e~,af e~bol: niIoudai de nauws e~bol eujw m~moc: e~swp aksan,a vai e~bol: n~yok p~sv/rm~p~ouroan: ouon gar niben eti~ri mmof n~ouro m~mauatf: af]e qoun e~h~ren p~ouro.
languages.english: From then on Pilate sought to release Him, but the Jews cried out, saying, “If you let this Man go, you are not Caesar’s friend. Whoever makes himself a king speaks against Caesar.”
languages.arabic: ومنذ ذلك كان بيلاطس يطلب أن يطلقه. ولكن اليهود كانوا يصرخون قائلين: ان أنت أطلقته فلست بصاحب لقيصر، لان كل من يجعل نفسه ملكاً فهو يقاوم قيصر.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: We were not born naked but clothed in righteousness but when we disobeyed the commandments of God we became naked and were thrown out of heaven, the place of rest and joy, and onto earth, pain, and suffering after God clothed our nakedness with garments made of skin. We started to work the ground, which had been cursed because of us and brought forth thorns and thistles as God had commanded. Years and generations had passed and we were still naked of righteousness until the Son of God, the second hypostasis of the Divine, condescended and was incarnate from the ever virgin Mary, the morning star, and lived amongst us, healing our sicknesses, raising our dead and giving sight to the blind. Envy filled the hearts of the Jewish priests and they hated Him. The devil tempted them to kill Him so they sentenced Him to death despite the fact that they could not execute this judgment because the Romans who ruled over them had denied the Jewish judges the right to kill any man. So they took Him to Pilate then to Herod then to Pilate again.
languages.arabic: إننا لمَّا خُلقنا لمْ نُخلق عُراة كما نحن اليوم ولكننا كنا مُكتسين بِردَاء البر ولما خالفنا وصية الله تَعرينا من هذا الرداء وخرجنا من الفردوس محل الراحة والنعيم إلى أرض التعب والشقاء بعد ما سَتر الله عريِنا برداء من الجلد. وصِرنا نِعمل في الأرض التي لُعنت من أجلنا وهي تنبت الشوك والحسك كأمر الله تعالى. ولقد مَرت السنون والأجيال ونحن على هذه الحال عراة من حلة البر حتى تنازل ابن الله، الأقنوم الثاني من اللاهوت، وتجسد من السيدة البتول نجمة الصبح العذراء مريم وعاش بيننا يَشفي أمراضنا ويُقيم مَوتانا ويُفتح أعين عمياننا. فملأ الحسد قلوب كهنة اليهود بُغضةٌ له وأغراهم الشيطان على قَتلهِ فَحكَموا عليه بالموت بيد أن تنفيذ هذا الحكم لم يكن في يدهم حيث قد نَزعه منهم الرومان المُتمَّلِكين عليهم في ذلك الزمان. فأخذوه وذهبوا به إلى بيلاطس فهيرودس فبيلاطس الثاني.

languages.english: The Lord was taken to be sentenced before judges and governors six times: three times before the Jewish judges, twice before Pilate and once before Herod. He was humiliated four times. He was declared innocent three times and was sentenced twice. When He was finally brought back to Pilate for the second time, Pilate tried to release Him several times because he was confident of His innocence but his efforts did not yield any results when he saw that the place was full of people and the mob was shouting for him to crucify Jesus. He therefore tried to come up with a solution that would pacify their anger and quench the envy in their heart. "You have brought this Man to me, as one who misleads the people. And indeed, having examined Him in your presence, I have found no fault in this Man concerning those things of which you accuse Him; no, neither did Herod, for I sent you back to him… I will therefore chastise Him and release Him… But they shouted, saying, "Crucify Him, crucify Him”
languages.arabic: فقد سِيق الرب إلى الحكم أمام القضاة والولاة ست مرات. ثلاثاَ قدام قضاة من اليهود واثنتين أمام بيلاطس وواحدة أمام هيرودس واستُهزئَ به أربع مرات: وبُررَّ ثلاث مرات وحُكمَ عليه مرتين. ولما جاءوا إلى بيلاطس للمرة الثانية حاول كثيراً أن يُطلِقه وبَذل قصارى جهده في إخلاء سبيله لما تحققه من برﺍءته ولكنه وجد مساعيه ذاهبة سُدى حيث رأى دار الولاية غاصة بالمزدحمين من الشعب والرعاع وقد بلغ صياحهم عنان السماء وهم يصرخون قائلين: اصلبه، دَبر طريقة لعلها تطفئ نار غيظهم وتستأصل جذوة الحسد من قلوبهم. "فدعا بيلاطس رؤساء الكهنة والعظماء والشعب وقال لهم قدمتم إلىَّ هذا الإنسان كمَن يُفسد الشعب. وها أنا قد فَحصت قدامكم ولم أجد في هذا الإنسان علة مما تشتكون عليه ولا هيرودس أيضاً... فأنا أؤدبه وأُطلَقه... فكانوا يلجون بأصوات عظيمة طالبين أن يُصلب".

languages.english: When Pilate saw that chastising Him was insufficient for them and that they would not stop shouting to have Him crucified he said, “…”(for it was necessary for him to release one to them at the feast. The Israelites had been enslaved by Pharaoh and were delivered at the same time of year that they celebrate the Passover. Therefore, when Pilate became their governor they agreed that he would release one captive each year at the time of Passover to show them mercy.) As for Pilate’s wife’s dream, interpreters have disagreed on its meaning. A distinguished group of commentators agreed that she saw the Lord sitting on a throne and rising to heaven with great glory surrounded by the creatures of both the earth and the heavens and when she inquired about the scene, a fearful loud voice from Heaven replied, “This is Jesus of Nazareth who was condemned by Pilate.” She suffered for him showing that she was scared by the enormity of what she saw.
languages.arabic: ولما رأى بيلاطس أن تأديبه وإطلاقه لا يرضيهم وأنهم لم ينفكوا من الصراخ طالبين قتله. "وكان الوالي معتاداً في العيد أن يُطلق للجمع أسيراً واحداً من أرادوه" وذلك لأن بني إسرائيل لما كانوا في عبودية فرعون وكان خلاصهم من تلك العبودية في مثل هذا الوقت الذي يُعيِّدون فيه الفصح. ولمَّا تولى عليهم بيلاطس جعلوا لهم عليه هذا الرسم بقرار كان بينهم وبينه بأن يستطلقوا منه في كل سنة تأتي عليهم في عيد الفصح أسيراً واحداً من أرادوه تظاهراً بالرحمة. وأما حلم امرأة بيلاطس فإن المفسرين قد اختلفوا فيه. والذي اتفق عليه جماعة منهم مُميزين هو أنها رأت السيد وهو جالس على كرسي عظيم القدر وهو صاعد إلى السماء بمَجد عظيم. والسمائيون مع الأرضيين حوله. فَسألت ما هذا وإذا صوتٌ مُخيف مُزعج يُنادي من السماء. هذا هو يسوع الناصري الذي قد دانَه بيلاطس. أما تألمها من أجله فيدل على أنها ارتعبت من هول ما قد رأت.

languages.english: Pilate tried hard to release Him but to no avail because the Chief priests and the teachers had provoked the multitudes to ask for Barabbas and to crucify Jesus. Because their hearts were full of envy they could no longer see the light of truth so they sentenced Jesus to death and released Barabbas, who was a sinful criminal.
languages.arabic: وعبثاً حاول بيلاطس إطلاق يسوع لأن "رؤساء الكهنة والشيوخ حرَّضوا الجموع على أن يطلبوا باراباس ويُهلكوا يسوع" لأن الحسد قد ملأ منهم القلوب وأعمى بصائرهم عن نور الحق فحكموا بالموت على يسوع وبالإفراج عن باراباس المجرم الأثيم.

languages.english: "When Pilate saw that he could prevail nothing, but that rather a tumult was made, he took water, and washed his hands before the multitude, saying, I am innocent of the blood of this just person” because he found no reason to condemn him to death, because of his wife’s dream, and because of the miracles he heard that Jesus had performed. He also knew that the Jews had delivered Jesus out of envy. “Then answered all the people, and said, His blood be on us, and on our children.” It was not sufficient for them to destroy themselves but they also sought to destroy their children. From that point of time onwards, God had become angry with them. They were no longer permitted to be priests or offer offerings or incense for forgiveness.
languages.arabic: "فلما رأى بيلاطس أنه لا ينفع شيئاً بل بالحري يُحدث شغب أخذ ماء وغسل يديه قدام الجمع قائلاً أني برئ من دم هذا البار" لأنه لم يجد فيه علة يَستحق عليها الموت ولأنه يَعلم أن اليهود قد أسلموه إليه حسداً ولِعلمهِ بالآيات التي كان يصنعها ولأجل الحلم الذي رأته امرأته "فأجاب جميع الشعب وقالوا دمه علينا وعلى أولادنا" فلم يقنعوا بهلاك أنفسهم بل أهلكوا معهم أولادهم. ومن ذلك الوقت إلى الآن وغضب الله عليهِم حيث لا كهنوت لهم ولا قربان ولا بخور ولا غفران.

languages.english: “Then Pilate therefore took Jesus, and scourged Him.” It was the Roman custom to scourge those sentenced to crucifixion before they were crucified. “And the soldiers platted a crown of thorns, and put it on His head, and they put on Him a purple robe And said, Hail, King of the Jews! And they smote Him with their hands.” By being scourged the Lord paid for the insults and the scourging that Adam and his offspring deserved. The soldiers humiliated Him after they had undressed Him. He was dressed in a purple robe instead of the leather robes given to Adam and Eve when they had fallen. A crown of thorns was put on his head instead of the thorns and thistles that He had ordered the earth to grow when Adam fell. He carried the thorns on His head to ensure that He carried our sins, for sins are thorns. Pilate’s intention of showing them Jesus like this was to persuade them not to kill Him but they shouted even more, “But they cried out, "Away with Him, away with Him! Crucify Him!" Pilate said to them, "Shall I crucify your King?" The chief priests answered, "We have no king but Caesar!" Observe those two-faced evil people and how, despite their extreme hatred of the foreign rule, they now acknowledged Caesar as their King. This was simply a means to achieve their evil end.
languages.arabic: "فحينئذ أخذ بيلاطس يسوع وجلده" وهذه كانت عادة الرومانيين جلْد المحكوم عليه بالصَلبِ قبل أن يُصلب. "وضفر العسكر إكليلاً من شوك ووضعوه على رأسه وألبسوه ثوب أرجوان. وكانوا يقولون السلام يا مَلك اليهود وكانوا يلطمونه" وقد أوفى الرب بجلده الإهانة والجلد عن آدم ونسله الذين كانوا يستحقونهما. وهزأ به الجند بعد أن عَروه ثيابه. فأوفى عن آدم الهزء الذي كان يستحقه من الله. وأوفى العُري الواجب عليه إلى الأبد لمَّا عَروه. ولبِس الثوب الأحمر بدل الثياب الجلد التي البسها لآدم وحواء عند مُخالفتهما. ووُضع على رأسه أكليل شوك بدل الشوك والحسك الذي أمَر الأرض أن تنبت لمَّا اخطأ آدم. حَمل الشوك على رأسه حتى يُحقق أنه أحتمل خطاياه. لأن الشوك هو الخطايا. وكان غَرض بيلاطس من عَرض يسوع على اليهود بهذه الحال ليُثنى عزمهم عن قتله ولكنهم كانوا يزدادون صياحاً "أصلبه أصلبه" فقال لهم: "أأصلب ملككم. أجاب رؤساء الكهنة ليس لنا ملك إلا قيصر" فتأمل يا هذا نفاق هذا الشعب الملتوي القلب وكيف أنه مع نفوره من الحكم الأجنبي أشد النفور يُرائي الآن ويَعترف بالقيصر ملكاً عليه وما جعلوا ذلك القول إلا واسطة لنيل أغراضهم السيئة.

languages.english: “And he released unto them him that for sedition and murder was cast into prison, whom they had desired; but he delivered Jesus to their will.” And so Pilate… delivered Jesus, when he had scourged Him, to be crucified… And it was the third hour, and they crucified Him.”
languages.arabic: "فأطلق لهم الذي طرح في السجن لأجل فتنة وقتل الذي طلبوه" "وأسلم يسوع بعد ما جلده ليُصلب... وكانت الساعة الثالثة فصَلبوه"

languages.english: There is no disagreement between Mark’s statement that Jesus was crucified on the third hour and John’s statement that He was crucified at sixth, because the former talked about the time when He was delivered to be crucified and the second talked about the time when He was actually crucified. Glory be given to our Lord Forever Amen.
languages.arabic: وقول مرقس هذا أن صلب يسوع كان في الساعة الثالثة لا خلاف بينه وبين قول يوحنا أنه كان في السادسة. لأن الأول قال عن الوقت الذي أُسلم فيه ليُصلب والثاني قال عن الوقت الذي تم فيه الصلب. والمجد لربنا دائماً أبدياً آمين.

languages.english: The exposition of the Third Hour of Great Friday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة الثالثة من يوم الجمعة العظيمة من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: I am baffled with this great prophet Isaiah. He has seen by inspiration the passions of the Savior who is the Word of God.
languages.arabic: أنا متحير مع هذا النبى ذو الصوت العظيم أشعياء النبى. الذي سبق فنظر بالسر لآلام المخلص التى لله الكلمة

languages.english: He said: "Who is He who comes from Adam, his clothes are red, and He is dressed in a magnificent robe and His mantle is from red blood as one who comes up from the press, and his clothes are stained by the blood of its vines."
languages.arabic: فقال: من هو هذا الآتى من آدوم وثيابه حمراء من بصرة؟ لابساً حلة بهية هكذا ولباسه من الدم الأحمر كمن يصعد من المعصرة ملطخ بدم عنقودها.

languages.english: Truly these are the words of this prophet who revealed this before these days. Truly, He is the Word of God, our Savior, Jesus.
languages.arabic: حقاً بالحقيقة هو كلام هذا النبى الذي أظهر هذا قبل هذه الأيام. بالحقيقة هو كلمة الإله مخلصنا يسوع المسيح.

languages.english: He wore the old flesh of Adam, the first creation. The sublime Divinity united with humanity.
languages.arabic: كالتدبير لبس الجسد القديم الذي لأبينا آدم أول الخلقة وصارت اللاهوتية العلوية متحدة بالبشرية

languages.english: An immutable mantle united with God the Word. He inflicted His wrath on the Jews and trampled over them in the press of His anger.
languages.arabic: بغير إستحالة بما لا يدرك. هى الحلة التى لأتتغير المتحدة مع الإله الكلمة. وصب غضبه على العبرانيين وداسهم فى معصرة غضبه.

languages.english: He granted His mercy and grace to the Gentiles whom He made a new people. As for Israel, his foolishness will prevail on him forever.
languages.arabic: ورحمته وبره أعطاهما للأمم الذين صنعهم له شعباً جديداً. فأما اسرائيل فإن البلادة إستولت عليه إلى الأنقضاء.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Pjwm n~te pia~riymoc n~te Mwu~c/c pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from the Book of Numbers of Moses the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من سفر العدد لموسى النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafcwtem n~je pi<ananeoc p~ouro n~Darad v/etsop hi p~safe: je afi~ n~je pIicra/l e~v~mwit n~Ayarim: ouoh afbwtc e~p~iIcra/l ouoh auwli n~oue,malwcia~ n~q/tou:
languages.english: And Arad the Canaanite king who dwelt by the wilderness, heard that Israel came by the way of Atharim; and he made war against Israel, and carried off some of them as captives.
languages.arabic: وسمع الكنعانى ملك داراد المقيم فى البرية أن اسرائيل قد جاء عن طريق اتاريم. فحارب اسرائيل و سبى منهم سبياً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aftwbh n~je p~iIcra/l n~oueu,/ m~P[oic ouoh pejaf: je eswp aksan] m~pailaoc e~q~r/i e~najij: ]naera~nay/mazin m~mof nem nefbaki:
languages.english: Then Israel made a vow to the Lord, and said, "If You will indeed deliver this people into my hand, I will anathematize him and his cities."
languages.arabic: فنذر اسرائيل نذراً للرب وقال: أن دفعت هذا الشعب إلي أيدينا حرمناه لك مع مدنه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afcwtem n~je P[oic e~t~c~m/ m~pIicra/l: ouoh af] m~pilaoc n~<ananeoc e~q~r/i e~totf: ouoh afera~nay/matizin m~mof nem nefbaki: ouoh au]ren v~ran m~pima etemmje pia~nay/ma:
languages.english: So the Lord listened to the voice of Israel and delivered up the Canaanites; and they anathematized him and his cities. Therefore, they called the name of that place Anathema.
languages.arabic: فسمع الرب صوت اسرائيل ودفع إليهم الكنعانيين. فحرموهم هم ومدنهم. فدعى اسم ذلك الموضع حرماً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tauouwteb e~bolqen Wrpitwou e~jen v~mwit mv~iom n~sari: aukwt e~p~kahi n~Edwm: ouoh aferkouji n~h/t n~je pilaoc hi v~mwit:
languages.english: Then they departed from Mount Hor by the Way of the Red Sea; and they went around the land of Edom, and the people became discouraged on the way.
languages.arabic: ثم إرتحلوا من جبل حور على طريق البحر الأحمر وداروا من حول أرض آدوم. فصغر قلب الشعب فى الطريق

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~pilaoc caji n~ca Vnou] nem Mwu~c/c: je eybe ou akenten e~bolqen p~kahi n~</mi e~qoyben hi p~wafe: je m~mon wik oude m~wou: ten'u,/ ach~ros e~piwik etsouw~ou:
languages.english: So the people spoke against God and against Moses, "Why did you bring us up out of Egypt to kill us in the desert? For there is no bread nor water, and our soul is weary of this worthless bread."
languages.arabic: وتكلم الشعب على الله وعلى موسى قائلين: لماذا أصعدتنا من أرض مصر لنموت فى البرية لأنه لا خبز ولا ماء؟ وقد سئمت نفوسنا هذا الخبز اليابس.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~P[oic ouwrp n~nihof e~qoun e~pilaoc e~sauqwteb: ouoh nau[iila.in n~ca pilaoc: ouoh afmou n~je ounis] m~m/s n~te nens/ri m~p~iIcra/l.
languages.english: So the Lord sent venomous serpents among the people, and they bit the people; and many of the children of Israel died.
languages.arabic: فأرسل الرب على الشعب حيات قاتلة. فكانت تلدغ الشعب. ومات قوم كثيرون من بنى اسرائيل.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~pilaoc i~ ha Mwu~c/c eujw m~moc: je anernobi ancaji n~ca Vnou] nem n~cwk: twbh oun m~P[oic marefwli n~naihof e~bolharon: ouoh a~Mwu~c/c twbh m~P[oic eybe pilaoc:
languages.english: Then the people came to Moses, and were saying, "We sinned, for we spoke against the Lord and against you; therefore, pray to the Lord, and let Him take away the serpent from us."
languages.arabic: فأتى الشعب إلي موسى وقالوا: قد أخطأنا إذ تكلمنا على الرب وعليك. فأدع الرب أن يبعد عنا هذه الحيات.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje P[oic m~Mwu~c/c: je mayamio nak n~ouhof n~homt: ouoh ,af hi oum/ini: ouoh ece~swpi a~resan pihof [ila.in e~ourwmi: efe~joust e~pihof n~homt ouoh safwn:
languages.english: So Moses prayed for the people. Then the Lord said to Moses, "Make a serpent for yourself and put it on a signal pole; and it shall be, if a serpent should bite someone, when the one bitten looks at it, he shall live."
languages.arabic: فتضرع موسى لأجل الشعب. فقال الرب لموسى: إصنع لك حية من نحاس، وإرفعها على سارية حتى إذا لدغت الحية إنساناً فينظر إلى الحية النحاس فيحيا.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afyamio n~je Mwu~c/c m~pihof n~homt ouoh aftahof hi oum/ini: ouoh acswpi hotan a~resan pihof [ila.in e~ourwmi: ouoh n~tefjoust e~pihof n~homt safwnq:
languages.english: So Moses made a copper serpent and put it on a signal pole; and it happened, when a serpent bit anyone, and he looked at the copper serpent, he lived.
languages.arabic: فصنع موسى حية من نحاس ورفعها على راية. فكان أى إنسان لدغته حية ونظر إلي الحية النحاسية يحيا.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen ?ca/~ac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Isaiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من إشعياء النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Mv~r/] n~oue~cwou e~auenf e~p~qolqel nem m~v~r/] n~ouhi/b m~pem~yo m~v/etqwk m~mof efoi n~atc~m/: pair/] n~fouwn n~rwf an:
languages.english: He was led as a sheep to the slaughter, and as a lamb is silent before his shearers, so He opens not His mouth.
languages.arabic: مثل خروف سيق إلى الذبح وكحمل صامت أمام الذي يجزه. هكذا لم يفتح فاه.

languages.coptic: Au w~li m~pefhap qen de nim eynas~vir e~roc: je cenaw~li m~pefwnq e~bolhijen p~kahi: eybe nia~nomia~ n~te pilaoc afi~ e~v~mou:
languages.english: In His humiliation His judgment was taken away, and who will declare His generation? For His life is taken from the earth, and because of the lawlessness of My people He was led to death.
languages.arabic: فرفع حكمه فى تواضعه. وجيله من يقدر أن يصفه. ستنزع حياته من الأرض. ولأجل آثام الشعب جاء إلى الموت.

languages.coptic: Ouoh eie~] n~nia~nomoc n~t~sebiw~ n~tefkaici nem niramaoi n~t~sebiw~ m~pefmou:je mpefi~ri n~ouanomia~: oude m~poujem ou,~rof qenrwf.
languages.english: I will appoint evil men for His burial and rich men for His death, because He committed no lawlessness, nor was deceit found in His mouth.
languages.arabic: وسأعطى المتجاوزى الناموس مجازاة دفنه. والأغنياء مكافأة موته. لأنه لم يصنع اثماً، ولم يوجد فى فمه غش.

languages.coptic: P[oic ouws e~toujof e~bolqen pierqot: e~swp a~reten san] e~jen v~nobi teten'u,/ nanau e~oujroj e~nase pefa~mahi:
languages.english: The Lord wishes to cleanse Him of His wound, and if You give an offering for sin, Your soul shall see a long-lived seed.
languages.arabic: أما الرب فشاء أن يشفيه من الجراح وإذا ما أسلمتم ذواتكم ذبيحة عن الخطية فسترى نفوسكم زرعاً أيامه كثيرة.

languages.coptic: P[oic ouws e~w~li e~bolqen pim~kah n~te tef'u,/ e~tamof e~ouwini: e~erp~lazin n~ouka]: e~yamio n~oumeym/i: efoi m~bwk n~oum/s n~kalwc: ouoh nounobi n~yof afe~nou e~p~swi:
languages.english: The Lord wishes to take away the pain of His soul, to show Him light, to form Him with understanding, and to pronounce righteous the Righteous One who serves many well; and He shall bear their sins.
languages.arabic: وشاء الرب أن ينزع الآلالم عن نفسه ويريه النور ويوجِد الفهم. ويصنع البر ويكون عبداً حسناً للجماعة. إذ يحمل خطاياهم

languages.coptic: Eybe vai n~yof efe~erk~l/ronomin n~oum/s: ouoh efe~vws n~niswl n~te nijwri: e~v~ma je af] n~tef'u,/ e~v~mou:
languages.english: Therefore He shall inherit many, and will divide the spoil with the strong, because His soul was delivered over to death.
languages.arabic: ومن أجل هذا هو يرث الكثيرين ويقسم غنائم الأقوياء حيث أسلم نفسه للموت

languages.coptic: Ouoh auopf nem nia~nomoc n~yof afen ninobi n~te oum/s e~p~swi: ouoh eybe oua~nomia~ aut/if:
languages.english: He was considered among the lawless, and He bore the sins of many, and was delivered over because of their sins.
languages.arabic: وأحصى مع الأثمة وهو قد حمل خطايا كثيرين وأسلم من أجل ذنوبهم.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen ?ca/~ac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Isaiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من إشعياء النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: H/ppe ic Vnou] pacwt/r: P[oic eie~swpi e~re h~y/i ,/e~rof: ouoh n~naerho] je: pawcu nem pac~mou pe P[oic: afswpi n/i n~ououjai.
languages.english: Behold, God is my Savior and Lord. I will trust in Him and be saved by Him. I will not be afraid, for the Lord is my glory and my praise. He has become my salvation."
languages.arabic: هوذا الله خلاصى. الرب الذي أنا متكل عليه فلا أخاف. مجدى وتسبيحى هو الرب. وقد صار لي خلاصاً.

languages.coptic: Moh n~oumwou qen ouounof e~bolqen nimoumi n~te pioujai.
languages.english: You will draw water with gladness from the wells of salvation.
languages.arabic: املأوا ماء بفرح من ينابيع الخلاص.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ,~najoc qen piehoou e~tem~mau: je c~mou e~P[oic ws oube pefran:
languages.english: In that day, you will say, "Praise the Lord; call upon His name.
languages.arabic: وتقولون فى ذلك اليوم سبحوا الرب وادعوا اسمه.

languages.coptic: hiwis qen nieynoc n~nefh~b/oui~ ettai/out: a~riv~meui~ je af[ici n~je pefran.
languages.english: Declare His glorious things among the Gentiles and make mention that His name is exalted.
languages.arabic: وبشروا فى الأمم بأعماله الكريمة. أذكروا بان اسمه قد تعالى.

languages.coptic: Hwc e~v~ran m~P[oic je afiri n~hanh~b/oui~ eu[oci.
languages.english: Sing to the name of the Lord, for He has done great things.
languages.arabic: سبحوا اسم الرب لأنه قد صنع أعمالاً عظيمة،

languages.coptic: Yel/l ouoh ounof n/etsop qen Ciwn: je af[ici qen tem/] n~je v/eyu n~te p~iIcra/l.
languages.english: Exult and be glad, O inhabitants of Zion, for the Holy One of Israel is exalted in her midst."
languages.arabic: وافرحى ياساكنة صهيون لأنه قد إرتفع فى وسطك قدوس اسرائيل.

languages.coptic: Alioui~ n~oum/ini hijen nitwou efoi m~metswt: [ici n~netenc~m/ m~pererho]: ma]ouw~ qen ]jij a~ouwn n~hanar,wn
languages.english: "Lift up a sign on the mountain of the plain and raise your voice to them. Do not fear; comfort with your hand; open the gates, O you rulers.
languages.arabic: إرفعوا راية على الجبال السهلة. إرفعوا أصواتكم ولا تخافوا. أشيدوا باليد. إفتحوا للرؤساء

languages.coptic: Anok de eyouahcahni e~e~nou n~ceyou/t: a~nok eynan/ou cenai~ n~je hana~vwv e~jwk m~pajwnt: eue~rasi ouoh eue~]ws eucop.
languages.english: I command and I lead them. They are sanctified, and I lead them. My mighty ones come to fulfill My anger—rejoicing and insulting at the same time."
languages.arabic: لأنى أنا الذي أمرت أن يأتوا ويجتمعوا. وأنا الآتى وستأتى الجبابرة ليكملوا غضبى، فيفرحون ويعيدون معاً.

languages.coptic: Tc~m/ n~oum/s n~eynoc: t~c~m/ n~te hanourwou nem haneynoc euyou/t: P[oic cabawy afouahcahni n~ous~lol n~ref]hoplon.
languages.english: The sound of many nations upon the mountains, like that of many nations, the voice of kings and nations gathered together—the Lord of hosts commanded a warring nation.
languages.arabic: صوت أمم كثيرة وصوت ملوك وشعوب مجتمعة. رب الصباؤوت أمر شعباً مسلحاً،

languages.coptic: Eyrefi~ e~bolqen oukahi efou/ou: icjen aur/jc n~]cen] n~te t~ve: P[oic nem nefref]hoplon: e~p~jintako n~]oikoumen/ t/rc.
languages.english: To come from a far country, from the end of heaven, the Lord and His weapons, to destroy all the inhabited world.
languages.arabic: أن يأتى من أرض بعيدة من أقطار أساس السماء. والرب مع جنوده المسلحة ليهلك المسكونة كلها.

languages.coptic: Esl/loui~ e~bol f~qent gar n~je pie~hou m~P[oic cabawy: ouqomqem efe~i~ e~bolhiten Vnou].
languages.english: Wail, for the day of the Lord is at hand, and destruction will come from God.
languages.arabic: ولولوا فان يوم رب الجنود قد قرب. والأنكسار يأتى من عند الله.

languages.coptic: Eybe vai jij niben eue~]ouw~ e~bol.u,/ niben n~te nirwmi eue~erslah n~h/t.
languages.english: Therefore every hand will grow weary, and every man's soul will be fearful.
languages.arabic: فلذلك تسترخى كل يد. ويذوب قلب كل إنسان.

languages.coptic: Eue~s~yorter n~je nip~recbuteroc: ouoh hannakhi eue~[itou m~v~r/] n~y/eynamici: ouoh eue~erh/ibi ouai oube ouai m~mwou eue~twmt: ouoh eue~sib] m~pouho mvr/] n~ousah.
languages.english: The elders will be troubled. Pangs will take hold of them, like a woman in childbirth, and they will wail at one another and be amazed; and their countenance shall change like a flame.
languages.arabic: وتضطرب المشائخ ويأخذهم المخاض. ويتضورون كالتى تلد وينوح الواحد قبالة صاحبه ويتحيرون وتتغير وجوههم كاللهيب.

languages.coptic: H/ppe gar ic p~e~hoou m~P[oic n~at,w e~bol: f~n/ou qenoum~bon nem oujwnt: e~,w n~]oikoumen/ t/rc hip~safe: ouoh e~tako n~nirefernobi e~bol iwtc.
languages.english: Behold, the day of the Lord is coming, a day that cannot be averted, a day of anger and wrath, to make all the inhabited world a desert, and to destroy the sinners from it.
languages.arabic: هوذا يوم الرب يأتى بغير مغفرة. قادم بسخط وغضب. ليجعل المسكونة كلها برية قاحلة. ليبيد الخطاة منها.

languages.coptic: Niciou n~te t~ve nem piw~rion nem picolcel t/rf n~te t~ve: n~nef] m~piouwini: ouoh eue~er,aki e~rev~r/ a~sai: piioh n~nef] m~pefouwini:
languages.english: For the stars of heaven and Orion and all the ornament of heaven will not give their light. It will be dark when the sun goes forth, and the moon will not give its light.
languages.arabic: كواكب السماء والاوريون (النجوم) وكل زينتها لا تعطى نورها. وتظلم الشمس عند طلوعها. والقمر لا يعطى نوره.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Amwc pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Amos the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من عاموس النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ece~swpi qen pie~hoou e~tem~mau peje P[oic Vnou]: efe~hwtp n~je v~r/ m~v~nau m~meri ouoh efe~er,aki hijen p~kahi n~je piouwini qen pie~hoou:
languages.english: "And it will come to pass in that day," says the Lord God, "the sun will set at noon, and the light of day will grow dark over the land.
languages.arabic: ويكون فى ذلك اليوم يقول السيد الرب: تغيب الشمس وقت الظهيرة. ويظلم النور على الأرض فى النهار.

languages.coptic: Ouou ]navwnh n~netensai e~ouh/b ouoh netenhwd/ t/rou eunehpi. Ouoh ]na,w n~oucwk e~jen ]pi niben: nem oumetkereh e~jen a~ve niben: ouoh ]na,af m~v~r/] nouh/bi n~te oumenrit: ouoh n/eynemaf m~v~r/] n~oue~hoou n~te oum~kah n~h/t.
languages.english: And I shall turn your feasts into mourning and all your songs into lamentation. I shall place sackcloth on every waist and make every head bald. They shall be as one mourning a 'beloved one, and those with them as a day of grief."
languages.arabic: وأحول أعيادكم نوحاً وجميع تسابيحكم رثاء. وأجعل على كل حقوٍ مسحاً. وعلى كل رأس قرحاً. وأجعله مثل حزن الحبيب والذين معه كمثل يوم الحزن.

languages.coptic: H/ppe ic hane~hoou cen/ou peje P[oic Vnou]: ]naouwrp n~ouh~bwn hijen p~kahi: n~ouh~bwn n~ouwik n~h~bwn an pe: oude ouh~bwn m~mwou an pe: alla ouh~bwn e~cwtem e~p~caji m~P[oic:
languages.english: "Behold, the days will come," says the Lord, "that I shall send a famine across the land; not a famine of bread nor a thirst for water, but famine to hear the words of the Lord.
languages.arabic: هوذا ستأتى أيام يقول السيد الرب، إنى أرسل جوع على الأرض. وليس هو جوع إلى الخبز أو عطش إلى الماء بل هو جوع إلي إستماع كلمة الرب.

languages.coptic: Ouoh eue~kim n~je nimwou sae~q~r/i e~v~iom: ouoh eue~[oji icjen p~emhit sa niman~sai: eue~kwt n~ca p~caji m~P[oic ouoh n~noujemf.
languages.english: The waters will be unsettled as far as the sea. And from the north to the east men will scurry about seeking to find the word of the Lord, but they will not find it.
languages.arabic: فتتحول المياه إلي قاع البحر. ويجرون من الشمال إلي المشرق ويطلبون كلمة الرب فلا يجدونها.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: }e~pictol/ n~te pencaq Pauloc: e~re pef c~mou eyouab swpi neman: a~m/n.
languages.english: An epistle of our teacher Paul; may his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: رسالة معلمنا بولس الرسول بركته المقدسة لتكن معنا آمين.

languages.coptic: Pauloc v~bwk m~Pen[oic I/couc Pi,~rictoc pia~poctoloc etyahem v/e~tauyasf e~pihisennoufi n~te V].
languages.english: Paul, the servant of our Lord Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, appointed to the Gospel of God.
languages.arabic: بولس عبد يسوع المسيح الرسول المدعو المفرز لبشرى الله.

languages.coptic: Anok de n~necswpi n/i n~tasousou m~moi e~b/l qen pic~tauroc n~te Pen[oic I/couc Pi,~rictoc...
languages.english: But God forbid that I should boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ...
languages.arabic: وأما من جهتى فحاشا لي أن أفتخر إلا بصليب ربنا يسوع المسيح...

languages.english: After the reading, the following conclusion is chanted.
languages.arabic: وفي ختامه يقول:

languages.coptic: Pih~mot gar nemwten nem t~hirin/ eucop je a~m/n ece~swpi.
languages.english: Grace and peace be with you all. Amen. So be it.
languages.arabic: النعمة لكم والسلام معاً. أمين يكون.

languages.coptic: Pauloc v~bwk m~Pen[oic I/couc Pi,~rictoc pia~poctoloc etyahem v/e~tauyasf e~pihisennoufi n~te V].
languages.english: Paul, the servant of our Lord Jesus Christ, called to be an apostle, appointed to the Gospel of God.
languages.arabic: بولس عبد يسوع المسيح الرسول المدعو المفرز لبشرى الله.

languages.coptic: Anok de n~necswpi n/i n~tasousou m~moi e~b/l qen pic~tauroc n~te Pen[oic I/couc Pi,~rictoc: vai e~te ebolhitotf aui~si m~pikocmoc n/i: ouoh a~nok hw auast m~pikocmoc.
languages.english: But God forbid that I should boast except in the cross of our Lord Jesus Christ, by whom the world has been crucified to me, and I to the world.
languages.arabic: وأما من جهتى فحاشا لي أن أفتخر إلا بصليب ربنا يسوع المسيح. هذا الذي به قد صلب العالم لي، وأنا أيضاً صلبت للعالم.

languages.coptic: Nh~r/i gar qen Pi,~rictoc I/couc: oude p~cebi h~li pe oude ]metatcebi: alla oucwnt m~beripe.
languages.english: For in Christ Jesus neither circumcision nor uncircumcision avails anything, but a new creation.
languages.arabic: لأنه فى المسيح يسوع ليس الختان بشئ ولا الغرلة بل الخليقة الجديدة

languages.coptic: Ouoh ouon niben e~tau]ma] qen paikanwn: t~hir/n/ e~h~r/i e~jwou nem pinai: nem e~jen p~iIcra/l n~te Vnou].
languages.english: And as many as walk according to this rule, peace and mercy be upon them, and upon the Israel of God.
languages.arabic: فكل الذين يسلكون بهذا القانون عليهم السلام والرحمة وعلى اسرائيل الله.

languages.coptic: Pcepi de n~nai m~peny~reh~li ouah qici e~roi: a~nok gar niswlh n~te Pi,~rictoc ]fai qarwou qen pacwma. Pih~mot m~Penoc I/couc Pi,~rictoc nem petenpna nac~n/ou a~m/n:
languages.english: From now on let no one trouble me, for I bear in my body the marks of the Lord Jesus. Brethren, the grace of our Lord Jesus Christ be with your spirit. Amen.
languages.arabic: فيما بعد لا يجلب أحد علىّ آتٍعاباً، لأنى حامل فى جسدى سمات الرب يسوع. نعمة ربنا يسوع المسيح مع روحكم يا أخوتى آمين.

languages.english: After the reading, the following conclusion is chanted.
languages.arabic: وفي ختامه يقول:

languages.coptic: Pih~mot gar nemwten nem t~hirin/ eucop je a~m/n ece~swpi.
languages.english: Grace and peace be with you all. Amen. So be it.
languages.arabic: النعمة لكم والسلام معاً. أمين يكون.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: Auberbwrt e~bol a~nok pimenrit m~v~r/] n~ourefmwout efo~reb: ouoh au]fit e~tacarx: m~per,at n~cwk P[oic panou].
languages.english: They have rejected me, the beloved one, as an abominable dead carcass; they have pierced my body with nails. Do not forsake me, O Lord, O my God.
languages.arabic: رفضونى أنا الحبيب مثل ميت مرذول. وجعلوا مساميراً فى جسدى. فلا تهملنى ياربى وإلهى.

languages.coptic: Au[wlk n~tot nem rat auwp n~nakac t/rou: auvws n~nah~bwc e~h~raf ouoh tahebcw auhiwp e~roc: aucaji qen nouc~votou aukim n~toua~ve:
languages.english: They pierced my hands and my feet. They numbered all my bones, They divided my garments among themselves, and for my clothing they cast lots.
languages.arabic: ثقبوا يدي ورجلى وأحصوا كل عظامى. إقتسموا ثيابى بينهم وعلى لباسى إقترعوا.

languages.coptic: Aucaji qen nouc~votou aukim n~toua~ve: aujoc je icje afnah] aferhelpic e~P~[oic marefnahmef: mareftoujof icje afouasf: a#l#.
languages.english: They spoke with their lips, they shook the head, saying, "He hoped in the Lord, let Him rescue him; Let Him save Him since He delights in him." Alleluia.
languages.arabic: تكلموا بشفاههم وحركوا رؤوسهم وقالوا: إن كان آمن وإتكل على الرب. فليخلصه ولينجيه إن كان أراده. هلليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Matyeon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Matthew. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار متى البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Tote nimatoi n~te pihugemwn auwli n~I/couc e~qoun e~pipretwrion: ouoh auywou] e~]c~p/ra t/rc e~h~r/ e~jwf.
languages.english: Then the soldiers of the governor took Jesus into the Praetorium and gathered the whole garrison around Him.
languages.arabic: حينئذ أخذ جند الوالى يسوع وأدخلوه إلى الايوان وجمعوا عليه كل الكتيبة.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~taubasf au] n~ou,lamic n~kokkoc hiwtf:
languages.english: And they stripped Him and put a scarlet robe on Him.
languages.arabic: فعروه وألبسوه رداءً قرمزياً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh auswnt n~ou,~lom e~bolqen hancouri aut/if ejen tefa~ve: ouoh au,w n~oukas qen tefjij n~oui~nam: ouoh nauhioui~ m~mwou e~jen noukeli m~pef m~yo eucwbi m~mof eujw m~moc: je ,ere p~ouro n~niIoudai.
languages.english: When they had twisted a crown of thorns, they put it on His head, and a reed in His right hand. And they bowed the knee before Him and mocked Him, saying, “Hail, King of the Jews!”
languages.arabic: وضفروا إكليلاً من شوك ووضعوه على رأسه. وجعلوا فى يمينه قصبة. وكانوا يجثون على ركبهم قدامه ويستهزؤن به قائلين: السلام يا ملك اليهود.

languages.coptic: Ouoh n~touhiyaf e~qoun qen pefho: auw~li m~pikas auhioui~ qen tefa~ve.
languages.english: Then they spat on Him, and took the reed and struck Him on the head.
languages.arabic: وكانوا يبصقون فى وجهه. وأخذوا القصبة وضربوه على رأسه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tauouw eucwbi m~mof: aubasf n~],~lamic ouoh au] n~nefh~bwc hiwtf ouoh auolf eyrouasf.
languages.english: And when they had mocked Him, they took the robe off Him, put His own clothes on Him, and led Him away to be crucified.
languages.arabic: فلما هزأوا به نزعوا عنه الرداء وألبسوه ثيابه وأخذوه ليصلب.

languages.coptic: Eun/ou de e~bol aujimi n~ourwmi n~Kurinneoc: e~pefran pe Cumewn: vai au[itf n~,~ba hina n~tef el pefc~tauroc:
languages.english: Now as they came out, they found a man of Cyrene, Simon by name. Him they compelled to bear His cross.
languages.arabic: وفيما هم خارجون وجدوا رجلاً قيروانياً إسمه سمعان فسخروه ليحمل صليبه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~taui~ euma e~saumou] e~rof je Golgoya: e~te pima n~te Pik~ranion pe:
languages.english: And when they had come to a place called Golgotha, that is to say, Place of a Skull,
languages.arabic: ولما أتوا إلى المكان الذي يقال له الجلجثة، الذي هو موضع الجمجمة،

languages.coptic: Au] n~ouhemj naf efmoujt nem ouensasi: ouoh e~tafjem]pi m~pefouws e~cw:
languages.english: They gave Him sour wine mingled with gall to drink. But when He had tasted it, He would not drink.
languages.arabic: أعطوه خلاً ممزوجاً بمرارة. ولما ذاق لم يرد أن يشرب.

languages.coptic: Etauasf de auvws n~nefh~bwc e~h~rau e~auhiwp e~rwou.
languages.english: Then they crucified Him, and divided His garments, casting lots.
languages.arabic: ولما صلبوه إقتسموا ثيابه بينهم مقترعين عليها.

languages.coptic: Ouoh nauhemci eua~reh e~rof m~mau:
languages.english: Sitting down, they kept watch over Him there.
languages.arabic: ثم جلسوا يحرسونه هناك.

languages.coptic: Ouoh auc~qe tefe~tia~ cap~swi n~tefa~ve ecc~q/out m~pair/] je Vai Pe I/couc Pouro Nte Niioudai:
languages.english: And they put up over His head the accusation written against Him: THIS IS THE KING OF THE JEWS.
languages.arabic: وكتبوا قضيته فوق رأسه مكتوبة هكذا: هذا هو يسوع ملك اليهود.

languages.coptic: Tote aui~si n~keconi c~nau nemaf: ouai catefoui~nam: nem ouai catefja[/.
languages.english: Then two robbers were crucified with Him, one on the right and another on the left.
languages.arabic: حينئذ صلبوا معه لصين، واحد عن يمينه وواحد عن يساره.

languages.coptic: N/de e~naucini naujeoua~ e~rof pe: eukim n~toua~ve:
languages.english: And those who passed by blasphemed Him, wagging their heads,
languages.arabic: وكان المجتازون يجدفون عليه ويحركون رؤوسهم.
