languages.coptic: Ouoh a~Vnou] yamio~ m~pitajro qen y~m/] m~pimwou. Ouoh afvwrj e~bol n~je Vnou] oute pimwou etcap~swi m~pitajro. Ouoh a~Vnou] mou] e~pitajro je t~ve.
languages.english: Thus God made the firmament, and God divided the water under the firmament from the water above the firmament. So God called the firmament Heaven,
languages.arabic: فخلق الله الجلد فى وسط المياه وفصل الله بين المياه التى تحت الجلد والمياه التى فوق الجلد. ودعا الله الجلد سماء.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afnau Vnou] je nanef. Ouoh a~rouhi swpi ouoh a~tooui swpi m~piehoou m~mah c~nau.
languages.english: And God saw that it was good; and there was evening and morning, the second day.
languages.arabic: ورأى الله أن ذلك حسن. وكان مساء وكان صباح يوماً ثانياً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Vnou] je marefywou] n~je pimwou etcaq~r/i n~t~fve e~ouma n~ywou] n~ouwt. Ouoh maref ouwnh e~bol n~je pisouie. Ouoh acswpi m~pair/].
languages.english: Then God said, "Let the water under heaven be gathered together into one place, and let the dry land appear"; and it was so.
languages.arabic: وقال الله لتجتمع المياه التى تحت السماء إلى مجمع واحد وليظهر اليبس. وكان كذلك.

languages.coptic: Ouoh auywou] n~je nimwou etcaq~r/i e~t~ve e~ouma n~you]: afouwnh e~bol n~je pisouie:
languages.english: The water under heaven was gathered into its places, and the dry land appeared.
languages.arabic: فاجتمعت المياه التى تحت السماء مجمعاً واحداً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afmou] n~je Vnou] e~pisouie je kahi.Ouoh niman~ywou] n~te nimwou afmou] e~rwou je nia~maiou: ouoh afnau n~je Vnou] je nanef.
languages.english: So God called the dry land Earth, and the gathering together of the waters He called Seas; and God saw that it was good.
languages.arabic: وظهر اليبس ودعا الله اليبس أرضاً. ومجتمع المياه سماه بحاراً. ورأى الله ذلك أنه حسن

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Vnou] je mare p~kahi i~ni e~bol n~hanc~meh n~cim n~ci] n~jroj kata genoc nem kata i~ni: nem ous~s/n m~macoutah: efi~ri n~ououtah e~re pefjroj n~q/tf kata genoc hijen p~kahi: ouoh acswpi m~pair/]:
languages.english: Then God said, "Let the earth bring forth the herb of grass, bearing seed according to its kind and likeness. Let the fruit tree bear fruit, whose seed is in itself according to its kind on earth." It was so.
languages.arabic: وقال الله لتنبت الأرض نباتاً عشباً يبزر بزراً كجنسه وشكله وشجراً ذا ثمر يخرج ثمراً كجنسه بزره فيه على الأرض. وكان كذلك.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ap~kahi i~ni e~bol nhanc~meh n~cim n~ci] n~jroj kata genoc nem kata i~ni: nem ouss/n m~macoutah efiri n~ououtah e~repefjroj n~q/tf kata genoc hijen p~kahi.
languages.english: Thus the earth brought forth the herb of grass, bearing seed according to its kind and likeness. The fruit tree bore fruit, whose seed is in itself according to its kind on earth.
languages.arabic: فأخرجت الأرض نباتاً عشباً يبزر بزراً كجنسه وشجراً مثمراً يعمل ثمراً بزره فيه كجنسه على الأرض

languages.coptic: Ouoh afnau n~je Vnou] je nanef: Ouoh a~rouhi swpi ouoh a~tooui~ swpi m~pie~hoou m~mah somt.
languages.english: God saw that it was good. So evening and morning were the third day.
languages.arabic: ورأى الله ذلك أنه حسن. وكان مساء وكان صباح يوماً ثالثاً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Vnou] je marouswpi n~je hanreferouwini qen pitajro n~te t~ve eyrouerouwini hijen p~kahi ouoh n~tou vwrj e~bol oute pie~hoou nem oute piwrh:
languages.english: Then God said, "Let there be lights in the firmament of heaven for illumination to divide day from night.
languages.arabic: وقال الله لتكن أنوار فى جلد السماء لتنير على الأرض ولتفصل بين النهار والليل.

languages.coptic: Ouoh marouswpi e~hanm/ini nem hanc/ou nemhanehoou nem hanrompi: Ouoh marouswpi euerouwini qen pitajro n~te t~ve e~p~jin touerouwini hijen p~kahi. ouoh acswpi m~pair/]:
languages.english: Let them be for signs and seasons, and for days and years. Let them be for illumination in the firmament of heaven to give light on the earth." It was so.
languages.arabic: وتكون لآيات أوقات وأيام وسنين. وتكون أنوار فى جلد السماء لتضئ على الأرض. فكان كذلك.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afyamio[oic n~je Vnou] m~pinis] c~nau n~referouwini: pinis] n~ref erouwini e~ouar,/ n~te pie~hoou: ouoh pikouji n~referouwini e~ouar,/ n~te pie~jwrh nem niciou:
languages.english: Then God made two great lights: the greater light to rule the day, and the lesser light to rule the night. He made the stars also.
languages.arabic: فخلق الله النيرين العظيمين. النور الأكبر لسلطان النهار. والنور الأصغر لسلطان الليل مع النجوم.

languages.coptic: Ouoh af,au n~je Vnou] qen pitajro n~te t~ve hwc de n~touerouwini hijen p~kahi:
languages.english: God set them in the firmament of heaven to give light on the earth,
languages.arabic: وجعلها الله فى جلد السما لتضئ على الأرض.

languages.coptic: Ouoh n~touerar,wn e~pie~hoou nem pie~jwrh: ouoh n~tou vwrj e~bol oute piouwini nem oute pi,aki:
languages.english: And to rule over the day and over the night, and to divide the light from the darkness.
languages.arabic: وتتسلط على النهار والليل ولتفصل بين النور والظلام.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afnau n~je Vnou] je nanef: Ouoh a~rouh swpi ouoh a~tooui~ swpi m~pie~hoou m~mah f~tou.
languages.english: God saw that it was good. So evening and morning were the fourth day.
languages.arabic: ورأى الله ذلك حسن. وكان مساء وكان صباح يوماً رابعاً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Vnou] je mare nimwou i~ni e~bol n~han [atfi m~'u,/ euonq nem hanhala] euh/l hijen p~kahi kata pitajro n~te t~ve ouoh acswpi m~pair/]:
languages.english: Then God said, "Let the waters bring forth creatures having life, and let birds fly above the earth across the face of heaven's firmament." It was so.
languages.arabic: وقال الله لتفض المياه زحافات ذات أنفس حية وطيوراً تطير فوق الأرض على وجه جلد السماء وكان كذلك.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afyamio~ n~je Vnou] n~ninis] n~k/toc nem'u,/ niben etonq n~te ni[a~tfi n/eta nimwou e~nou e~bol kata genoc: nem hal/t niben e]h/l kata genoc: ouoh afnau n~je Vnou] je naneu.
languages.english: Thus God made great sea creatures and every living thing that moves with which the waters abounded, according to their kind, and every winged bird according to its kind. God saw that it was good.
languages.arabic: فخلق الله الحيتان العظام. وكل ذى نفس حية من الهوام التى فاضت بها المياه كأجناسها. وكل طائر ذى جناح كجنسه. ورأى الله ذلك أنه حسن

languages.coptic: Ouoh afc~mou e~rwou n~je Vnou] efjw m~moc je aiai ouoh a~sai ouoh mah nimwou etqen niamaiou. ouoh nihala] maroua~sai hijen p~kahi:
languages.english: God blessed them, saying, "Be fruitful and multiply, and fill the waters in the seas, and let birds multiply on earth."
languages.arabic: وباركها الله قائلاً: إنمى وإكثرى واملأى المياه فى البحار وليكثر الطير على الأرض.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~rouhi swpi ouoh a~tooui swpi m~pie~hoou m~mah t~iou.
languages.english: So evening and morning were the fifth day.
languages.arabic: وكان مساء وكان صباح يوماً خامساً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Vnou] je mare p~kahi i~ni e~bol nou'u,/ econq hanftevat nem han[atfi nem hany/rion n~te p~kahi kata genoc nem [atfi t/rou n~te p~kahi kata genoc: ouoh acswpi m~pair/]:
languages.english: Then God said, "Let the earth bring forth the living creature according to its kind: the quadrupeds, the creeping things, and the wild animals of the earth according to their kind." It was so.
languages.arabic: وقال الله لتخرج الأرض أنفس حية ذوات أربع وهوام ووحوش الأرض كأجناسها

languages.coptic: Ouoh afyamio n~je Vnou] n~niy/rion t/rou n~te p~kahi kata genoc nem nitebnwoui~ kata genoc nem ni[atfi t/rou n~te p~kahi kata genoc: ouoh afnau n~je Vnou] je naneu.
languages.english: So God made the wild animals of the earth according to their kind, the cattle according to their kind, and all the creeping things on earth according to their kind. God saw that it was good.
languages.arabic: وكل دبابات الأرض كأجناسها. والبهائم كأجناسها. وكل دبابات الأرض كأجناسها. ورأى الله ذلك أنه حسن.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Vnou] je marenyamio n~ourwmi kata tenhukwn nem kata peni~ni ouoh marouera,wn enitebt n~te v~iom nem nihala] n~te t~ve nem nitebnwoui nem p~kahi t/rf: nem [a~tfi niben etkim hijen p~kahi:
languages.english: Then God said, "Let Us make man in Our image, according to Our likeness. Let them have dominion over the fish of the sea, over the birds of heaven, over the cattle, and over all the earth, and over every creeping thing that moves on the earth."
languages.arabic: وقال الله لنخلق أنساناً على صورتنا وكمثالنا. وليتسلط على سمك البحر وطير السماء. والبهائم وعلى جميع الأرض. وكل ما يدب ويتحرك على الأرض.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~Vnou] yamio m~pirwmi kata ]hukwn n~te Vnou] afyamiof: afyamiwou n~ouhwout nem ouc~himi:
languages.english: God made man; in the image of God He made him; male and female He made them.
languages.arabic: فخلق الله الإنسان على صورته على صورة الله خلقه. ذكراً وأنثى خلقهم.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afc~mou e~rwou n~je Vnou] eujw m~moc je aiai ouoh a~sai ouoh mah p~kahi ouoh a~ri [oic e~jwf
languages.english: Then God blessed them; and God said to them, "Be fruitful and multiply; fill the earth and subdue it,
languages.arabic: وباركهم الله قائلاً: إنموا وإكثروا وإملأوا الأرض وسودوا عليها,

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~riar,wn e~nitebt n~te v~iom: nem nihala] n~te t~ve: nem nitebnwoui~ t/rou nem p~kahi t/rf: nem [a~tniben etkim hijen pikahi:
languages.english: And have dominion over the fish of the sea, over the birds of heaven, and over every living thing that moves on the earth."
languages.arabic: وتسلطوا على سمك البحر وطير السماء وجميع البهائم وسائر الأرض. وكل الهوام التى تدب على الأرض.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Vnou] je h/ppe ai]nwten n~cim niben n~ci] m~pci] n~oujroj v/et,/ hijen p~kahi t/rf.
languages.english: Then God said, "Behold, I have given you every seed-bearing herb that sows seed on the face of all the earth,
languages.arabic: وقال الله: ها قد أعطيتكم كل عشب مزروع يبزر بزراً على وجه الأرض كلها.

languages.coptic: Nem ss/n niben e~te pefoutah n~q/tf n~jroj n~ci] eue~ swpi nwten euq~re:
languages.english: And every tree whose fruit yields seed; to you it shall be for food.
languages.arabic: وكل شجرة فيه ثمر يبزر بزراً يكون لكم طعاماً.

languages.coptic: Nem niy/rion t/rou n~te p~kahi nem nihala] t/rou n~te t~ve nem [a~tfi niben etkim hijen pkahi e~touon ou'u,/ n~wnq ,/ m~mof nem cim niben e~touetouwt e~ouq~re ouoh acswpi m~pair/]:
languages.english: I also give every green plant as food for all the wild animals of the earth, for all the birds of heaven, and for everything that creeps on the earth in which is the breath of life." It was so.
languages.arabic: ولجميع وحوش الأرض وكل طير السماء. وكل ما يدب ويتحرك على الأرض مما فيه نفس حية. أعطيت كل عشب أخضر طعاماً. وكان كذلك.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afnau n~je Vnou] e~hwb niben e~tafyamiw~ou ouoh h/ppe naneu emasw ouoh a~rouhi swpi ouoh a~tooui~ swpi m~piehoou m~mah coou.
languages.english: Then God saw everything He had made, and indeed, it was very good. So evening and morning were the sixth day.
languages.arabic: ورأى الله جميع ما خلقه فأذا هو حسن جداً. وكان مساء وكان صباح يوماً سادساً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aujwk e~bol n~je t~ve nem p~kahi nem poucolcel t/rf:
languages.english: Thus heaven and earth and all their adornment were finished.
languages.arabic: فأكملت السماء والأرض وكل زينتها.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~Vnou] jek n~nef h~b/oui~ e~bol n/e~t afyamiwou qen pie~hoou m~mah coou:
languages.english: And on the seventh day God finished the works He made,
languages.arabic: وأكمل الله أعماله التى خلقها فى اليوم السادس.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afm~ton m~mof m~pie~hoou m~mah sasf e~bolha nefhb/oui~ t/rou n/e~tafyamiw~ou:
languages.english: And He rested on the seventh day from all the works He made.
languages.arabic: وأستراح فى اليوم السابع من جميع أعماله التى صنعها.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~Vnou] c~mou e~pie~hoou m~mah sasf ouoh: aftoubof je n~q~r/i n~q/tf am~ton m~mof e~bol ha nefhb/oui~ t/rou: n/e~taferh/tc n~yamiw~ou n~je Vnou].
languages.english: Then God blessed the seventh day and sanctified it, because in it He rested from all His works God began to make.
languages.arabic: وبارك الله اليوم السابع وقدسه. لأن فيه أستراح من جميع أعماله التى ابتدأ الله بخلقتها.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen ?ca/~ac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Isaiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من إشعياء النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Eie~]w~ou m~v/e~taumenritf qen oujw n~te pimenrit n~te pai iaha~loli:
languages.english: "Now I will sing to My Beloved a song of My Beloved regarding My vineyard:
languages.arabic: أمجد الذي أحبه بنشيد حبيب هذا الكرم.

languages.coptic: Ouma n~a~loli afswpi m~pimenrit qen ouhwp qen ouma efkeniw~out: ouoh aitakto n~ou[~lo e~rof ouoh aitalof e~p~kas
languages.english: There was a vineyard for My Beloved, a place in power and richness. He placed a wall around it and fortified it.
languages.arabic: كان للحبيب كرم فى رابية. فى موضع خصب. فاحطت به سياجاً ورفعته على القصب.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ai[o n~oubw n~a~loli qen Ewr/, ouoh aikwt n~oupurgoc n~q/tf qen tefm/]:
languages.english: He planted it with the choicest vine. He built a tower in its midst and also made a winepress in it.
languages.arabic: وغرست كرماً فى سورق وبنيت فيه برجاً فى وسطه. وحفرت فيه معصرة.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aiswk n~ouhrwt n~q/tf. Ouoh aiohi je f~naera~loli afercouri.
languages.english: So I waited for it to bring forth grapes, but it produced thorn-plants.
languages.arabic: وأنتظرت أن يخرج عنباً فأخرج شوكاً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ]nou v~rwmi n~Iouda nem n/etsop qen Ieroucal/m mahap outwi nem oute paiah a~loli aiohi je f~naera~loli afercouri.
languages.english: And now, man of Judah and inhabitants of Jerusalem, judge between Me and My vineyard.
languages.arabic: والآن يا رجال يهوذا وسكان أورشليم. أحكموا بينى وبين كرمى

languages.coptic: Oupe ]naaif m~paiaha~loli: ouoh m~piaif e~rof.
languages.english: What shall I yet do for My vineyard that I have not done for it?
languages.arabic: أى شئ يصنع لكرمى وأنا لم أصنعه به.

languages.coptic: }narwqt m~pef[~lo je aioh~i je f~naer a~loli afercouri:
languages.english: For I waited for it to bring forth grapes, but it produced thorn-plants.
languages.arabic: لأنى رجوت أن يخرج عنباً فأخرج شوكاً.

languages.coptic: }nou de ]natamwten.je oupe]naaif m~pa iaha~loli: ouoh efe~swpi e~p~hwlem: ]nasorser n~tefjoi ouoh efe~swpi e~p~qomqem:
languages.english: And now I will tell you what I will do to My vineyard; I will take away its hedge, and it shall be for a spoil; and I will pull down its walls, and it shall be left to be trodden down.
languages.arabic: فالآن أعلمكم ماذا أفعل بكرمى. أقلع سياجه فيصير للنهب. وأهدم جوسقه فيكون مدوساً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh eie~cwjp m~pai iaha~loli m~v~r/] n~ouesensatf oude n~nou [~r/ m~mof: ouoh eue~rwt n~q/tf n~je hancouri m~v~r/] n~ou kapso: eie~honhen e~totou n~ni[/pi e~s~temhwou n~oumou n~w~si e~h~r/i e~jwf.
languages.english: I will forsake My vineyard. It shall not be pruned or cultivated, but thorns shall sprout forth as in a barren land. I will also command the clouds not to rain on it."
languages.arabic: وأهمل هذا الكرم لا يقضب ولا يفلح وينبت فيه الشوك مثل السلا. وأوصى السحاب ألا تمطر عليه مطراً.

languages.coptic: Piiaha~loli gar n~te P[oic cabawy pe p~/i m~p~Icra/l: ouoh v~rwmi n~Iouda pitwji m~beri pee~taumenritf:
languages.english: For the vineyard of the Lord of hosts is the house of Israel, and the man of Judah is His beloved plant.
languages.arabic: لأن كرم رب الصباؤوت هو بيت اسرائيل. ورجال يهوذا الغرس الجديد المحبوب.

languages.coptic: Aiohi je f~nai~ri n~ouhap afi~ri n~oua~nomia~: ouoh n~oumeym/i an alla ouqrwou:
languages.english: I waited for it to bring forth judgment, but it brought forth lawlessness, and not righteousness, but a cry.
languages.arabic: رجوت أن يصنع إنصافاً فصنع إثماً. وعدلاً فاذا صراخ.

languages.coptic: Ouoi n~n/ettem /i e~/i nem n/etqent iohi eiohi hina n~touwli n~nien,ai n~te nous~v/r:
languages.english: Woe to those who join house to house, who add field to field, to take something from their neighbor.
languages.arabic: ويل للذين يصلون بيتاً ببيت. ويقرنون حقلاً بحقل. لكى يسلبوا أصحابهم.

languages.coptic: M/ teten naswpi m~maua~ten y/nou hijen p~kahi: aucwtem nai gar qen nenmasj m~P[oic cabawy.
languages.english: Surely you will not dwell alone in the land. For these things reached the ears of the Lord of hosts.
languages.arabic: إذن أنتم تسكنون الأرض وحدكم. قد سمع هذا فى مسامع رب الصباؤوت.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen I/cou n~te Cira, pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from the Wisdom of Joshua, son of Sirach, the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من يشوع بن سيراخ النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Tcovia~ t/rc ou e~bol hiten P[oic: ouoh c~sop nemaf sa e~neh: nim eynaswp m~pisw n~te v~iom nem niteltili m~mounhwou nem nie~hoou n~te nie~neh:
languages.english: All wisdom comes from the Lord and is with Him forever. Who can count the sand of the seas, the drops of rain, and the days of eternity?
languages.arabic: كل الحكمة فهى من قبل الرب. وهى دائمة معه إلي الأبد. من يقدر أن يحصى رمل البحر. وقطرات المطر وأيام الدهور.

languages.coptic: Nem eynas~qotqet m~pi[ici n~te t~ve: nem p~tajro m~pikahi nem p~s/k m~v~noun nem ]covia~:
languages.english: Who can search out the height of heaven, the breadth of the earth, the abyss, and wisdom?
languages.arabic: من يستطيع أن يمسح علو السماء. ورحب الأرض وعمق الغمر والحكمة.

languages.coptic: Qajen en,ai niben aucwnt n~]covia~ ouoh ]metcabe nem pika] icjen p~e~neh: }moumi n~te ]covia~ pe picaji n~te Vnou] qen n/e[oci: ouoh necmwit: ne nientol/ sa e~neh. Ynouni n~]covia~ e~tac[wrp e~nim ouoh nim pee~tafe~mi e~neckotc: p~ka] n~]covia e~tafouwnh e~nim:
languages.english: Wisdom was created before all things, and the insight of prudence was from eternity. To whom has the root of wisdom been revealed? And who has come to know her great deeds?
languages.arabic: قبل كل شئ حيزت الحكمة. لمن أستعلن أصل الحكمة. ومن عرف خفاياها لمن تجلت معرفة الحكمة. ومن أدرك كثرة خبرتها.

languages.coptic: Ouoh pece~mi t/rf nim pee~taf couwnf ouwt pe oucovoc ef] ho] e~masw efhemci hijen pefy~ronoc:
languages.english: There is one who is wise and is feared exceedingly, He who sits upon His throne.
languages.arabic: واحد هو حكيم عظيم المهابة جالس على عرشه.

languages.coptic: P[oic de n~yof v/e~tafcontc ouoh afnau e~roc afopc: ouoh afvwn m~moc e~h~r/i e~jen nech~b/oui~ t/rou:
languages.english: The Lord Himself created wisdom. He saw and numbered her and poured her out on all His works,
languages.arabic: الرب هو الذي حازها ورآها وأحصاها. وأفاضها على جميع أعمالها.

languages.coptic: Cswp nem carx niben kata v~r/] e~tefnat/ic. ouoh saft/ic n~n/eymei m~mof.
languages.english: In the midst of all flesh according to His gift; and He provided her for those who love Him.
languages.arabic: فهى مع كل ذى جسد على حسب عطيته. وقد منحها لمحبيه.

languages.coptic: }ho] n~te P[oic ouwou te nem ou sousou: nem ouounof te nem ou ,~lom n~yel/l.
languages.english: The fear of the Lord is glory and boasting, and gladness and a crown of rejoicing.
languages.arabic: مخافة الرب هى مجد وفخر. وسرور وأكليل فرح.

languages.coptic: Sare ]ho] n~te P[oic ]erouwt m~pih/t: sac] n~ouounof nem ourasi ne ouei n~te hane~hoou: v/eterho] qath/ m~P[oic: pipeynanef naswpi naf qen te qae~: ouoh eue~a~moni m~mof qen pie~hoou n~]e pefmou:
languages.english: The fear of the Lord will cheer the heart and will give gladness, joy, and long life. For those who fear the Lord, it shall be well to the utmost, and on the day of his death, he will be blessed.
languages.arabic: مخافة الرب تبهج القلب. وتعطى فرحاً وسروراً وطول أيام. المتقى الرب يكون له الخير فى آخرته. وينال حظوة فى موته.

languages.coptic: Tar,/ n~]covia pe e~erho] qath/ m~P[oic: ouoh aucontc nem nipictoc qen to]: auhicen] m~moc saeneh nem nirwm ouoh c~naten houtc nem poujroj.
languages.english: The beginning of wisdom is to fear the Lord, and she was joined with the faithful in the womb. She constructed a foundation of life among men and will be trusted among their seed.
languages.arabic: رأس الحكمة مخافة الرب. أنها تولدت فى الرحم مع المؤمنين وتأسست مع البشر مدى الدهر. وهى تثبت مع نسلهم.

languages.coptic: Pcai n~]covia~ pe e~erho] qath/ m~P[oic: ouoh ouon ouounof e~bol qen neckarpoc:
languages.english: The gratification of wisdom is to fear the Lord, and she intoxicates them with her fruits.
languages.arabic: كمال الحكمة مخافة الرب. والفرح من ثمارها.

languages.coptic: Sacmah pec/i t/rf n~n/etcwtp: ouoh nec twbi hane~bolqen necgen/ma ne:
languages.english: She will fill every house of theirs with objects of desire and their storehouses with her harvest.
languages.arabic: تملأ كل بيتها من المختارين. وترويهم من عصيرها.

languages.coptic: P,lom n~]covia~ pe ]ho] n~te Vnou] ec]ouw n~ouh~r/ri nem ouh~mot n~tal[o: afnau e~roc afopc: afbebi m~pika] nem pie~mi nem ]metcabe: af[ici m~p~w~ou m~v/et a~moni m~moc.
languages.english: The fear of the Lord is the crown of wisdom, making peace and soundness of health to flourish. The Lord saw and numbered her, and poured out the power of comprehension; and He exalted the glory of those who hold fast to her.
languages.arabic: تاج الحكمة مخافة الله. وهى تنشئ زهرة ونعمة الشفاء. قد رآها وأحصاها وأفاض الفهم والمعرفة والعلم. ورفع مجد المتمسك بها.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

title.english: Homily of Abba Shenouda the Archimandrite
title.arabic: عظة لابينا القديس أنبا شنودة رئيس المتوحدين

readings.reference: Homily of Abba Shenouda the Archimandrite

languages.coptic: Oukat/,/cic n~te peniwt eyouab abba Senou] pia~r,/mand~rit/c: e~re pefc~mou eyouab swpi neman: a~m/n.
languages.english: A homily of our holy father Abba Shenouda the archimandrite. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: عظة لابينا القديس أنبا شنودة رئيس المتوحدين: بركته المقدسة تكون معنا آمين.

languages.coptic: Eswp je ]nou w~ni e~n/ou tenouws e~er e~bol e~nenjij m~Vnou] ouoh n~tenjimi n~ounai nahraf marenhemci e~q~r/ n~rouhi m~m/ni ca p~ca m~maua~ten: menenca p~jwk m~pie~hoou n~tenqetqwten m~min m~mon: je e~tan] nou m~piaggeloc eterdiakonin e~ron eyref enf e~p~swi haP[oic.
languages.english: Brethren, if we want to escape God’s punishment and find mercy in his eyes, let us sit every evening alone by ourselves and search our souls for what we presented to our guardian angel to offer to the Lord.
languages.arabic: يا أخوة. إن كنا نريد الآن أن نفلت من يدي عقاب الله. ونجد رحمة أمامه. فلنجلس بالعشاء كل يوم منفردين وحدنا عند كمال النهار. ونفتش ذواتنا عما قدمناه للملاك الذي يخدمنا (الملازم لنا) ليصعده إلى الرب.

languages.coptic: Ouoh on aresan pie~jwrh jwkebol. ntefi~ e~p~swi n~,e piouwini n~tenqetqwten m~mauaten ouoh n~tene~mi jeoupe e~tennat/if m~piaggeloc ety/s e~ron: eyrefenf ep~swi ha Vnou]:
languages.english: Again, as the night goes by and a new day dawns and light prevails, let us search ourselves to know what we presented to our companion angel to offer to God.
languages.arabic: وأيضاً إذا إنقضى الليل وطلع النهار (وأشرق النور) نفتش ذواتنا وحدنا ونعلم ما الذي قدمناه للملاك الموكل بنا ليصعده إلى الله.

languages.coptic: Ouoh mper[icanic holwc e~bol: je rwmi niben i~tehwout: i~te c~himi i~tekouji: ite nis]: e~af[iwmc e~v~ran m~Viwt nem Ps/ri nem Pip~neuma eyouab: sare Vnou] ,w n~ouaggeloc efy/s e~rof: sapie~hoou ntepefmou:
languages.english: Let it be beyond doubt that everyone of us—male or female—young or old, who was baptized in the name of the Father, the Son and the Holy Spirit has been assigned to a designated angel until the day of his death;
languages.arabic: ولا نشك البتة لأن كل أنسان ذكراً كان أو أنثى صغيراً أو كبيراً. قد أعتمد بإسم الآب والإبن والروح القدس قد جعل الله له ملاكاً موكلاً به إلى يوم وفاته.

languages.coptic: efi~ni e ps~wi harof m~m/ni n~nih~b/oui~ n~te pirwmi e~tefjal/out e~rof napiehoou nem napiejwrh:
languages.english: To report to him every day what his assigned individual has done by day or by night.
languages.arabic: وليصعد إليه كل يوم أعمال الإنسان الموكل به (الليلية والنهارية)

languages.coptic: e~boljenafoi n~ate~mi an n~jeVnou] e~nenhb/oui~ n~necswpi: alla fcwoun m~mwou n~qouo:
languages.english: Not that God is unaware of what we have done. Heaven forbid. He is more knowledgeable about it.
languages.arabic: ليس لأن الله غير عارف بأعمالنا. حاشا. بل هو عارف بها أكثر.

languages.coptic: Kata v~r/] etc~q/out: jeic nenbal m~P[oic cejoust n~c/ou niben qen mai niben e~jen n/eti~ri mpipethwou nem n/eti~ri m~pipeynanef:
languages.english: As is written, the eyes of the Lord are watching all the time everywhere on those who commit evil and on those who do good.
languages.arabic: كما هو مكتوب أن عينى الرب ناظرة كل حين فى كل مكان على صانعى الشر وفاعلى الخير.

languages.coptic: Alla hanrefsemsi ne e~tafyasou hi nai n~je pidumiourgoc m~p~t/rf: eybe n/eynaer k~l/ronomin n~ounohem.
languages.english: Rather the angels are servants installed by the Creator of the universe for those who will inherit the salvation.
languages.arabic: إنما الملائكة هم خدام قد أقامهم خالق الكل. من أجل المزمعين لوراثة الخلاص.

languages.coptic: Marener c~v~rajizin n~]kat/,/cic n~te peniwt eyouab abba Senou] pia~r,/mand~rit/c v/e~taferouwini m~pennouc: nem nibal n~te nenh/t: qen v~ran m~Viwt nem Ps/ri nem Pip~neuma eyouab ounou] n~ouwt: a~m/n.
languages.english: We conclude the homily of our holy father Abba Shenouda the archimandrite who enlightened our minds and our hearts. In the name of the Father and the Son and the Holy Spirit, one God. Amen.
languages.arabic: فلنختم موعظة أبينا القديس أنبا شنودة رئيس المتوحدين الذي أنار عقولنا وعيون قلوبنا بإسم الآب والإبن والروح القدس الإله الواحد آمين.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: Fc~marwout n~je P[oic Vnou] m~Pic~ra/l: v/eti~ri n~hans~v/ri m~mauatf.
languages.english: Blessed is the Lord God, the God of Israel, who alone does wondrous things;
languages.arabic: مبارك الرب إله اسرائيل الصانع العجائب وحده.

languages.coptic: Fc~marwout n~je piran eyouab n~te pefw~ou sae~neh: ece~swpi ece~swpi: all/louia.
languages.english: And blessed is the name of His glory forever. So be it. So be it. Alleluia.
languages.arabic: مبارك أسم مجده القدوس إلى الأبد يكون يكون. الليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Markon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Mark. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار مرقس البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~pefrac] e~taui e~bol qen e~B/ya~nia~ afh~ko:
languages.english: Now the next day, when they had come out from Bethany, He was hungry.
languages.arabic: وفى الغد لما خرجوا من بيت عنيا جاع.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafnau e~oubw n~kente hivouei e~ouon hanjwbi hiwtc afi~ de hara afnajem h~li hiwtc
languages.english: And seeing from afar a fig tree having leaves, He went to see if perhaps He would find something on it.
languages.arabic: فرأى شجرة تين عن بعد وكان عليها ورق. فجاء إليها لعله يجد فيها شيئا.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafi~ e~jwc m~pefjem h~li e~b/l e~hanjwbi: ne p~c/ou gar n~kente an pe:
languages.english: When He came to it, He found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
languages.arabic: فلما جاء إليها لم يجد شيئا إلا ورقا لأنه لم يكن وقت التين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafe~rouw~ n~je I/couc pejaf nac je n~neh~li je ouem outah hiw~] sae~neh ouoh naucwtem n~je nefmay/t/c.
languages.english: In response Jesus said to it, “Let no one eat fruit from you ever again.” And His disciples heard it.
languages.arabic: فأجاب يسوع وقال لها لا يأكل أحد منك ثمرة بعد إلى الأبد. وكان تلاميذه يسمعون.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aui~ e~Ieroucal/m ouoh e~tafi~ e~qoun n~je I/couc e~piervei aferh/tc n~hioui~ e~bol n~n/t/rou et] e~bol nem n/etswp qen piervei
languages.english: So they came to Jerusalem. Then Jesus went into the temple and began to drive out those who bought and sold in the temple,
languages.arabic: وجاءوا إلى أورشليم ولما دخل يسوع الهيكل ابتدأ يخرج الذين كانوا يبيعون ويشترون فى الهيكل.

languages.coptic: Ouoh nit~rapeza n~te nikolibict/c nem nikayedra n~te n/et] [~rompi e~bol afcocou:
languages.english: And overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
languages.arabic: وقلب موائد الصيارفة وكراسى باعة الحمام.

languages.coptic: Ouoh naf,w nhli an pe hina ntefen ouc~keuoc e~bolhiten piervei:
languages.english: And He would not allow anyone to carry wares through the temple.
languages.arabic: ولم يدع أحداً يجتاز الهيكل بمتاع.

languages.coptic: Ouoh naf]c~bw ouoh nafjw m~moc nwou je c~cq/out je pa/i eue~mou] e~rof je ou/i m~p~roceu,/ n~nieynoc t/rou n~ywten de a~retenaif m~b/b n~coni.
languages.english: Then He taught, saying to them, “Is it not written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all nations’? But you have made it a ‘den of thieves.’”
languages.arabic: وكان يعلم قائلاً لهم أليس مكتوباً أن بيتى بيت الصلاة يدعى لجميع الأمم. وأنتم جعلتموه مغارة للصوص.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aucwtem n~je niar,/e~reuc nem nicaq ouoh naukw] je aunatakof n~as n~r/] nauerho] gar pe qatefh/ pim/s gar t/rf nauers~v/ri e~jen tefc~bw:
languages.english: And the scribes and chief priests heard it and sought how they might destroy Him; for they feared Him, because all the people were astonished at His teaching.
languages.arabic: وسمع رؤساء الكهنة والكتبة وكانوا يطلبون بأى نوع يهلكونه لأنهم كانو يخافونه لأن الجمع كله كان يتعجب من تعليمه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~swp n~te rouhi swpi nafh/l cabol n~]baki:
languages.english: When evening had come, He went out of the city.
languages.arabic: وإذا كان المساء خرج إلى خارج المدينة.

languages.coptic: Ouoh euciniw~ou n~hana~tooi~ afnau e~]bw n~kente e~acswoui~ hi tecnouni:
languages.english: Now in the morning, as they passed by, they saw the fig tree dried up from the roots.
languages.arabic: وفى الصباح إذ كانوا مجتازين رأوا التينة قد يبست من أصلها.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~taferv~meui n~je Petroc pejaf naf je rabbi ic ]bw n~kente etakcahoui~ e~roc acswoui~:
languages.english: And Peter, remembering, said to Him, “Rabbi, look! The fig tree which You cursed has withered away.”
languages.arabic: فتذكر بطرس وقال له. ربى ها أن التينة التى لعنتها قد يبست.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~taferouw~ n~je I/couc pejaf nwou je ,aounah] n~te Vnou] n~teny/nou: a~m/n gar ]jwm~moc nwten je v/eynajoc m~paitwou je fitk ouoh hitk e~v~iom ouoh n~tefs~tem [icanic qen pefh/t alla n~tefnah] je v/e~tefjwm~mof f~naswpi efe~swpi naf:
languages.english: So Jesus answered and said to them, “Have faith in God. For assuredly, I say to you, whoever says to this mountain, ‘Be removed and be cast into the sea,’ and does not doubt in his heart, but believes that those things he says will be done, he will have whatever he says.
languages.arabic: فأجاب يسوع وقال لهم ليكن لكم إيمان بالله. لأنى الحق أقول لكم أن من يقول لهذا الجبل إنتقل وإنطرح فى البحر وهو لا يشك فى قلبه بل يؤمن بأن ما يقوله يكون فإنه يكون له.

languages.coptic: Eybe vai ]jwm~moc nwten je hwb niben e~tetennaere~tin m~mof qen oup~roceu,/ nah] je a~reten[i ouoh ece~swpi nwten.
languages.english: Therefore I say to you, whatever things you ask when you pray, believe that you receive them, and you will have them.
languages.arabic: فلأجل ذلك أقول لكم أن كل شئ تسألونه فى الصلاة آمنوا أنكم تنالونه فيكون لكم.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: The exposition of the First Hour of Monday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة الاولى، من يوم الإثنين من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: In the beginning God created the heavens and the earth and adorned them with His Spirit. He covered darkness and unveiled the light, and distinguished between them with new names. He called the light day and the darkness night. He created all these in the same day with wisdom and prudence.
languages.arabic: فى البدء خلق الله السماء والأرض وزينها هكذا بروح فيه وغطى الظلمة وأخرج النور وفرق بينهما بأسماء جديدة. ودعا النور نهاراً ودعا الظلمة ليلاً. وفى ذلك اليوم خلق هذه جميعها بحكمة وفهم رفيع.

languages.english: On the second day God created the firmament and separated between waters and waters. God established the waters above the firmament and called them Heaven.
languages.arabic: وفى اليوم الثانى خلق الله جلد السماء وفصل بين مياه ومياه. وبعد هذا ثبت الله الماء العلوى وأسماه سماء.

languages.english: On the third day He gathered the waters and made the land appear over the waters, and He created all kinds of grass, plants, and fruit yielding trees.
languages.arabic: وفي اليوم الثالث جمع المياه وثبت الأرض فوق المياه وخلق أجناس الشجر وزرع الحقل والعشب المزروع المثمر.

languages.english: On the fourth day God created the sun, the moon, and the multitude of stars.
languages.arabic: والشمس والقمر وكثرة النجوم خلقها الله في اليوم الرابع.

languages.english: On the fifth day God created the birds, the big whales, the farm animals.
languages.arabic: وخلق الهوام والطير والحيتان الكبار وحيوانات الحقل في اليوم الخامس.

languages.english: On the sixth day God created Adam the first man and a companion for him from his own flesh, male and female as designated; and gave them dominion over all the creatures He created.
languages.arabic: وفى اليوم السادس خلق المخلوق الحي العظيم آدم أول البشر ومعينة له من جسده ذكراً وأنثى كالتدبير. هذان جعلهما رؤساء على جميع أعماله التى خلقها الخالق.

languages.english: God rested on the seventh day from all the work which He had done. These are the designs of the Creator and the Initiator of all creation.
languages.arabic: وأستراح فى اليوم السابع لأن فيه أكمل جميع أعماله. هذا تدبير الخالق ومؤسس كل الموجودات.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen ?ca/~ac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Isaiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من إشعياء النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoi n~n/etjw m~moc: Je nane pipethwou: ouoh f~hwou n~je pipeynanef: n/et swp m~pi,aki je ououwini: ouoh piouwini je ou,aki pe:
languages.english: Woe to those who call evil good and good evil, who put darkness for light and light for darkness,
languages.arabic: ويل للقائلين للخير شراً وللشر خيراً. الجاعلين الظلام نوراً والنور ظلاماً.

languages.coptic: N/etjwm~moc e~petholj je ouensasi ouoh euoi piensasi je f~holj:
languages.english: Who put bitter for sweet and sweet for bitter!
languages.arabic: القائلين عن الحلو مراً والمر حلواً.

languages.coptic: Ouoi n~n/etoi n~cabe nwou m~mauatou: ouoh n~refka] m~poum~yo e~bol:
languages.english: Woe to those who are intelligent in their own eyes and expert in their own sight!
languages.arabic: ويل للحكماء الذين هم فى أعين أنفسهم والفهماء عند ذاوتهم.

languages.coptic: Ouoi n~netenjwri nai etcw m~pi/rp ouoh n~dunact/c nai etywq m~picikira.
languages.english: Woe to your strong ones who drink wine and the mighty ones who mix intoxicating drink,
languages.arabic: ويل للأقوياء الذين يشربون الخمر. وللأبطال الذين يمزجون المسكر.

languages.coptic: N/ety~maio m~pia~ceb/c eybe oudwron: ouoh auwli n~]meym/i n~te piy~m/i:
languages.english: Who justify the ungodly for a bribe and take away justice from the righteous man!
languages.arabic: الذين يبررون المنافق لأجل الرشوة. وينزعون حق البار.

languages.coptic: Eybe vai m~v~r/] e~sare ourwoui~ moh hiten hanjebc n~,~rwm: ouoh e~saurwkh hiten ousah efcwk:
languages.english: Therefore in the manner that stubble shall be burned by coals of fire and consumed by a violent flame,
languages.arabic: فلذلك كما يحترق البروبى (القش) بجمر النار. ويلتهب بلهيبه المشتعل.

languages.coptic: Ece~swpi n~je tounouni m~v~r/] n~our/ici ouoh touh~r/ri ece~swpi m~v~r/] noushij: m~pouws gar m~vnomoc mVnou] P[oic cabawy: alla au]jwnt m~p~caji m~peyouab m~pI~cra/l:
languages.english: Their root shall be like chaff and their flower shall go up like dust, for they did not will to do the law of the Lord of hosts, but despised the word of the Holy One of Israel.
languages.arabic: فهكذا يكون أصلهم كالغبار وزهرهم كالهشيم لأنهم رذلوا شريعة الله رب الجنود. وأغضبوا واستهانوا بكلمة قدوس اسرائيل.

languages.coptic: Ouoh P[oic cabawy afjwnt qen oum~bon e~jen peflaoc: ouoh afentotfe~sari e~rwou: ouoh afm~bon e~jen nitwou:
languages.english: Thus the wrath of the Lord of hosts is furious against His people. He laid His hand on them and struck them.
languages.arabic: فحمى غضب رب الصباؤوت على شعبه. ورفع يده عليهم ليضربهم.

languages.coptic: Auswpi n~je nourefmwout m~v~r/] nnii~ten qeny~m/] m~pimwit
languages.english: The mountains were provoked, and their carcasses were as refuse in the midst of the road.
languages.arabic: وسخط على الجبال وصارت جثث موتاهم مثل الزبل فى وسط الطريق.

languages.coptic: Ouou e~jen nai ]/rou m~peftacyo n~je pijwnt alla e~ti c~[oci n~je ]jij.
languages.english: For all this His anger was not turned away, but His hand is still uplifted.
languages.arabic: ومع هذا كله لم يرتد غضبه. بل يده لم تزل عالية ممدودة.

languages.coptic: Eybe vai je efe~w~li n~oum/ini e~nieynoc ethiv~ouei ouoh n~tefcokou icjen aur/jf m~p~kahi.
languages.english: Consequently He shall take up a banner to the nations from afar and will whistle to them from the ends of the earth.
languages.arabic: من أجل ذلك يرفع علامة للأمم من بعيد ويجتذبهم من أقاصى الأرض

languages.coptic: Ouoh h/ppe ce e~nou n~,wlem eua~ciw~ou: N~nouh~ko gar oude n~nouqici n~cenahunim an:
languages.english: Behold, they are coming swiftly and with speed. They shall not hunger or be weary.
languages.arabic: فإذا هم يأتون سريعاً بخفة. لا يجوعون ولا يتعبون. ولا ينعسون.

languages.coptic: Ouoh n~cenaenkot an: n~noubwl n~nou moujq e~bolqen nou]pi: n~nefcwlp e~bol n~je oumouc/r n~te pouyw oui~: Naie~re noucoynefsepswp: ouoh nouvi] ce[olk:
languages.english: Neither shall they slumber nor sleep. Nor shall they loose the belts from their loins. Neither shall their sandal-straps be broken. Their arrows are sharp, and their bows are bent.
languages.arabic: ولا ينامون. ولا يحلون مناطقهم من أوساطهم. ولا تنقطع سيور أحذيتهم. هؤلاء الذين سهامهم مسنونة وقسيهم مشدودة (موترة)

languages.coptic: Ere ni[alauj n~te nouh~ywr oi m~v~r/] n~oupetra n~,oh n~swt: e~re nit~ro,oc n~te nouhm~v~r/] n~oucaray/ou.
languages.english: The hooves of their horses seem like flint and their chariot wheels like a sudden blast of wind.
languages.arabic: حوافر خيولهم كصخرة صلبة. وبكرات مراكبهم كالعاصف.

languages.coptic: Sau] m~pououoi m~v~r/] n~nimoui~: ouoh efe~a~moni n~tefws e~bol m~vr/] n~ouy/rion: n~tef hioui~ e~bol n~tef s~temswpi n~je v/eynanahmou:
languages.english: They rush like lions, and stand like a young lion that seizes his prey and roars like a wild animal, then carries it away; and there shall be no one to deliver them.
languages.arabic: يتقدمون كالأسد. ويمسك ويصيح كوحش ويطرح وليس من يخلص.

languages.coptic: Ouoh efe~ws e~bol eyb/tou qen pie~hoou e~tem~mau: m~v~r/] n~ouqrwou n~te v~iom efhihiwmi: ouoh eue~joust e~p~wsi e~t~ve nem e~q~r/i e~p~kahi: n~cejimi n~ou,aki efnast: ou,aki qen outwmt.
languages.english: In that day, they will bellow against them like the sound of the roaring sea. They will look at the land and behold harsh darkness in their despair.
languages.arabic: ويصرخ عليهم فى ذلك اليوم كصوت البحر المتموج (الخضم). وينظرون إلى علو السماء وإلى أسفل الأرض فيجدوا ظلاماً شديداً. وظلمة مدلهمة.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Iermiac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Jeremiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من أرميا النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Nim pe pirwmi n~cabe marefe~mi e~vai: ouoh v/e~te picaji n~terwf m~P[oic ,/ nahraf maref tame y/nou: je eybe ou aftako n~je p~kahi: afrwkh m~vr/] n~ousafe: e~stem y~roumosi nq/tf:
languages.english: Who is a wise man? Let him understand this. Let him declare to you by the word of the Lord to him why the land was ravaged by fire, and became like a desert no one could pass through.
languages.arabic: من هو الإنسان الحكيم فليفهم هذا. والذي عنده كلام فم الرب فليخبركم. لماذا هلكت الأرض واحترقت وصارت كبرية بلا عابر.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje P[oic n/i: je e~bolje au,w m~panomoc n~cwou: v/e~tait/if m~poum~yo e~bol: ouoh m~poucwtem n~catac~m/:
languages.english: The Lord answered me, "Because they forsook My law which I set before their face, and they did not hear My voice.
languages.arabic: فقال لى الرب. لأنهم تركوا ناموسى عنهم الذي جعلته أمامهم. ولم يسمعوا لصوتى.

languages.coptic: Alla auoua~hou n~ca n/eyranaf m~pouh/t ethwou: ouoh aumosi cavahou n~nii~dwlon: nai e~tanouio] tcabwou e~rwou.
languages.english: Instead, they walked after what pleased their evil heart, and after the idols their fathers taught them."
languages.arabic: بل ساروا وراء عناد قلوبهم الشريرة. وسلكوا وراء الأصنام

languages.coptic: Eybe vai nai ne n/e~tefjw m~mwou n~je P[oic Vnou] m~pI~c#l#: je h/ppe a~nok eie~temmwou n~hanwik n~a~nagk/: ouoh n~tat~cwou n~han,ol/:
languages.english: Therefore, thus says the Lord God of Israel: "Behold, I will feed them as a matter of necessity, and give them the water of bitterness to drink.
languages.arabic: التى علمهم إياها أباؤهم لذلك قال الرب إله اسرائيل. هأنذا أطعمهم خبز الضيق وأسقيهم العلقم.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ]najorou e~bol qen nieynoc nai e~te n~cecwoun m~mwou an nem noukeio]: ouoh ]naouwrp e~h~r/i ejwou n~ouc/fi sa ]mounkou n~q~r/i n~q/tc:
languages.english: I will also scatter them among the Gentiles, amongst whom neither they nor their fathers are known. I shall send a sword after them until I consume them with it."
languages.arabic: وأشتتهم فى الأمم التى لم يعرفوها هم ولا أباؤهم. وأرسل عليهم سيفاً حتى أفنيهم به.

languages.coptic: Nai ne n/ e~tefjw m~mwou n~je P[oic: je mou] e~nihiomi n~refnehpi maroui~: ouoh ouwrp n~ca nicabeu marououwn n~rwou e~caji n~cejw n~ounehpi e~jen y/nou:
languages.english: Thus says the Lord: "Call for the mourning women and let them come. Send for the wise women and let them give utterance.
languages.arabic: هذا ما يقوله الرب. ادعوا النساء النائحات ليأتين وأرسلوا إلى الحكيمات ليفتحن أفواههن بالكلام لينشدن عليكم نوحاً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh netenbal maroutaouw e~q~r/i n~hanermwoui~: ouoh ne tenbouhi marousoue~ mwou e~bol: je aucwtem e~p~qrwou n~ounehpi qen Ciwn.
languages.english: Let them take up a lamentation over you. Let your eyes run with tears, and your eyelids flow with water. For a voice of wailing is heard from Zion.
languages.arabic: ولتسكب عيونكم دموعاً. ولتفض أجفانكم ماء. لأنه قد سمع صوت النوح فى صهيون.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: Aiounof e~jen n/e~taujoc n/i: jetenna senan e~p~/i m~P[oic.
languages.english: I was glad when they said to me, "Let us go into the house of the Lord."
languages.arabic: فرحت بالقائلين لى إلى بيت الرب نذهب.

languages.coptic: Nen[alauj auo~hi e~ratou: qen niaul/ou n~te Ieroucal/m: all/louia.
languages.english: Our feet stand in your courts, O Jerusalem. Alleluia.
languages.arabic: وقفت أرجلنا فى ديار أورشليم: الليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Markon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Mark. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار مرقس البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafi~ n~je I/couc e~Ieroucal/m e~qoun e~piervei ouoh e~tafcomc e~p~t/rf: ouoh e~tarouhi h/d/ swpi n~te ]ounou afi~ e~bol e~B/ya~nia~ nem pim/t c~nau:
languages.english: And Jesus went into Jerusalem and into the temple. So when He had looked around at all things, as the hour was already late, He went out to Bethany with the twelve.
languages.arabic: ولما جاء يسوع إلى أورشليم دخل الهيكل ونظر الجمع. وإذ كان المساء فى تلك االساعة خرج إلى بيت عنيا مع الإثنى عشر.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~pefrac] e~taui~ e~bol qen B/ya~nia~ afh~ko:
languages.english: Now the next day, when they had come out from Bethany, He was hungry.
languages.arabic: وفى الغد لما خرجوا من بيت عنيا جاع.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafnau e~oubw n~kente hivouei e~ouon hanjwbi hiwtc afi~ de hara afnajem h~li hiwtc ouoh e~tafi~ e~jwc m~pefjem hli e~b/l e~hanjwbi: ne p~c/ou gar n~kente an pe.
languages.english: And seeing from afar a fig tree having leaves, He went to see if perhaps He would find something on it. When He came to it, He found nothing but leaves, for it was not the season for figs.
languages.arabic: فرأى شجرة تين عن بعد. وكان عليها ورق فجاء إليها لعله يجد فيها شيئاً. فلما جاء إليها لم يجد شيئا الا ورقاً لأنه لم يكن أوان التين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafe~rouw pejaf nac je n~neh~li je ouem outah hiw] sae~neh ouoh naucwtem n~je nefmay/t/c.
languages.english: In response Jesus said to it, “Let no one eat fruit from you ever again.” And His disciples heard it.
languages.arabic: فأجاب وقال لها لا يأكل أحد منك ثمرة إلى الأبد وكان تلاميذه يسمعون.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aui e~Ieroucal/m ouoh e~tafi~e~qoun e~piervei aferh/tc n~hioui~ e~bol n~n/ t/rou et] e~bol nem n/et swp qen piervei
languages.english: So they came to Jerusalem. Then Jesus went into the temple and began to drive out those who bought and sold in the temple,
languages.arabic: وجاءوا إلى أورشليم. ولما دخل إلى الهيكل ابتدأ يخرج جميع الذين كانوا يبيعون ويشترون فى الهيكل.

languages.coptic: Ouoh nit~rapeza n~te nikolibict/c nem nikayedra n~te n/et] [~rompi e~bol afcocou.
languages.english: and overturned the tables of the money changers and the seats of those who sold doves.
languages.arabic: وقلب موائد الصيارفة وكراسى باعة الحمام.

languages.coptic: Ouoh naf,w n~h~li aipe hina n~tefen ouc~keuoc e~bolhiten piervei.
languages.english: And He would not allow anyone to carry wares through the temple.
languages.arabic: ولم يدع أحد ينقل متاعاً إلى الهيكل.

languages.coptic: Ouoh naf]c~bw ouoh nafjw m~moc n~wou je c~c~q/out je pa/i eue~mou] e~roc je ou/i m~p~roceu,/ n~nieynoc t/rou n~ywten de a~retenaif m~b/b n~coni.
languages.english: Then He taught, saying to them, “Is it not written, ‘My house shall be called a house of prayer for all nations’? But you have made it a ‘den of thieves.’”
languages.arabic: وكان يعلم قائلاً لهم أليس مكتوباً أن بيتى بيت صلاة يدعى لجميع الأمم. وأنتم جعلتموه مغارة للصوص.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~taucwtem n~je niar,/e~reuc nem nicaq ouoh naukw] je aunatakof n~as n~r/] nauerho] gar pe qatefhi pim/s gar t/rf nauer sv/ri e~jen tefc~bw:
languages.english: And the scribes and chief priests heard it and sought how they might destroy Him; for they feared Him, because all the people were astonished at His teaching.
languages.arabic: فلما سمع رؤساء الكهنة والكتبة كانوا يطلبون بأى نوع يهلكونه. لأنهم كانوا يخافونه. إذ الجمع كله كان يتعجب من تعليمه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~swp n~te rouhi swpi nafh/l cabol n~]baki.
languages.english: When evening had come, He went out of the city.
languages.arabic: ولما صار المساء خرج إلى خارج المدينة.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: The exposition of the Third Hour of Monday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة الثالثة، من يوم الإثنين من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: On the evening of Hosanna Sunday our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ went with his disciples outside the city. He felt hungry and said I want something to eat.
languages.arabic: فى عشية يوم الشعانين أتى خارج المدينة مع تلاميذه. أعنى المسيح إلهنا يسوع المخلص فجاع و قال أطلب طعاماً.

languages.english: He saw a fig tree afar and went to it seeking some of its fruits. He found it fruitless. He condemned the tree and it dried from its roots.
languages.arabic: فرأى شجرة تين من بعيد فأتى إليها يطلب ثمراً فيها فوجد ورقاً بغير ثمر فلعنها فيبست من أصلها.

languages.english: The disciples were taken by the incident and said to the Lord; the fig tree withered. Keep faith in your hearts and you shall be granted your requests. Do not be surprised that by one single word, the fig tree dried from its roots up.
languages.arabic: فتعجب جميع تلاميذه وقالوا للرب: أن شجرة التين يبست. ضعوا الإيمان فى قلوبكم وكل شئ يسمع منكم ولا تتعجبوا من شجرة التين هذه أنها بكلمة واحدة يبست من أصلها.

languages.english: Because, if you have faith in your hearts you can transfer the mountain from its location. Come all you ignorant and see what happened to the fig tree and present the Lord with good fruits to save yourselves from the evil. Repent all you indolent so that you may receive forgiveness.
languages.arabic: فإذا كان فى قلوبكم إيمان لنقلتم هذا الجبل من مكانه. تعالوا وأنظروا أيها الناس الجهال ما كان من شجرة التين هذه، وأصنعوا ثمره صالحة للرب لكى تخلصوا من الشرير، وأصنعوا توبة أيها الكسالى لكى تنالوا الغفران

languages.english: Clean your faces with many tears because tears wipe out sins. Light your lamps with virtues so that their light may shine upon you in the day of judgment. Share the suffering with your brother and remember how the Lord suffered for our salvation.
languages.arabic: وأغسلوا وجوهكم بدموع غزيره فإن الدموع تمحى الآثام، وأوقدوا مصابيحكم بالفضائل لتضئ عليكم فى الحكم. تألم مع أخيك وأنظر الرب كيف تألم عنا حتى خلصنا.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Pidoxodoc n~te Mwu~c/c pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from the Book of Exodus of Moses the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من سفر الخروج لموسى النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Afcaji de n~je P[oic nem Mwu~c/c efjw m~moc: je mosi n~,wlem e~bol qen paima n~tekse e~pec/t: je afera~nomin n~je peklaoc nai etake~nou e~bol qen p~kahi n~,/mi:
languages.english: But the Lord said to Moses, "Go quickly! Get down from here! For your people whom you brought out of the land of Egypt are transgressing the law.
languages.arabic: وكلم الرب موسى قائلاً: إمضى مسرعاً من هنا وإنزل لأنه قد أثم شعبك الذي أخرجته من أرض مصر.

languages.coptic: Auerparabenin n~,wlem e~bol qen pimwit etakhonhen m~mof etotou: auyamio nwou n~oumaci: auouwst mmof: ouoh auset souswousi n~noumonk n~jij: ouoh pejwou
languages.english: They turned aside quickly from the way I commanded them. They made themselves a calf and are worshiping and sacrificing to it, and are saying,
languages.arabic: وقد زاغوا سريعاً عن الطريق الذي أوصيتهم به. وصنعوا لهم عجلاً (مسبوكاً) وسجدوا له وذبحوا ذبائح لصنعة أيديهم. قائلين

languages.coptic: Je nai ne neknou] p~Icra/l e~tauenk e~h~r/i e~bol qen p~kahi n~,/mi:
languages.english: 'These are your gods, O Israel, that brought you out of the land of Egypt.'
languages.arabic: هذه هى آلهتك يا اسرائيل التى أخرجتك من أرض مصر.

languages.coptic: }nou de ,at n~tajwnt e~rwou qen oum~bon: n~ta fotou e~bol: n~taaik nounis] n~eynoc:
languages.english: Now therefore, let Me be, that I may burn in wrath against them and consume them. Then I will make of you a great nation."
languages.arabic: والآن دعنى أغضب عليهم بحنق وأبيدهم وأجعلك لأمة أعظم.

languages.coptic: Af]ho de n~je Mwu~c/c m~pem~yo m~P[oic Vnou] efjw m~moc: je eybeou P[oic ,~najwnt qen oum~bon ejen peklaoc n/e~take~nou e~bol qen p~kahi n~,/mi n~h~r/i qen teknis] n~jom nem pekswbs et[oci:
languages.english: But Moses prayed before the Lord God and said, "Why, O Lord, does Your wrath burn hot against Your people whom You brought out of the land of Egypt with great power and a mighty hand?
languages.arabic: فتضرع موسى أمام الرب الإله قائلاً: لماذا يارب تغضب غضباً على شعبك الذي أخرجته من أرض مصر بقوتك العظيمة وذراعك الرفيعة.

languages.coptic: M/pote n~cejoc n~je nirem n~,/mi: je etaue~nou e~bol qen oupon/ria~ eqoybou qen nitwou nem e~fotou e~bol hijen pikahi:
languages.english: Why should the Egyptians speak and say, 'He brought them out with evil intent, to kill them in the mountains and to consume them from the face of the earth'?
languages.arabic: لئلا يقول المصريون أنك أخرجتهم بكيد لتقتلهم فى الجبال وتفنيهم عن وجه الأرض.

languages.coptic: <ake~bol P[oic qen pjwnt n~te pekm~bon: ouoh ,w e~bol n~]kakia~ n~te peklaoc:
languages.english: Turn from Your fierce wrath and be merciful to the wickedness of Your people.
languages.arabic: أرجع يارب عن حمو غضبك و أغفر شر شعبك.

languages.coptic: N~tekervmeui~ n~Abraam nem Icaak nem Iakwb: nai etakwrk m~mok nwou m~min m~mok:
languages.english: Remember Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, Your servants, to whom You swore by Yourself and said to them,
languages.arabic: وأذكر إبراهيم وإسحق ويعقوب الذين أقسمت لهم بذاتك وقلت لهم

languages.coptic: Je eie~ eyre petenjroj a~ sai e~masw m~v~r/] n~niciou n~te t~ve qen poua~sai: ouoh paikahi t/rf vai e~takjoc e~t/if m~poujroj: ouoh je cenaerk~ l/ronomin e~jwf sae~neh.
languages.english: 'I will greatly multiply your seed as the stars of heaven for multitude; and all this land I spoke about to give their seed, they shall inherit unto the ages.'''
languages.arabic: إنى أكثر نسلكم جداً كنجوم السماء فى كثرتها. وكل هذه الأرض التى قلت عنها أن تعطيها لنسلهم فيرثوها إلى الأبد.

languages.coptic: P[oic de af,w e~bol n~]kakia~ n~te peflaoc:
languages.english: So the Lord granted mercy for the harm He said He would do to His people.
languages.arabic: فصفح الرب عن شر شعبه.

languages.coptic: Aftacyo n~je Mwu~c/c afi~ e~pec/t e~bolhipitwou: e~re nip~lax c~nou] n~te ]metmeyre ,/ qen nefjij: e~ne han p~laxn~w~nine euc~q/out m~p~ho cnau paica nem fai.
languages.english: Then Moses turned and went down from the mountain with the two tablets of the testimony in his hands. They were stone tablets written on both sides, on one side and on the other.
languages.arabic: ثم رجع موسى ونزل من الجبل ولوحا الشهادة فى يديه وهما لوحان من حجارة مكتوبان من الوجهين من هنا ومن هنا.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen }covia~ n~te Colomon pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from the Wisdom of Solomon the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من حكمة سليمان النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Menre ]meym/i n/et ]hap e~p~kahi: a~ri v~meui~ m~P[oic qen oumeta~gayoc: e~retenkw] n~cwf qen ]metaplouc n~te petenh/t:
languages.english: Love righteousness, you who judge on the earth. Think about the Lord in goodness and seek Him with sincerity of heart;
languages.arabic: أحبوا العدل يا قضاة الأرض. أذكروا الرب بالصلاح. أطلبوه ببساطة قلوبكم

languages.coptic: Je saujimi m~mof n~je n/e~te n~ceerpirazin m~mof an: safouwnh e~bol e~n/e~te n~ceoi n~aynah] e~rof an:
languages.english: because He is found by those who do not tempt Him, and He is manifest to those who do not disbelieve Him.
languages.arabic: فانما يجده الذين لا يجربونه. ويتجلى للذين لا يكفرون به.

languages.coptic: Sare pimeui~ gar ethwou vorjou e~bolha Vnou]: ouoh tefjom eyouwnh e~bol sac cohi n~niath/t:
languages.english: For dishonest reasoning separates people from God, and when His power examines someone, it convicts the undiscerning;
languages.arabic: لأن الفكر الشرير يبعدهم عن الله. وقوته الظاهرة تبكت الجهال.

languages.coptic: Je m~pare ]covia~ se e~qoun e~ou'u,/ echwou: oude m~pac ouwnh qen oucwma n~refernobi:
languages.english: For wisdom will not enter the soul that plots evil, nor will it dwell in a body involved in sin.
languages.arabic: لأن الحكمة لا تدخل فى نفس شريرة. ولا تحل فى جسم خاطئ.

languages.coptic: Pip~neuma gar eyouab n~te ]covia~: safvwt e~bol n~,~rof niben: ouoh safouei e~bol n~nimokmek n~te niath/t: ouoh safcohi m~pi[in~jonc afsani~.
languages.english: For a holy spirit of discipline [wisdom] flees from deceit and sends away undiscerning reasoning; it will put wrongdoing to shame when it comes near.
languages.arabic: لأن روح الحكمة الطاهرة يهرب من كل غش. ويبتعد عن أفكار الجهال ويبكت الظالم إذا أقبل.

languages.coptic: Oumairwmi gar pe pip~neuma n~te ]covia~: ouoh f~erm~aio an m~v/etjeoua~ qen nefc~votou:
languages.english: For wisdom is a spirit that loves mankind, but she will punish a blasphemer because of his words;
languages.arabic: روح الحكمة محب للبشر. فلا يبرئ المجدف بشفتيه.

languages.coptic: Je Vnou] pe pimeyre n~te nef[lwt: ouoh petqotqet m~m/i n~te pefh/t ouoh etcwtem e~peflac:
languages.english: For God is the witness of his thoughts, the true examiner of his heart, and the hearer of his tongue;
languages.arabic: لأن شاهد كليتاه هو الله وهو الفاحص الحقيقى لقلبه والسامع للسانه.

languages.coptic: Je Pip~neuma n~te P[oic afmoh n~]oikoumen/ ouoh v/etcwjp m~pt/rf: f~cwoun m~pouqrwou:
languages.english: Because the Spirit of the Lord fills the world, and He who holds all things together knows what is said.
languages.arabic: لأن روح الرب ملأ المسكونة وبقية الكل وهو يعرف أصواتهم

languages.coptic: Eybe vai m~monhli nas~hwp e~pefcaji qen ou[in~jonc oude n~nefer e~bol e~]k~ricic eyn/ou: eue~jemp~sini gar m~pia~ceb/c qen pefco[ni:
languages.english: Therefore no one who speaks unrighteous things will escape notice. Nor will justice, when it cross-examines, pass him by. For there shall be a close examination into the deliberations of an ungodly man,
languages.arabic: فلذلك لا يستطيع أحد يخفى كلامه بالظلم ولا ينجو من الدينونة الآتية. لأنه يفحص مشورة المنافق.

languages.coptic: Ouoh P[oic nacwtem an e~nefcaji e~v~ouwnh e~bol n~nefa~nomia.
languages.english: And a report of his words will come to the Lord as proof of his lawlessness.
languages.arabic: والرب لا يسمع لكلامه حتى يظهر آثامه.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: Etausenwou gar e~p~swi: e~mau n~je nivul/: nivul/ n~te P[oic eumetmeyre m~p~Icra/l:
languages.english: There the tribes went up, the tribes of the Lord, a testimony to Israel,
languages.arabic: لأنه هناك صعدت القبائل قبائل الرب شهادة لاسرائيل,

languages.coptic: Euouwnh e~bol m~v~ran m~P[oic: all/louia.
languages.english: To give thanks to the name of the Lord. Alleluia.
languages.arabic: يعترفون لأسم الرب. الليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Iwann/n a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint John. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار يوحنا البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~tafi~ n~je i/couc e~h~r/i eIeroucal/m: Ouoh afjimi qen piervei n~niet ]ehe e~bol nem e~cwou nem [rompi nem nifaikerma euhemci.
languages.english: And Jesus went up to Jerusalem. And He found in the temple those who sold oxen and sheep and doves, and the money changers doing business.
languages.arabic: وصعد يسوع إلى أورشليم فوجد فى الهيكل باعة البقر والغنم والحمام والصيارفة جلوساً

languages.coptic: Ouoh afyamio~ n~ouvragellion e~bol qen hannoh afhitou t/rou e~bol qen piervei
languages.english: When He had made a whip of cords, He drove them all out of the temple,
languages.arabic: فصنع مخصرة (سوطاً) من حبال وطرد الجميع من الهيكل

languages.coptic: Nie~cwou nem nie~hwou ouoh nikerma n~te nifaikerma afvonou e~bol ouoh nout~rapeza afvonjou.
languages.english: with the sheep and the oxen, and poured out the changers’ money and overturned the tables.
languages.arabic: الغنم والبقر ونثر دراهم الصيارف وقلب موائدهم.

languages.coptic: Ouoh pejaf n~n/et ][~rompi e~bol je alioui~ n~nai e~bol tai m~perer p/i mpaiwt n~ou/i n~swt:
languages.english: And He said to those who sold doves, “Take these things away! Do not make My Father’s house a house of merchandise!”
languages.arabic: وقال لباعة الحمام ارفعوا هذه من هنا ولا تصيروا بيت أبى بيت تجارة.

languages.coptic: Auerv~meui n~je nefmay/t/c je c~c~q/out je p~,oh m~pek/i petafouomt.
languages.english: Then His disciples remembered that it was written, “Zeal for Your house has eaten Me up.”
languages.arabic: فتذكر تلاميذه أنه مكتوب غيرة بيتك أكلتنى.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: The exposition of the Sixth Hour of Monday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة السادسة، من يوم الإثنين من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: O Israel, God's first children: What impudence did you commit and how insensitive are your priests. The place of forgiveness became a place to sin.
languages.arabic: يا لهذه الجسارة التى صنعتها يا شعب اسرائيل أول ميلاد الله. وهذه البلادة التى من كهنتك إذ موضع الغفران صار موضع الخطية

languages.english: The house of prayer and supplication became a den of thieves and a market place for cattle, sheep, pigeon merchants and for currency exchangers. What profit is that unclean and corrupt, and what injustice have you done.
languages.arabic: وبيت الصلاة وموضع الطلبة صيرتموه مسكناً للصوص ومجمعاً للعجول والخراف ولباعة الحمام والصيارف. ما هو هذا الربح المملوء من كل نجس وهذا الظلم الذي صنعتموه.

languages.english: When the Son of God saw all this done in His Fathers House—for they have turned it in to a den for thievery, bandits, the unjust and a house of trade—he toppled the seats of pigeon merchants and the tables of the currency exchangers and scattered their funds.
languages.arabic: لما نظر إبن الله بيت أبيه وهذه كلها تصنع فيه. فإنهم صيروه مسكناً للصوص الخاطفين والظلمة وبيت التجارة ، فأخرج البقر والغنم معها كراسى باعة الحمام وموائد الصيارف قلبها وبدد دراهمهم.

languages.english: As they watched him do that, His disciples knew that it was thus written about Him; the Zeal for Your house has consumed me. That is why He did that with impunity.
languages.arabic: فلما نظر تلاميذه إلى هذا علموا أنه هكذا هو مكتوب لأجله أن غيرة بيتك أكلتنى فلهذا صنع هكذا بغير خوف.

languages.english: Your reign is forever in heaven and on earth and Your fear, O Lord, has shaken the mountains. But Israel has erred and thus was denied God's help.
languages.arabic: سلطانك دائم فى السماء وعلى الأرض وخوفك يارب زعزع الجبال. أما اسرائيل فقد جهل فلذلك أستترت عنه معونته.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Pjom n~}genecic n~te Mwu~c/c pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from the Book of Genesis of Moses the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من سفر التکوين لموسى النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~P[oic Vnou] [i m~pirwmi etafyamiof: ouoh af,af qen piparadicoc n~te p~ounof eyreferhwb ouoh n~tefa~reh e~rof:
languages.english: Then the Lord God took the man He formed and put him in the garden to tend and keep it.
languages.arabic: وأخذ الرب الإله الإنسان الذي خلقه. ووضعه فى فردوس النعيم ليفلحه ويحفظه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~P[oic Vnou] honhei etotf nAdam efjwm~moc: je e~bolqei s~s/n niben etqen piparadicoc ekeouwm e~bol m~mwou euqre:
languages.english: And the Lord God commanded Adam, saying, "You may eat food from every tree in the garden;
languages.arabic: وأمر الرب الإله آدم قائلاً: من جميع الأشجار التى فى الفردوس تأكل أكلاً.

languages.coptic: Ebol qen pis~s/n n~te p~emi m~p~sib] m~pipeynanef nem pipethwou n~netenouwm e~bol m~mof:
languages.english: But from the tree of the knowledge of good and evil you may not eat;
languages.arabic: وأما شجرة معرفة ابتدال الخير والشر فلا تأكل منها.

languages.coptic: Pie~hoou de e~tetennaouwm e~bol m~mof te tennamou qenoumou.
languages.english: For in whatever day you eat from it, you shall die by death."
languages.arabic: فإنك فى اليوم الذي تأكل منها موتأ تموت.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje P[oic Vnou] je nanec ann~tepirwmi swpi m~mauatf marenyamio naf n~ouboiyoc kata rof:
languages.english: And the Lord God said, "It is not good for man to be alone. I will make him a helper comparable to him."
languages.arabic: وقال الرب الإله لا يحسن أن يكون الإنسان وحده فلنصنع له معيناً مثله.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~P[oic Vnou] [wlp e~bol qen p~kahi n~niy/rion t/rou n~te t~koi nem nihala] t/rou n~te t~ve: ouoh afe~nou ha Adam e~nau je afnamou] e~rwou je nim:
languages.english: Also, God formed out of the ground all the wild animals of the field and all the birds of heaven, and brought them to Adam to see what he would call them.
languages.arabic: وجبل الرب الإله من الأرض جميع وحوش البرية وجميع طيور السماء. وأحضرها إلى آدم ليرى ماذا يسميها

languages.coptic: Ouoh hwb niben e~te Adam mou] e~ou'u,/ ecwnq vai pe pou ran:
languages.english: Thus whatever Adam called each living creature, that was its name.
languages.arabic: فكل ما سماه به آدم من نفس حية فهو أسمها.

languages.coptic: A Adam ]ran e~nitebnwoui~ t/rou nem nihala] t/rou n~te t~ve nem niy/rion t/rou n~te t~koi:
languages.english: So Adam gave names to all the cattle, to all the birds of heaven, and to all the wild animals of the field.
languages.arabic: وسمى آدم جميع البهائم وطيور السماء وجميع وحوش البرية.

languages.coptic: Adam de m~poujem oubo/yoc naf efoni m~mof:
languages.english: But for Adam there was not found a helper comparable to him.
languages.arabic: وأما آدم فلم يجد له معيناً يشبهه

languages.coptic: A Vnou] hioui~ n~ouc~rom n~hunim e~jen Adam: af[i n~oub/t e~bol qen nefc~virwoui~ ouoh afmah pecma n~carx:
languages.english: Thus God brought a trance upon Adam, and he slept; and He took one of his ribs, and filled up the flesh in its place.
languages.arabic: فألقى الله على آدم سباتاً فنام وأخذ ضلعاً من جنبه وملأ موضعها لحماً

languages.coptic: ouoh a~P[oic Vnou] kwt n~]b/t e~taf[itc e~bol qen Adam euc~himi: ouoh afenc ha Adam.
languages.english: Then the Lord God built the rib He took from Adam into a woman, and brought her to him.
languages.arabic: وبنى الرب الإله الضلع التى أخذها من آدم إمرأة وأحضرها إلى آدم.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje Adam: je vai ]nou oukac pe e~bol qen nakac: nem oucarx e~bol qen tacarx: yai eue~mou] e~roc je c~himi: je e~tau[i yai e~bol qen pechai:
languages.english: So Adam said, "This is now bone of my bones, and flesh of my flesh. She shall be called Woman, because she was taken out of Man.
languages.arabic: فقال آدم هذه الآن عظم من عظامى. ولحم من لحمى هذه تدعى امرأة لأنها من امرء أخذت.

languages.coptic: Eybe vai e~re pirwmi ,a pefiwt nem tefmau n~cwf: ouoh efe~tomf e~tefc~himi: ouoh eue~swpi m~p~c~nau eucarx n~ouwt.
languages.english: For this reason a man shall leave his father and mother and be joined to his wife; and the two shall become one flesh."
languages.arabic: لذلك يترك الرجل أباه وأمه ويلتصق بامرأته. ويكونان كلاهما جسداً واحداً.

languages.coptic: Ouoh naub/s m~p~c~nau eucop n~je Adam nem tefc~himi: ouoh nausipi an pe:
languages.english: Now the two were naked, both Adam and his wife, and were not ashamed.
languages.arabic: وكانا كلاهما عريانين معاً. آدم وامرأته. وهما لا يخجلان.

languages.coptic: Pihof de ne oucabe pe e~bol oute niy/riont/rou n/et,/ hijen p~kahi n/e~ta P[oic Vnou] yamiw~ou:
languages.english: Now the serpent was more cunning than all the wild animals the Lord God made on the earth.
languages.arabic: وكانت الحية أحكم جميع الوحوش التى خلقها الرب الإله على الأرض.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje pihof n~]c~himi: je eybe ou a~Vnou] joc je n~netenouwm e~bol qen s~s/n niben etqen piparadicoc:
languages.english: And he said to the woman, "Has God indeed said, 'You shall not eat from every tree of the garden'?"
languages.arabic: فقالت الحية للمرأة. لماذا قال الله لكما أن لا تأكلا من جميع شجر الفردوس.

languages.coptic: Peje ]c~himi m~pihof je e~bol qen p~outah n~te nis~s/n n~te piparadicoc tennaouwm: e~bol de qen p~outah
languages.english: And the woman said to the serpent, "We may eat the fruit from the trees of the garden;
languages.arabic: فقالت المرأة للحية: أن من جميع ثمار شجر الفردوس نأكل

languages.coptic: Mpis~s/n etqen y~m/] m~piparadicoc afjoc n~je Vnou] je n~neten ouwm e~bol m~mof: oude n~neten[oh e~rof hina n~tetens~tem mou.
languages.english: But from the fruit of the tree in the middle of the garden, God said, 'You shall not eat from it, nor shall you touch it. Lest you die.’”
languages.arabic: وأما ثمر الشجرة التى فى وسط الفردوس فقال الله لا تأكلا منه ولا تمساه لئلا تموتا.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje pihof n~]c~himi je nareten na mou gar an qen oumou:
languages.english: Then the serpent said to the woman, "You shall not die by death.
languages.arabic: فقالت الحية للمرأة لن تموتا موتاً.

languages.coptic: Nafe~mi gar n~je Vnou] je pie~hoou e~teten naouwm e~bol m~mof cenaouwn n~je netenbal: tetennaer m~vr/] n~hannou]: e~retencwoun e~oupeynanef nem oupethwou:
languages.english: For God knows in the day you eat from it your eyes will be opened, and you will be like gods, knowing good and evil."
languages.arabic: أنما الله عالم أنكما فى اليوم الذي تأكلان منه تنفتح أعينكما. وتصيران كآلهة عارفي فى الخير والشر.

languages.coptic: Ouoh acnau n~je ]c~himi je nane piss/n e~v~ouomf f~rana n~nibal e~v~nau e~rof: ouoh e~necwf e~p~]nittc naf: ac[i e~bol qen pefoutah acouwm:
languages.english: So when the woman saw the tree was good for food, was pleasant to the eyes, and a tree beautiful to contemplate, she took its fruit and ate.
languages.arabic: فرأت المرأة أن الشجرة جيدة للأكل ومبهجة للنظر حسنة التأمل إليها فأخذت من ثمرها و أكلت.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ac] ~mpeckehai nemac ouoh afouwm: ouoh auouwn n~je noubal m~p~c~nau: ouoh au e~mi je ceb/s:
languages.english: She also gave it to her husband with her, and he ate. Then the eyes of the two were opened, and they knew they were naked.
languages.arabic: و أعطت بعلها أيضاً معها فأكل. فأنفتحت أعينهما كلاهما وعلما أنهما عريانان

languages.coptic: Ouoh auywrp n~hanjwbi n~te oubw n~kente: auyamio~ nwou nhan a~k/c:
languages.english: So they sewed fig leaves together and made themselves coverings.
languages.arabic: فخاطا من ورق التين وصنعا لهما مآزر.

languages.coptic: Ouoh aucwtem e~ t~c~m/ m~P[oic Vnou] efmosi qen piparadicoc m~v~nau n~rouhi: ouoh au,wp n~je Adam nem ]efc~himi e~bolha p~ho m~P[oic Vnou]: qen y~m/] n~niss/n n~te piparadicoc.
languages.english: Then they heard the voice of the Lord God walking in the garden that afternoon, and Adam and his wife hid themselves within the tree in the middle of the garden from the presence of the Lord God.
languages.arabic: فسمعا صوت الرب الإله ماشياً فى الفردوس وقت المساء. فأختفيا آدم وزوجته من وجه الرب الإله فى وسط شجر الفردوس.

languages.coptic: Ouoh afmou] n~je P[oic Vnou] e~Adam pejaf je akvwn Adam:
languages.english: So the Lord God called Adam and said to him, “Adam, where are you?”
languages.arabic: فنادى الرب الإله آدم وقال له. أين أنت يا آدم.

languages.coptic: Pejaf naf: je tekc~m/ e~taicoymec ek mosi qen piparadicoc aierho]: je ]b/s ouoh ai,wp:
languages.english: He replied, “I heard Your voice as You were walking in the garden, and I was afraid because I was naked; so I hid myself.”
languages.arabic: فقال له سمعت صوتك ماشياً فى الفردوس فخفت. لأنى عريان فإختبأت.

languages.coptic: Pejaf naf je nim pe taftamok je k~b/s e~b/l je akouwm e~bol qen pis~s/n e~taihenhonk e~rof je vai m~mauatf m~per ouwm e~bol m~mof: ouoh akouwm e~bol m~mof:
languages.english: Thus He said, “Who said you were naked? Have you eaten from the one tree from which I commanded you not to eat?”
languages.arabic: فقال له ومن أعلمك أنك عريان إلا أنك أكلت من الشجرة التى أوصيتك أن لا تأكل منها وحدها. فأكلت منها.

languages.coptic: Peje Adam je ]c~himi e~takt/ic n/i n~yoc ac]n/i e~bol qen pis~s/n aiouwm. Ouoh peje P[oic Vnou] n~]c~himi je eybeou a~re ervai: peje ]c~himi: je pihof pee~taferhal m~moi aiouwm:
languages.english: Then Adam said, “The woman You gave me, gave me of the tree, and I ate.” So the Lord God said to the woman, “What is this you have done?” The woman said, “The serpent deceived me, and I ate.”
languages.arabic: فقال آدم أن المرأة التى أعطيتها لى هى التى أعطتنى من الشجرة فأكلت. فقال الرب الإله للمرأة. لما فعلت هذا. فقالت المرأة الحية أغوتنى فأكلت.

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje P[oic Vnou] m~pihof: je akervai k~c~houort n~yok e~bol oute nitebnwoui~ t/rou: nem e~bol oute niy/rion t/rou n/et hijen pikahi: eke~mosi e~jen tekmectenh/t nem tekneji: ouoh eke~ouem kahi n~nie~hoou t/rou n~te pewnq:
languages.english: Thus the Lord God said to the serpent, “Because you have done this, you are cursed more than all cattle, and more than all the wild animals on the earth. On your breast and belly you shall go, and you shall eat dust all the days of your life.
languages.arabic: فقال الرب الإله للحية. لأنك فعلت هذا. فملعونة أنت من بين جميع البهائم. وجميع الوحوش التى على وجه الأرض و تدبين على صدرك وبطنك. وتأكلين تراباً طول أيام حياتك.

languages.coptic: Ouoh eie~,w n~ou metjaji outwk nem oute ]c~himi: nem oute pekj~roj: nem oute pecjroj: ouoh n~yof efe~a~reh e~teka~ve: ouoh n~yok eke~a~reh e~pefyibc:
languages.english: I will put enmity between you and the woman, and between your seed and her seed. He shall bruise your head, and you shall be on guard for His heel."
languages.arabic: وأضع عداوة بينك وبين المرأة. وبين نسلك ونسلها. فهو يسحق رأسك وأنت ترصدين عقبه.

languages.coptic: Ouoh pejaf n~]c~himi: je qen oua~sai eie~y~re nem~kah n~h/t a~sai nem pefia~ hom: qen han m~kah n~h/t e~remec hans/ri:
languages.english: To the woman He said, "I will greatly multiply your pain and your groaning, and in pain you shall bring forth children.
languages.arabic: وقال للمرأة. تكثيراً أكثر أحزان قلبك وتنهدك. وبالأحزان (بالأوجاع) تلدين البنين.

languages.coptic: Ouoh efe~swpi ha pehai n~je pejintacyo: ouoh n~yof efe~er[oic e~ro.
languages.english: Your recourse will be to your husband, and he shall rule over you.”
languages.arabic: وإلى بعلك يكون رجوعك وهو يسود عليك.

languages.coptic: Pejaf de n~Adam: je e~pid/ akcwtem n~ca tekc~himi: ouoh akouwm e~bol qen pis~s/n: v/e~tai henhonk e~rof je vai m~mauatf n~nekouwm e~bol m~mof akouwm: pikahi c~houort e~bol qen nekh~b/oui~:
languages.english: Then to Adam He said, “Because you heeded the voice of your wife, and ate from the one tree of which I commanded you, saying, ‘You shall not eat from it,’ cursed is the ground in your labors.
languages.arabic: وقال لآدم. بما أنك أطعت إمرأتك. وأكلت من الشجرة التى أوصيتك قائلاً: هذه وحدها لا تأكل منها. فملعونة الأرض بسبب أعمالك.

languages.coptic: Eke~ouomf qen han e~m~kah n~h/t n~nie~hoou t/rou n~te pekwnq: hanson] nem hancouri efe~rwt m~mwou nak: ouoh eke~ouwm nak m~picim n~te p~kahi: eke~ouwm m~pekwikqen p~fw] m~pekho:
languages.english: In toil you shall eat from it all the days of your life. Both thorns and thistles it shall bring forth for you, and you shall eat the herb of the field.
languages.arabic: بالمشقة تأكل منها كل أيام حياتك. وشوكاً وحسكاً تنبت لك. وتأكل عشب الأرض وبعرق وجهك تأكل خبزك.

languages.coptic: Satektacyok e~pikahi e~tau[itk e~bol n~q/tf je n~yok oukahi,~natacyok e~p~kahi.
languages.english: In the sweat of your face you shall eat bread till you return.”
languages.arabic: حتى تعود إلى الأرض التى أخذت منها. لأنك تراب وإلى التراب تعود.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~Adam mou] e~v~ran n~tefc~himi je Zw/~: je yai te y~mau n~n/etwnq t/rou.
languages.english: So Adam called his wife’s name Life, because she was the mother of all living.
languages.arabic: ودعا آدم أسم امرأته حواء. لأنها أم كل حي.

languages.coptic: Ouoh a~P[oic Vnou] yamio nAdam nem tef c~himi n~han s~y/n n~sar: aft/itou hiw~tou.
languages.english: Also for Adam and his wife the Lord God made garments of skin, and clothed them.
languages.arabic: وصنع الرب الإله لآدم وأمرأته أقمصة من جلد وكساهما

languages.coptic: Ouoh peje P[oic Vnou] je h/ppe ic Adam afer m~v~r/] n~ouai e~bol m~mon: e~pjincouen ou peynanef nem oupethwou.
languages.english: Then the Lord God said, “Behold, the man has become like one of Us, to know good and evil.
languages.arabic: وقال الرب الإله. هوذا آدم قد صار كواحد منا يعرف الخير والشر.

languages.coptic: Ouoh ]nou m/pwc n~tefcoutwn tefjij ouoh e~bol qen pis~s/n n~te p~wnq n~tefouwm ouoh n~tefwnq sae~neh.
