languages.english: But they made light of it and went their ways, one to his own farm, another to his business. And the rest seized his servants, treated them spitefully, and killed them.
languages.arabic: أما هم فتهاونوا. فذهب واحد إلى حقله وآخر إلي تجارته والباقون أمسكوا عبيده وأهانوهم وقتلوهم,

languages.coptic: Piouro de e~tafcwtem afjwnt ouoh aftaouc n~nefctrateuma ouoh aftako n~nirefqwteb e~tem~mau: ouoh toubaki afrokhc qen pi,rwm:
languages.english: But when the king heard about it, he was furious. And he sent out his armies, destroyed those murderers, and burned up their city.
languages.arabic: فلما سمع الملك غضب وأرسل جنوده وأهلك أولئك القتلة، وأحرق مدينتهم بالنار.

languages.coptic: Tote pejaf n~nefe~biaik: je pihop men efcebtwt: n/etyahem de nau m~p~sa an pe:
languages.english: Then he said to his servants, ‘The wedding is ready, but those who were invited were not worthy.
languages.arabic: حينئذ قال لعبيده: أما العرس فمعد وأما المدعوون فلم يكونوا مستحقين،

languages.coptic: Masenwten de e~bol e~nimanmosi n~te nimwit: ouoh n/e~tetennajemou yahmou e~qoun e~pihop.
languages.english: Therefore go into the highways, and as many as you find, invite to the wedding.’
languages.arabic: فإذهبوا إلي مفارق الطرق، وكل من وجدتموه فإدعوه إلي العرس.

languages.coptic: Ouoh e~taui e~bol n~je nie~biaik e~tem~mau hinimwit: auywou] n~ouon niben e~taujemou n/ethwou nem n/eynaneu: ouoh afmoh n~je pihop e~bolqen n/eyrwteb:
languages.english: So those servants went out into the highways and gathered together all whom they found, both bad and good. And the wedding hall was filled with guests.
languages.arabic: فخرج أولئك العبيد إلى الطرق وجمعوا كل من وجدوا أشرار وصالحين فإمتلأ العرس من المتكئين،

languages.coptic: Etafi~ de e~qoun n~je piouro e~nau e~n/eyrwteb: afnau e~ourwmi m~mau n~t~hebcw m~pihop toi hiwtf an:
languages.english: “But when the king came in to see the guests, he saw a man there who did not have on a wedding garment.
languages.arabic: فلما دخل الملك لينظر إلي المتكئين، ورأى هناك رجلاً ليس عليه ثياب العرس، فقال له: يا صاحب،

languages.coptic: Ouoh pejaf naf je pas~v/r: pwc aki e~qoun e~m~nai n~t~hebcw m~pihop toihiwtk an: n~yof de a~rwf ywm.
languages.english: So he said to him, ‘Friend, how did you come in here without a wedding garment?’ And he was speechless.
languages.arabic: كيف دخلت إلي هنا وليس عليك ثياب العرس؟ أما هو فسكت،

languages.coptic: Tote peje p~ouro n~nidia~kwn: je conh m~vai n~totf nem ratf: hitf e~pi,aki etcb~ol: pima e~te v~rimi naswpi mmof nem pic~yerter n~te ninajhi:
languages.english: Then the king said to the servants, ‘Bind him hand and foot, take him away, and cast him into outer darkness; there will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.’
languages.arabic: حينئذ قال الملك للخدام: أوثقوا يديه ورجليه وأطرحوه في الظلمة الخارجية، هناك يكون البكاء وصرير الأسنان،

languages.coptic: Ouon hanm/s gar euyahem hankouji de ne nicwtp.
languages.english: “For many are called, but few are chosen.”
languages.arabic: لأن المدعوين كثيرون والمختارين قليلون.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: The exposition of the First Hour of the eve of Wednesday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة الاولى، من ليلة الأربعاء من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: That king who held the marriage feast and invited the guests to the banquet, is God the Father. His Son is Jesus Christ our Savior.
languages.arabic: الإنسان الملك الذي صنع العرس, ودعا المدعوين هو الله الآب, وإبنه هو المسيح يسوع مخلصنا

languages.english: The feast is the world to which he came, and was incarnated from the Theotokos and was among the people as one of them.
languages.arabic: والعرس هو العالم الذي ظهر فيه، الذي ولدته بالجسد والدة الإله وصار مع الناس كواحد منهم،

languages.english: The servants He commissioned to carry His message are the prophets who preceded Him preaching to the nations His imminent coming.
languages.arabic: والعبيد الذين أرسلهم هم الآنبياء الذين سبقوه، ودعوا الأمم قبل مجيئه قائلين إن الآتي سوف يآتٍي ولا يبطئ،

languages.english: The (nations), however, neglected the matter and rejected their message; they went each one his own way: the one to his field, the other to his business, and the rest killed the messengers.
languages.arabic: فتكاسلوا ولم يقبلوا أقوالهم، ثم مضوا متهاونين واحداً إلي حقله وآخر إلي تجارته والباقون أمسكوا عبيده وقتلوهم

languages.english: The king became enraged, sent his troops, disciplined these killers, and burned their town. Who were those who were invited to the real banquet which was for God the Word, but the contrary antipathetic Jews whose names were eliminated from the Book of Life.
languages.arabic: فغضب الملك وأرسل عسكره وضرب أولئك القتلة وأحرق مدينتهم. من هم الناس الذين دعوا إلي الوليمة الحقيقية التي لله الكلمة إلا اليهود المخالفون الذين محيت أسماؤهم من سفر الحياة؟

languages.english: He (God the Father) sent others and commanded them. to go in the streets and to invite everyone they find. When they did that, they invited many who were good and many who were bad, until they had a full house.
languages.arabic: فعاد أيضاً وأرسل آخرين وأوصاهم هكذا قائلاً أخرجوا إلي مسالك الطرق وأدعوا كل الذين تجدونهم. فلما خرجوا دعوا كثيرين صالحين وطالحين فامتلأ العرس من المتكئين،

languages.english: The king came in to inspect the guests; he saw someone who was not appropriately attired for the occasion. He asked him: Friend how did you get in without the proper dress. The man was speechless and embarrassed and the servants shoved him out to the outer darkness..
languages.arabic: فلما دخل الملك لينظر المتكئين رأى هناك إنساناً لم يكن لابساً لباس العرس فقال له: يا صاحب كيف دخلت هنا وليس عليك ثياب العرس؟ فللوقت صمت وصار في فضيحة، ثم ألقاه الخدام إلي الظلمة الخارجية.

languages.english: Who is this individual, but Judas, who lost his heavenly garment and became dressed with damnation which seeped as water into his stomach because he denied His master's blessings and dared to betray Him. He was thus estranged from his (master's) glory and his priesthood was given to another.
languages.arabic: من هو هذا الإنسان إلا يهوذا الذي تعري من الحلة السمائية ولبس اللعنة مثل الثوب، ودخلت إلي أمعاءه مثل الماء؟ لأنه أنكر نعمة سيده وتجرأ أن يسلم معلمه، فذلك صار غريباً من مجده ورئاسة كهنوته.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Amwc pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Amos the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من عاموس النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Ouoi n~n/eter e~piyumin e~pie~hoou n~te P[oic: e~rou nwten e~pai. ehoou n~te P[oic: ouoh pai e~hoou n~ou,aki ouoh n~ouwini an pe: Mv~r/] afsanvwt n~je ourwmi e~bolha p~ho n~oumoui~ ouoh n~tec i~ ejwf n~je ou laboi:
languages.english: Woe to you who desire the day of the Lord! What is the day of the Lord to you? It will be darkness and not light. It will be as though a man fled from a lion, and a bear met him!
languages.arabic: ويل للذين يشتهون يوم الرب ما بغيتكم في يوم الرب، فإن هذا اليوم ظلام لا نور, كما إذا هرب إنسان من وجه أسد فصادفته لبوة

languages.coptic: Ouoh n~tefvwt e~qoun e~pef/i ouoh n~teffai n~tef jij e~h~r/i e~jen ]joi n~tef jokhf n~je ouhof: M/ ou,aki an pe p~e~hoou m~P[oic: ouoh ououwinia~n pe oug~novoc pe m~mon tefmoue~.
languages.english: As though he rushed into the house, leaned his hand on the wall, and a serpent bit him! Is not the day of the Lord darkness and not light? Is it not a deep darkness, with no brightness in it?
languages.arabic: فيجيئ إلي بيته ويرفع يده علي الحائط فتلدغه حية، أليس يوم ظلام لا نور وضباب لا ضياء له؟

languages.coptic: Aimecte netensai ouoh ai,au n~cwi: oun~naswlem qen netennis] n~e~hoou n~sai:
languages.english: "I hate, I reject your feast days. I do not savor your sacred assemblies.
languages.arabic: لقد أبغضت أعيادكم ورذلتها ولست أشم رائحة في أيام أعيادكم العظيمة،

languages.coptic: Je ou/i e~swp a~retensan i~ni n/i n~han[~lil nem hansouswousi n~]nasopou an: ouoh ]najoust an e~jen na pioujai n~te petenouwnh e~bol:
languages.english: Though you offer Me burnt offerings and meat offerings, I will not accept them. I will not regard your fattened peace offerings.
languages.arabic: إني إذا قربتم لي محرقاتكم وتقدماتكم لا أقبلها، ولست أنظر إلي فدية شكركم.

languages.coptic: Matacyo e~bolharoi mp~q~rwou n~nekhwd/: ouoh pi'almoc n~te nekorganon n~]nacwtem e~rof an:
languages.english: Take away from Me the sound of your songs, for I will not hear your musical instruments.
languages.arabic: أبعد عني صوت تسابيحك، ومزمار أرغنك لست أسمعه،

languages.coptic: Ouoh efe~c~kerker n~je ouhap m~v~r/] n~oumwou: ouoh ]meym/i m~v~r/] n~oumouncwrem n~aterjinior m~mof. M/ hanqolqel nem han souswousi a~retene~nou e~h~r/i n/i hip~safe nh~me n~rompi p~/i m~p~Icra/l peje P[oic:
languages.english: Instead, let justice run swiftly as does water from the mountain, and let righteousness be as a stream that cannot be crossed. "O house of Israel, did you offer Me sacrifices and offerings those forty years in the wilderness? [Says the Lord.]
languages.arabic: وليجر القضاء كالماء والعدل كالوادي الذي لايعبر. هل قربتم لي ذبائح وتقدمات في البرية أربعين سنة يا بيت اسرائيل يقول الرب؟

languages.coptic: A~reten[i n~]c~kun/ n~te Molo, nempiciou n~te petennou] Revan: nitupoc e~taretenyamiw~ou nwten.
languages.english: You have carried the tabernacle of Moloch and the star of your god Raiphan, which you made for yourselves.
languages.arabic: بل حملتم خيمة ملوخ وكوكب إلهكم رافان التماثيل التي صنعتموها لكم,

languages.coptic: Ouoh eie~oue~teb y/nou e~bol cam~n/ n~Damackoc: peje P[oic Vnou] pipantokratwr pe pefran.
languages.english: Therefore I shall exile you beyond Damascus," says the Lord—the Almighty God is His name.
languages.arabic: فأسبيكم إلي ماوراء دمشق. قال الرب الإله الضابط الكل هو إسمه.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: Wouniatf m~v/e~takcotpf ouoh aksopf e~rok: efe~swpi qen nekaul/ou sae~neh.
languages.english: Blessed is he whom You choose and help; he shall dwell in Your courts.
languages.arabic: طوبى لمن أخترته وقبلته ليسكن في ديارك إلي الأبد,

languages.coptic: F~ouab n~je pekervei: ouoh foi n~s~v/ri qen oumeym/i: all/louia.
languages.english: Holy is Your temple, wondrous in righteousness. Alleluia.
languages.arabic: قدوس هو هيكلك وعجيب بالبر. الليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Matyeon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Matthew. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار متى البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Eybe pie~hoou de etem~mau nem ]ounou: m~mon hli e~mi e~rwou: oude niaggeloc n~te niv/oui: e~b/l e~Viwt m~mauatf:
languages.english: “But of that day and hour no one knows, not even the angels of heaven, but My Father only.
languages.arabic: وأما ذلك اليوم وتلك الساعة، فلا يعلمهما أحد ولا ملائكة السموات، إلا الآب وحده.

languages.coptic: Mv~r/] gar n~nie~hoou n~te Nwe~: pair/] eynaswpi qen t~paroucia~ m~p~s/ri m~v~rwmi.
languages.english: But as the days of Noah were, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be.
languages.arabic: وكما كان في أيام نوح، كذلك يكون في ظهور إبن الإنسان،

languages.coptic: Mv~r/] gar e~nausop qen nie~hoou etqajwf m~pikatak~lucmoc euouwm ouoh eucw: eu[ic~himi ouoh eu[ihai: sa pie~hoou e~ta Nwe~ senaf e~qoun e~]kubwtoc m~mof. Ouoh m~poue~mi satefi~ n~je pikatak~lucmoc ouoh n~tefel ouon niben: pair/] eynaswpi qen p~jini~ m~p~s/ri m~v~rwmi:
languages.english: For as in the days before the flood, they were eating and drinking, marrying and giving in marriage, until the day that Noah entered the ark, and did not know until the flood came and took them all away, so also will the coming of the Son of Man be.
languages.arabic: لأنه كما كانوا قبل أيام الطوفان يأكلون ويشربون ويتزوجون ويزوجون إلي اليوم الذي دخل فيه نوح إلي الفلك، ولم يعلموا حتي جاء الطوفان وغرق الجميع، كذلك يكون أيضاً مجيء إبن الإنسان.

languages.coptic: Tote c~nau eu,/ qen t~koi: ouai eue~olf ouoh ouai eue~,af:
languages.english: Then two men will be in the field: one will be taken and the other left.
languages.arabic: حينئذٍ يكون إثنان في الحقل ويؤخذ الواحد ويترك الآخر

languages.coptic: Ouoh c~nou] eunout qen oueuni: oui~ eue~olc ouoh oui~ eue~,ac.
languages.english: Two women will be grinding at the mill: one will be taken and the other left.
languages.arabic: وإثنتان تطحنان علي رحي، فتؤخذ الواحدة وتترك الأخرى.

languages.coptic: Rwic oun je tetene~mi an je a~re peten[oic n/ou qen as n~ounou:
languages.english: Watch therefore, for you do not know what hour your Lord is coming.
languages.arabic: إسهروا إذا لأنكم لا تعلمون في أية ساعة يأتي ربكم.

languages.coptic: Vai de a~rie~mi e~rof: je e~nare pineb/i e~mi je a~re piconi n/ou naf qen as n~ounou: nafnarwic: hina n~tefstem,au e~[i m~pef/i:
languages.english: But know this, that if the master of the house had known what hour the thief would come, he would have watched and not allowed his house to be broken into.
languages.arabic: وإعلموا هذا، أنه لو علم رب البيت في أية ساعة يأتى السارق لسهر ولم يدع بيته ينقب،

languages.coptic: Eybe vai hwten swpi e~retencebtwt: je qen ]ounou e~tetencwoun m~moc an e~re p~s/ri m~v~rwmi n/ou n~q/tc.
languages.english: Therefore you also be ready, for the Son of Man is coming at an hour you do not expect.
languages.arabic: فلذلك كونوا انتم أيضاً مستعدين لأن إبن الإنسان يأتي في ساعة لا تعرفونها.

languages.coptic: Nim hara pe pipictoc m~bwk ouoh n~cabe: v/e~te pef[oic na,af e~h~r/i e~jen nefe~biaik e~] nwou n~touq~re qen p~c/ou n~t/ic:
languages.english: “Who then is a faithful and wise servant, whom his master made ruler over his household, to give them food in due season?
languages.arabic: فمن تري هو العبد الأمين الحكيم الذي يقيمه سيده علي عبيده ليعطيهم طعامهم في حينه؟

languages.coptic: Wouniatf m~pibwk e~tem~mau: eswp afsani~ n~je pef[oic n~tefjemf efi~ri m~pair/]: a~m/n ]jw m~moc nwten: je efe,af hijen peten~taf t/rf.
languages.english: Blessed is that servant whom his master, when he comes, will find so doing. Assuredly, I say to you that he will make him ruler over all his goods.
languages.arabic: طوبى لذلك العبد الذي إذا جاء سيده فيجده يفعل هكذا. الحق أقول لكم, إنه يقيمه علي جميع ماله.

languages.coptic: E~swp de afsanjoc n~je pibwk ethwou e~te m~mau qen pefh/t: je pa[oic nawck:
languages.english: But if that evil servant says in his heart, ‘My master is delaying his coming,’
languages.arabic: ولكن إن قال ذلك العبد الشرير في قلبه,

languages.coptic: Ouoh n~teferh/tc n~hioui~ n~nefs~v/r n~e~biaik: n~tef ouwm de ouoh n~tefcw nem n/etyaqi:
languages.english: And begins to beat his fellow servants, and to eat and drink with the drunkards,
languages.arabic: إن سيدي يبطئ قدومه فيبتدئ يضرب العبيد رفقاءه ويأكل ويشرب مع السكيرين،

languages.coptic: Efe~i~ de n~je P[oic m~pibwk e~tem~mau qen pie~hoou e~tefnajoust qajwf an: nem qen ]ounou e~ten~f~cwoun m~moc an:
languages.english: The master of that servant will come on a day when he is not looking for him and at an hour that he is not aware of,
languages.arabic: فيأتي سيد ذلك العبد في يوم لا ينتظره، وساعة لا يعرفها

languages.coptic: Ouoh efevorjf tefm/] ouoh efe~,w n~teftoi nem nisobi: pima e~te v~rimi naswpi m~mof nem pic~yerter n~te ninajhi.
languages.english: And will cut him in two and appoint him his portion with the hypocrites. There shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.
languages.arabic: فيشقه من وسطه ويجعل نصيبه مع المرائين هناك يكون البكاء وصرير الاسنان.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: The exposition of the Third Hour of the eve of Wednesday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة الثالثة، من ليلة الأربعاء من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: Contemplate you who exalt the Lord, the kindness of Christ our God: observe how He calls His chosen who fulfill His will, prudent and honest servants.
languages.arabic: تأملوا يا عابدي الإله إلي تحنن المسيح إلهنا، كيف يدعو أصفياءه الصانعين إرادته عبيداً حكماء وأمناء,

languages.english: I mean those who preserve his commandments, and look forward to their awards; those who remain awake and alert, that they may receive the promised rewards.
languages.arabic: أعني الذين يحفظون وصاياه المتوقعين أجراً صالحاً، الساهرين المتيقظين لكي ينالوا المواعيد

languages.english: As the Bible asserts that those servants—whose master makes a surprise visit and finds them awake—are blessed. Assuredly I say: He will make His servant ruler over His property.
languages.arabic: كما قال في الإنجيل إن ذلك العبد مغبوطاً، أعني الذي يأتي سيده بغتة فيجده يفعل هكذا أقول لكم إنه يقيمه وكيلاً على جميع ماله

languages.english: So for He whom He finds derelict in his duties and neglecting them often, one day, his Master will make a surprise visit, repudiate him, and make his lot with the hypocrites, in the place of darkness and suffering.
languages.arabic: فأما ذلك الذي يجده متغافلاً فيطرد يوماً بيوم، فيجئ سيده في ساعة لا يعرفها فيشقه من وسطه ويجعل نصيبه مع المرائين في الظلمة وموضع العذاب،

languages.english: Let us awake and be alert in anticipation of the day of the Lord so that we may rejoice with Him in His dwelling-place and receive His clemencies and mercies.
languages.arabic: فلنتيقظ من غفلتنا وننتظر يوم الرب لنفرح معه في دياره ونفوز بمراحمه ورافاته.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Iermiac pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Jeremiah the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من أرميا النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Nai ne n/e~tefjw m~mwou n~je P[oic n~te nijom Vnou] n~te p~Icra/l:
languages.english: "Thus says the Lord: 'In this manner I will destroy the insolence of Judah and that of Jerusalem.
languages.arabic: هذا ما يقوله رب القوات إله اسرائيل: هأنذا أفسد كبرياء يهوذا وكبرياء أورشليم

languages.coptic: Je ich/ppe a~nok ]natako e~bolqen paima mpem~yo n~netenbal e~bol: ouoh n~h~r/iqen netene~hoou n~ne ouc~m/ n~ounof nem ouc~m/ n~rasi swpi: t~c~m/ n~oupatsel/t nem oucm/ n~selet:
languages.english: Behold, before your eyes and in your days, I will bring to an end from this place the voice of joy and the voice of gladness, the voice of the bridegroom and the voice of the bride.
languages.arabic: العظيمة في هذا المكان أمام عيونكم وفي أيامكم لا يكون صوت سرور ولا صوت فرح ولا صوت عريس ولا صوت عروس،

languages.coptic: Ouoh ece~swpi aksantame pai laoc e~nai caji t/rou ouoh eue~joc nak je eybeou a~P[oic caji e~h~r/i e~jwn n~nai pethwou t/rou.
languages.english: "It shall come to pass that when you shall announce all these words to this people and they say to you, 'Why has the Lord pronounced all these evils against us?
languages.arabic: ويكون إذا عرفت الشعب بجميع هذه الأقوال وقالوا: لماذا تكلم الرب علينا بكل هذه الشرور،

languages.coptic: Ie as pe pi[in~jonc: ie v~nobi e~tanaif m~pem~yo e~bol m~P[oic Pennou]:
languages.english: What is our wrongdoing, and what sin did we commit before the Lord our God?'
languages.arabic: وما الظلم أو الخطية التي صنعناها أمام الرب إلهنا؟

languages.coptic: Eke~joc nwou: je eybe je a~netenio] ,at n~cwou peje P[oic:
languages.english: Then you shall say to them, 'Because your fathers forsook Me,' says the Lord,
languages.arabic: فتقول لهم، لأن أباءكم تركوني عنهم قال الرب،

languages.coptic: Ouoh aumosi cavahou n~ninou] n~semmwou auerbwk nwou
languages.english: 'For they went after foreign gods; and they served and worshiped them.
languages.arabic: وأتبعوا آلهة غريبة وعبدوها وسجدوا لها،

languages.coptic: Ouoh auouwst m~mwou a~nok de au,at n~cwou: ouoh panomoc m~poua~reh e~rof:
languages.english: They forsook Me, and did not keep My law.
languages.arabic: وأما أنا فتركوني عنهم ولم يحفظوا ناموسي

languages.coptic: Nywten hwten atetenerpethwou ehote netenio]. Ic h/ppe n~ywten hwten e~retenmosi v~ouai v~ouai cavahou n~niouws n~te netenh/t ethwou e~s~tem y~reten cwtem n~cwi:
languages.english: But you acted in ways more evil than your fathers; for behold, you, each one of you, walk after the evil pleasures of your heart and do not obey Me.
languages.arabic: وقد عملتم أنتم أيضاً الشر أكثر من أبائكم، فهوذا أنتم أيضاً تسلكون كل واحد وراء شهوات قلوبكم الشريرة غير مطيعين لي,

languages.coptic: Ouoh ]nahiy/nou e~bolqen pai kahi eoukahi e~retencwoun m~mof an n~ywten nem netenio] ouoh qen pima e~te m~mau: eretene~erbwk e~hankenou]: nai e~te n~cenanai nwten an.
languages.english: Therefore I will cast you out of this land and into a land neither you nor your fathers know; and there you shall serve other gods, who will show you no mercy.
languages.arabic: فساطرد كم من هذه الأرض، إلي أرض لا تعرفونها أنتم ولا أبائكم, وفي ذلك الموضع تتعبدون لألهة أخري، هؤلاء الذين لا يرحمونكم.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: P[oic cwtem e~ta p~roceu,/: mare paq~rwou i~ e~p~swi m~pekm~yo.
languages.english: Hear my prayer, O Lord, and let my cry come to You.
languages.arabic: يارب استمع صلاتي وليصعد أمامك صراخي

languages.coptic: Qen pie~hoou e~]naws e~p~swi oub/k n~q/tf: ,wlem cwtem e~roi: all/louia.
languages.english: In the day when I call upon You, hear me quickly. Alleluia.
languages.arabic: في اليوم الذي أدعوك فيه أستجب لي سريعاً. الليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Matyeon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Matthew. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار متى البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Tote c~o~ni n~je ]metouro n~te niv/oui m~m/] m~paryenoc nai e~tau[i n~noulampac aui~ e~bol e~h~ren pipatselet:
languages.english: “Then the kingdom of heaven shall be likened to ten virgins who took their lamps and went out to meet the bridegroom.
languages.arabic: حينئذ يشبه ملكوت السموات عشر عذارى اخذن مصابيحهن وخرجن للقاء العريس

languages.coptic: Ne ouon t~iou de n~coj n~q/tou nem tiou n~cab/: Nicoj gar e~tau[i n~noulampac. mpouel neh nemwou. Nicabeu de auel neh n~q~r/i qen noumoki nem noulampac:
languages.english: Now five of them were wise, and five were foolish. Those who were foolish took their lamps and took no oil with them, but the wise took oil in their vessels with their lamps.
languages.arabic: وكان خمس منهن جاهلات وخمس حكيمات اما الجاهلات فأخذن مصابيحهن ولم يأخذن معهن زيتا واما الحكيمات فأخذن زيتا فى انيتهن مع مصابيحهن

languages.coptic: Etafwck de n~je pipatselet auhi nim t/rou ouoh auenkot: E~ta t~vasi de mpie~jwrh swpi: a~ouq~rwou swpi je ic pipatselet afi~ teny/nou a~mwini e~bol e~h~raf.
languages.english: But while the bridegroom was delayed, they all slumbered and slept. “And at midnight a cry was heard: ‘Behold, the bridegroom is coming; go out to meet him!’
languages.arabic: فلما ابطا العريس نعسن كلهن ونمن ففى نصف الليل صار صوت: هوذا العريس قد اقبل فقمن اخرجن للقائه

languages.coptic: Tote autwounou n~je niparyenoc t/rou e~tem~mau ouoh aucolcel n~noulampac:
languages.english: Then all those virgins arose and trimmed their lamps.
languages.arabic: حينئذ قامت اولئك العذارى وزين مصابيحهن،

languages.coptic: Peje nicoj de n~nicabeu je moi nan ebolqen petenneh je nenlampac na[e~no:
languages.english: And the foolish said to the wise, ‘Give us some of your oil, for our lamps are going out.’
languages.arabic: فقالت الجاهلاتٍ للحكيمات: اعطينا من زيتكن فان مصابيحنا تنطفى

languages.coptic: Aue~rouw~ de n~je nicabeu eujwm~moc: je m/pote n~tefs~tem rasten nemwten: masenwten de mallon ha n/et] e~bol ouoh swp nwten.
languages.english: But the wise answered, saying, ‘No, lest there should not be enough for us and you; but go rather to those who sell, and buy for yourselves.’
languages.arabic: فاجابت الحكيمات وقلن: لعله لا يكفينا واياكن فاذهبن بالاحرى الى الباعة وابتعن لكن

languages.coptic: Etausenwou de je n~touswp: afi n~je pipatselet ouoh n/etcebtwt ausenwou nemaf e~qoun e~ pihop ouoh aumasyam m~piro:
languages.english: And while they went to buy, the bridegroom came, and those who were ready went in with him to the wedding; and the door was shut.
languages.arabic: فلما ذهبن ليبتعن جاء العريس ودخلت معه المستعدات الى العرس واغلق الباب

languages.coptic: Ep~qae~ de aui n~je p~cwjp n~niparyenoc eujwm~moc: je Pen[oic Pen[oic aouwn nan:
languages.english: “Afterward the other virgins came also, saying, ‘Lord, Lord, open to us!’
languages.arabic: وأخيراً جاءت بقية العذارى قائلات: ربنا ربنا افتح لنا

languages.coptic: N~yof de aferouw~ pejaf je a~m/n ]jwm~mmoc nwten je ]cwoun m~mwten an: Rwic oun je tetencwoun an m~piehoou oude ]ounou.
languages.english: But he answered and said, ‘Assuredly, I say to you, I do not know you.’ “Watch therefore, for you know neither the day nor the hour.
languages.arabic: اما هو فاجاب وقال: الحق اقول لكن اننى لا اعرفكن فاسهروا اذا لانكم لا تعرفون اليوم ولا الساعة.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: The exposition of the Sixth Hour of the eve of Wednesday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة السادسة، من ليلة الأربعاء من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: Behold these virtues and parables that our Lord of Glory Jesus the source of every grace and good—has told us; He who granted mankind to partake in His kingdom.
languages.arabic: يا لهذه الفضائل وهذه الامثال التى قالها ملك المجد الذي هو يسوع ملك النعمة والخيرات المكمل السلام الذي انعم لجنس البشر بشركة ملكوته.

languages.english: Listen, think, and understand and know His blessed parables, for the sake of the prudent virgins who were mentioned in the Bible and were likened to his kingdom; full of joy and exultation.
languages.arabic: اسمعوا وتأملوا وافهموا واعلموا أمثاله الطوباوية من اجل العذارى الحكيمات اللواتى نطق من أجلهن في الإنجيل وشبههن بملكوته المملوء فرحا وسروراً.

languages.english: Ten virgins: five foolish and five wise, said the Lord. As He tells us, there was no distinction between them as virgins; but they differed in their deeds. Thus, He praised and blessed the wise five (virgins) because they were earnest and prudent.
languages.arabic: عشر عذارى قال الرب خمس جاهلات وخمس حكيمات قال هؤلاء العشرة كن عذراى ولكنهن افترقن لاجل أعمالهن فطوب الحكيمات الفهيمات لانهن صنعن الحكمة بايجاد

languages.english: They filled their lanterns with oil and kept the surplus in their containers. As for the foolish virgins, they were lackadaisical and did not prepare their lanterns.
languages.arabic: وملأن مصابيحهن من الزيت واوعيتهن مما فضل عنهن فاما العذارى الجاهلات فتكاسلن ولم يفهمن قيمة مصابيحهن

languages.english: When they all woke up at the given time to proceed with their procession ahead of the bridegroom, their lanterns became useless at the wedding time and were unable to accompany the bridegroom.
languages.arabic: فلما قمن جميعهن فى ساعة واحدة ليمشين قدام العريس تعطلت مصابيحهن وقت الفرح ولم يحضرن مع العريس

languages.english: Those who were prepared, accompanied him to the wedding and the somnolent ones stayed outside.
languages.arabic: والمستعدات دخلن معه الى العرس والمتكاسلات وقفن خارجا.

languages.coptic: Ebolqen Wcie~ pip~rov/t/c: e~repefc~mou eyouab swpi neman a~m/n efjw m~moc:
languages.english: A reading from Hosea the prophet. May his holy blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: من هوشع النبي بركته المقدسة تکون معنا. آمين.

languages.coptic: Moinwou P[oic oupe teknat/if nwou: moinwou n~hanio] nats/ri: nem hanm~no] eusouw~ou.
languages.english: Give to them, O Lord! What will You give to them? A childless womb and dry breasts.
languages.arabic: أعطهم يارب. وماذا تعطيهم؟ أعطيهم آباء بلا بنين وأثداء يابسة،

languages.coptic: Noukakia~ t/rou qen Galgala: je aimectwou qen pima etem~mau: eybe nikakia~ n~te nouh~b/oui~: ]nahitou e~bolqen pa/i: n~naouahtot e~menritou nouar,wn t/rou han atcwtem ne:
languages.english: "All their evils are in Gilgal, for there I hated them. Because of their evil practices, I will cast them out of My house. I will not love them anymore. All their leaders are disobedient.
languages.arabic: لأن جميع شرورهم في الجلجال، لأن في ذلك المكان أبغضتهم من أجل سوء أعمالهم، سأطردهم من بيتي ولا أعود أحبهم، لأن جميع رؤسائهم متمردون.

languages.coptic: Aferm~kah n~je Evrem: epefnouni afswoui~: n~nefouahtotf je e~en ou tah e~bol: je ou/i ausan erpkemici ]naqwteb n~niepiyumia~ n~te touneji:
languages.english: Ephraim underwent trouble; he is dried up at his roots; he shall bear fruit no more. Wherefore, even if they should beget children, I would kill the desired fruit of their womb."
languages.arabic: حزن أفرايم الذي أصله قد جف ولا يعود أن يأتٍي بثمرة. وإن ولدوا فإني اقتل شهوات بطونهم.

languages.coptic: F~na,au n~cwf n~je Vnou] je m~poucwtem n~cwf: ouoh eueswpi eucwrem qen nieynoc. Oubw n~a~loli e~nece neck~ladoc pe p~Icra/l: f~euyenin n~je pecoutah:
languages.english: God shall reject them because they have not hearkened to Him. So they shall be wanderers among the nations. Israel is a luxuriant vine; her fruit is abundant.
languages.arabic: يرفضهم الله لأنهم لم يسمعوا له، فيكونون تائهين في الأمم. إن اسرائيل كرمة حسنة الأغصان وثمرتها شهية

languages.coptic: Kata p~a~sai n~te pefoutah afy~re hanmanerswousi a~sai: ouoh kata nia~gayon n~te pefkahi afkwt n~hanc~tul/: Auvwq n~nouh/t: ]nou cenatako:
languages.english: According to the multitude of her fruits she has multiplied her altars; according to the wealth of his land he has set up pillars. They have divided their hearts; now shall they be utterly destroyed.
languages.arabic: وعلي حسب كثرة ثمره كثر المذابح وعلي حسب خصب أرضه بني الآنصاب، قد قسوا قلوبهم والآن سيهلكون,

languages.coptic: Nyof f~nasorser n~nou manerswousi cenaertalepwrin n~je nouc~tul/.
languages.english: He shall raze their altars to the ground; their pillars shall experience distress.
languages.arabic: هو يحطم مذابحهم ويخرب انصابهم.

languages.coptic: Ouw~ou n~]t~riac eyouab Pennou] sa e~neh nem sa e~neh n~te nie~neh t/rou: a~m/n.
languages.english: Glory be to the Holy Trinity, our God, forever and unto the ages of all ages. Amen.
languages.arabic: مجداً للثالوث القدوس إلهنا الى أبد الآبدين كلها آمين.

languages.coptic: "almoc tw Dauid.
languages.english: A Psalm of David.
languages.arabic: من مزامير داود النبي.

languages.coptic: Nohem n~ta'u,/ e~bol n~totc n~t~c/fi: nem tamets/ri m~mauatc e~bol n~totf n~ououhor.
languages.english: Deliver my soul from the sword and my only-begotten from the hand of the dog;
languages.arabic: نج من السيف نفسي ومن الكلب بنوتى الوحيدة

languages.coptic: Nahmet e~bolqen rwf n~oumoui~: ouoh payebio~ e~bolha pitap n~te napitap n~ouwt: all/louia.
languages.english: Save me from the lion's mouth, my humiliation from the horns of the unicorns. Alleluia.
languages.arabic: خلصني من فم الأسد وتواضعي من قرن ذى القرن الواحد. الليلويا.

languages.coptic: Ke u~pertou kata xiwy/ne /~mac: t/c akro a~cewc tou a~giou euaggeliou: kurion ke ton Yeon /~mwn: i~keteucwmen covia~ oryi a~koucwmen tou a~giou euaggeliou.
languages.english: We beseech our Lord and God, that we may be worthy to hear the Holy Gospel. In wisdom and uprightness let us attend to the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: من أجل أن نكون مستحقين لسماع الإنجيل المقدس. إلي ربنا وإلهنا نتوسل بحكمة مستقيمين انصتوا للانجيل المقدس.

languages.english: O God have mercy and compassion upon us, and make us worthy to hear the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: اللهم تراءف علينا وارحمنا و اجعلنا مستحقين لسماع انجيلك المقدس.

languages.coptic: Oua~nagnwcic e~bolqen pieuaggelion eyouab kata Matyeon a~giou.
languages.english: A reading according to Saint Matthew. May his blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: فصل من بشارة الإنجيل لمعلمنا مار متى البشير بركاته علينا آمين.

languages.coptic: Doxa ci Kurie.
languages.english: Glory be to God forever.
languages.arabic: والمجْدُ للهِ دَائماً.

languages.english: Note that the correct translation is "Glory to You O Lord"

languages.coptic: Ouoi nwten nicaq nem nivariceoc nisobi: je tetenkwt n~nim~hau n~te nip~rov/t/c ouoh n~tetencolcel n~nib/b n~te niy~m/i:
languages.english: “Woe to you, scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites! Because you build the tombs of the prophets and adorn the monuments of the righteous,
languages.arabic: الويل لكم ايها الكتبة والفريسيون المراؤون لانكم تبنون قبور الأنبياء وتزينون مدافن الصديقين

languages.coptic: Ouoh tetenjwm~moc je e~nan,/ pe qen nie~hoou n~te nenio] nan naswpi enoi n~s~v/r e~rwou an pe qen p~c~nof n~te nip~rov/t/c:
languages.english: And say, ‘If we had lived in the days of our fathers, we would not have been partakers with them in the blood of the prophets.’
languages.arabic: وتقولون: لو كنا فى ايام ابائنا لما كنا شاركناهم فى دم الآنبياء

languages.coptic: Hwcte tetenermeyre qarwten: je n~ywten nens/ri n~te n/e~tau qwteb n~nip~rov/t/c.
languages.english: “Therefore you are witnesses against yourselves that you are sons of those who murdered the prophets.
languages.arabic: فانتم تشهدون إذاً على انفسكم انكم بنو قتلة الأنبياء

languages.coptic: Ouoh n~ywten hwten jek p~si n~netenio] e~bol: Nihof m~mici e~bolqen nia~jw: pwc tetennas~vwt ebolqen ]k~r/cic n~te ]geenna.
languages.english: Fill up, then, the measure of your fathers’ guilt. Serpents, brood of vipers! How can you escape the condemnation of hell?
languages.arabic: فكملوا انتم مكاييل ابائكم ايها الحيات اولاد الافاعى. كيف تهربون من دينونة جهنم،

languages.coptic: Eybe vai h/ppe a~nok ]naouwrp harwten n~han p~rov/t/c nem hancabeu n~caq ouoh e~reten e~qwteb e~bol n~q/tou ouoh e~retene~i~si ouoh eretene~ermactiggoin m~mwou qen netencunagwg/ ouoh e~retene~[ojincwou icjen baki e~baki.
languages.english: Therefore, indeed, I send you prophets, wise men, and scribes: some of them you will kill and crucify, and some of them you will scourge in your synagogues and persecute from city to city,
languages.arabic: من اجل ذلك ها انا ارسل اليكم انبياء وحكماء وكتبة فمنهم من تقتلون وتصلبون ومنهم من تجلدون فى مجامعكم وتطرودنهم من مدينة الى مدينة

languages.coptic: Hopwc n~tefi~ e~jen y/nou n~je cnof niben n~y~m/i e~tavonf e~bol hijen pikahi: icjen p~c~nof nAbel piy~m/i sa p~c~nof n~Za,ariac p~s/ri m~Bara,iac: v/e~taretenqoybef oute piervei nem pimanerswousi:
languages.english: That on you may come all the righteous blood shed on the earth, from the blood of righteous Abel to the blood of Zechariah, son of Berechiah, whom you murdered between the temple and the altar.
languages.arabic: لكى يأتى عليكم كل دم بار سفك على الارض من دم هابيل الصديق الى دم زكريا بن برشيا الذي قتلتموه بين الهيكل المذبح.

languages.coptic: Am/n ]jwm~moc nwten je e~re nai t/rou n/ou e~jen tai genea.
languages.english: Assuredly, I say to you, all these things will come upon this generation.
languages.arabic: الحق اقول لكم ان هذه كلها ستأتى علي هذا الجيل.

languages.coptic: Ouwst m~pieuaggelion eyouab.
languages.english: Bow down before the Holy Gospel.
languages.arabic: إسجدوا للإنجيل المقدس.

languages.english: The exposition of the Ninth Hour of the eve of Wednesday of Holy Pascha. May its blessings be with us. Amen.
languages.arabic: طرح الساعة التاسعة، من ليلة الأربعاء من البصخة المقدسة بركته علينا. آمين.

languages.english: Christ paints a sad picture of the conduct and the compartment of the Pharisees.
languages.arabic: يا لهذه الأوصاف المحزنة التي قالها المسيح علي الفريسيين،

languages.english: He admonishes them in an unmistakable way for carrying on the same questionable traditions of their predecessors and even surpassed them.
languages.arabic: إذ يعطيهم الويل بغير محاباة، لأنهم تشبهوا بأبائهم وكملوا مكاييلهم.

languages.english: Their predecessors slew the prophets and saints. Their successors built the tombs of these prophets. They were the snakes born of serpents.
languages.arabic: أولئك الذين قتلوا الأنبياء القديسين، وهؤلاء الآخرون بنوا مقابرهم, هؤلاء هم الحيات المولودون من الأفاعي

languages.english: They will not escape their deeds. They will be charged with the blood of these saints; from the blood of Abel to the blood of Zechariah whom they slew between the sanctuary and the altar.
languages.arabic: الذين لم يهربوا من جهنم، فسينتقم منهم عن دم جميع الأبرار الذي سفك علي وجه الأرض، من دم هابيل إلي دم زكريا الذي قتلوه بين الهيكل والمذبح. كل هذه الضوائق

languages.english: All these preceding tribulation will fall on this generation because they all had conspired to kill the Son of God.
languages.arabic: وهذه الشدائد تسبق وتأتي علي هذا الجيل لأنهم جميعهم تشاوروا مشورة واحدة علي إبن الله ليقتلوه،

languages.english: That is why God scattered them all over the earth, their enemies dominated them their heritage was given to others and their dwellings became desolate.
languages.arabic: لذلك فرقهم الله في الأرض وأعداؤهم تسلطوا عليهم وميراثهم صار لقوم آخرين وصارت منازلهم خراباً.
